Teaching and Exams

The University provides technologies that support classroom learning and individual study, including [Sensus Access](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/services/sensus-access "Sensus Access") (for converting documents into accessible alternative formats) and the Blackboard Ally plug-in, in addition to textbooks in digital format.

Regarding exams, compensatory and dispensatory measures are assigned according to medical documentation, which may include extra time or the use of calculators or a computer.

For more information, see the _Compensatory/Dispensatory Measures During Exams: Initial Request, Types and Deadlines_ paragraph of the [Guides to the University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/guide-university "Guide all'Università") _._

Library Services

The Bocconi Library is an institution authorized by the Italian Ministry of Culture to [produce accessible copies](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/about-us/accessibility-and-inclusion) of works protected by copyright for people with SLDs.

Learning Needs Help Desk

The [Desk](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/learning-needs "Learning Needs") was established to provide every student with an SLD the chance to receive individual counseling on the best study strategies to tackle the university experience.

Peer to Peer Tutoring Project

This [service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/peer-peer-tutoring-project "Peer to Peer Tutoring Project") aims to improve and optimize study abilities through peer support, provided by student volunteers who have received specific training.

You may contact the Academic Specific Needs office via B in Touch > Exams > Disability and Specific Needs.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/inclusion-disabilities-and-learning-disorders/services-students-disabilities-slds-adhd-and-other-specific-needs#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Services for Students with Disabilities, SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs - Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Students with SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs

text:
About Us

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us)

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion)


Every day Bocconi endeavors to build a university that is truly reflective of the society in which we want to live. It continues to be an increasingly diverse and inclusive institution — a physical and virtual space that is both safe and welcoming for all.

Ensuring that members of its community — regardless of gender, gender identity, religion, nationality, ethnicity, socioeconomic background, sexual orientation, disability or mental health needs — feel both seen and heard remains a crucial objective of the University.

metadata.title: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University

text:
### Gender Diversity

The University is committed to fostering a welcoming and inclusive environment for everyone, regardless of gender, gender identity and sexual orientation.

Additionally, Bocconi recognizes the importance of gender equality as a core value for an equitable and respectful academic community by tracking gender representation within the University and taking actions to raise awareness and achieve parity.

The Inclusive Gender Equality Plan (IGEP) 2025–2027 is a three-year strategy that reflects on the present while outlining future initiatives to promote equal opportunities at the University and in academia.

[Find Out More](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/governance-people-and-policies/inclusive-gender-equality-plan-and-gender-report)

The Gender Report analyzes gender equality trends over time using various indicators, while also outlining activities aimed at promoting gender equality, equal opportunities, and the wellbeing of all university members.

[Find Out More](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/governance-people-and-policies/inclusive-gender-equality-plan-and-gender-report)

This document outlines Bocconi University’s commitment to gender equality, defining principles and objectives for an inclusive, fair and respectful environment, in line with the Strategic Plan 2026–2030.

[Read More](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/852%20Gender%20Equality%20Policy%20eng.pdf?VersionId=sh9qfSVuWoiL5Re71Y8evc0BuZ_pD7H4)

The Alias identity is an alternative and temporary bureaucratic profile that replaces the registry name of a person going through a gender reassignment process with a chosen name.

[Find Out More](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/organization/statute-regulations-and-rules/rules/regulations-activation-and-management-alias-identities)

To ensure comfort for non-binary, transgender and all individuals, Bocconi has established [all gender restrooms](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/buildings-and-classrooms) in every building alongside restrooms already available at the University.

metadata.title: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University > Gender Diversity

text:
### Gender Equality Certification

Bocconi has obtained the Certification for Gender Equality according to UNI/PdR 125:2022. The certification is based on the principles of transparency, measurability and responsibility. It has been implemented through a Management System that includes objectives, indicators and periodic monitoring, inspired and supported by the principles of the Gender Equality Policy.

This milestone reflects adopted policies and practices, and confirms our University’s role as a center for the production of knowledge on gender equality issues, with the aim of actively contributing to change in society and the economic system.

[Download the Certification](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/UNI_PdR%20125_2022%20-%20Certificate%20IPDR-0326-13.pdf?VersionId=sd9I_YA7WWGT0Czqo2vhaUVvo6ASCgkD)

metadata.title: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University > Gender Equality Certification

text:
### Multiculturalism

The university campus is located in Milan, an international and innovative metropolis — a place where languages, experiences, ideas and interests are exchanged on a daily basis, fostering both professional and personal growth.

Bocconi embraces cultural diversity by fostering an inclusive environment where individuals from different backgrounds feel valued through ongoing dialogue that acknowledges and responds to their unique experiences and needs.

**Multicultural Classes:** Multicultural classes are a key resource for students since they encourage the exchange of experiences and ideas, and present an important source of inspiration for faculty. However, they are also incredibly complex to navigate. This is why “Diversity and Inclusion in Teaching” was created — a training course created for faculty that, among other topics, addresses the management of multicultural classes, providing practical solutions for situations that might occur in the classroom.

[**Bocconi Alumni Community**](https://www.bocconialumni.it/): The Bocconi Alumni Community allows its over 146,000 members (graduates of Bachelor, Master, Specialized Master and PhD programs, as well as SDA program graduates, who live and work in various countries) — located in numerous chapters around the world — to keep in touch and stay engaged with Bocconi. The Community organizes events and themed seminars, and also offers Career Advice services.

**Multifaith Space:** A multifaith space is located on campus, open to the entire student body, faculty and staff. It was designed to permit anyone to practice their faith, pray or simply devote a moment to reflection and silence.

**Ethical-Religious Meals:** The offering of halal, kosher and vegan meals in dining areas underscores Bocconi's commitment to recognizing and supporting the dietary needs of the entire community — which may be related to religious, ethical and/or cultural beliefs.

[**Student Associations**](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/attivita-studentesche/elenco-delle-associazioni "Elenco delle associazioni") **:** As of today, there are more than 100 student associations on campus, including ones representing various international communities. The associations offer an opportunity to engage with people from different backgrounds who share common interests, thus becoming a point of reference and mutual support.

metadata.title: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University > Multiculturalism

text:
### Disabilities and Specific Learning Disorders (SLDs)

The University provides support and access for people with disabilities and/or specific learning disorders in its **community**, making efforts to guarantee equal opportunities through accessible infrastructure, assistive technologies, and other specialized services.

Students can receive support during their **university career**, including: personalized measures for the admission test, lectures and exams; accessible accommodation; and digitized materials. Additionally, they can receive personalized assistance regarding exchange programs and career guidance.

The objective is to encourage a participatory experience, fostering the full realization of each individual’s potential.

Dedicated services are available for both [prospective students](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/services-prospective-students-disabilities-learning-disorders-and-other-specific-0 "Services for Prospective Students with Disabilities, Learning Disorders and Other Specific Needs") and [current students](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/inclusion-disabilities-and-learning-disorders/services-students-disabilities-slds-adhd-and-other-specific-needs "Services for Students with Disabilities, SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs").

metadata.title: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University > Disabilities and Specific Learning Disorders (SLDs)

text:
### Accessibility

#### **Physical Accessibility and Dedicated Services**

Accessibility of campus spaces is guaranteed for all students, faculty, staff and visitors. The University has installed elevator platforms, wheelchair ramps, and automated doors, and has also constructed accessible classrooms with built-in or additional desks.

For people with blindness or low vision, the following services can be found on campus: radio beacons (signal transmitters placed outside buildings and along connecting paths); Braille maps of the campus; and Braille plaques outside classrooms, offices and bathrooms. Other services available to all members of the community include:

- [access for service animals](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/campus-access-service-animals "On-Campus Access of Service Animals")
- [home-university transportation](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/home-university-transfer-people-motor-disabilities "Home-University Transfer for People with Motor Disabilities")
- a [mobility companion service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/mobility-companion-service-people-disabilities "Mobility Companion Service for People with Disabilities") between buildings
- public and private parking spaces for people with disabilities

#### Digital Accessibility

Bocconi promotes a just, inclusive and accessible digital environment for its entire community by voluntarily adhering to current digital accessibility standards, including Law 4/2004 and AgID guidelines for accessibility of IT tools. The University works hard to continuously modernize and improve its digital content, as demonstrated by the [accessibility statement](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/accessibility-statement "Accessibility Statement"). In order to stay informed of new technologies and standards in digital accessibility, Bocconi has partnered with a digital accessibility expert.

metadata.title: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Equal Opportunities and Belonging | Bocconi University > Accessibility

text:
### Health and Wellbeing

The physical and mental wellbeing of members of the Bocconi community (students, faculty and staff) is a priority for the University. The University promotes a healthy lifestyle, offering specific resources dedicated to balancing both physical and mental health through one's professional and academic path.

Available initiatives include:

- [wellbeing activities](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/wellbeing "Wellbeing")
- [counseling service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/counseling "Counseling")
- [on-campus safety and security](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/campus-safety-and-security "On-Campus Safety and Security")
- counseling helpdesk for core faculty and staff (ISSIM)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging#addtoany "Show all")

text:
About Us

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us)

Quality Assurance

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/quality-assurance)


Quality Assurance


Beyond ensuring the quality of teaching and research, Bocconi University also prioritizes offering efficient services that meet students' needs, supporting and facilitating campus life and the learning process.

With a commitment to continuous improvement and listening to user feedback, the University will be conducting an annual online survey. This survey is addressed to all students enrolled in Bachelor/Master of Science programs and PhD programs, focusing on internal services.

metadata.title: Services and Infrastructures Survey | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Services and Infrastructures Survey | Bocconi University

text:
## Use of Survey Results

The insights gathered from these surveys are meticulously examined by the heads of assessed services. They identify both strengths and areas of improvement in the resources and performance of each organizational unit and define interventions aimed at overcoming recognized challenges and inefficiencies to enhance the level of service offered to students.

One year later, customer satisfaction surveys are then also used to assess the extent to which the improvement initiatives implemented (or in progress) have been perceived and appreciated by the student population.

Also, the University regularly conducts an online survey given to Staff and Faculty. Its purpose is to gather their opinions on a range of services offered by the University. The collected feedback is then used to identify valuable insights for organizational improvements.

The data collected will be a useful source of information for organizational improvement. In fact, after the results have been published, Direction/Service are asked to indicate improvement actions through an online process.

Annually, heads of services are asked to provide an update on the actions implemented. This process allows them to assess the progress made and to ensure that improvement initiatives are effectively implemented.

- [Services evaluation: Students and PhD Students - latest edition](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/Report%20servizi%202024_1.pdf)


Quality Assurance

Europ Assistance Group offers discounts from 10% on the following Travel Study&Stage, Travel Nonstop Vacation, Outdoor Noproblem and Ski Noproblem policies:
- ViaggiStudy&Stage: The travel policy for a study trip or internship abroad. 24/7 health care anywhere in the world, medical expense reimbursement (including for Covid-19 or terrorist acts) up to 500k€, liability up to 250k, interpreter available in case of hospitalization abroad. May also cover any study course not enjoyed in case of hospitalization/medical return.
- Travel Nostop Vacanza: Travel insurance offering 24/7 health care and medical expense reimbursement (including for Covid-19 or terrorist acts) in Italy, Europe or the World. Traveling in Europe you also have the option to protect your vehicle (car, RV, motorcycle or bike) and have coverage in case of forced extension of stay for Covid-19.
- Outdoor Noproblem: Policy for those who do outdoor activities. In case of injury, assistance and medical expense reimbursement, plus coverage for rescue and search costs, third-party liability.
- Ski Noproblem: Policy designed for Ski/Snowboard/Skating enthusiasts, weekly or annual. Provides liability coverage, medical expense coverage in case of injury while skiing/snowboarding/skating, reimbursement of ski passes-ski lessons-rental of equipment following injury.

text:
IMA Italia Assistance

Get a **30% discount on Imaway travel policies**, a brand of IMA Italia Assistance, the Italian subsidiary of France's leading assistance group Inter Mutuelles Assistance. While you're traveling, you can count on our assistance thanks to a health network of more than 12,700 affiliated medical centers and our 55,000 professionals worldwide.

**Imaway Student Travel**\- Imaway Student Travel Insurance is designed for young people up to age 45 who are traveling abroad for up to 365 days. It is the ideal policy for those who want to insure health care and payment of medical expenses during their study stays.

**Imaway Single Trip**\- insures your travel for up to 60 days. Choose travel insurance that offers you 24-hour health care, some of the highest maximums, direct payment of medical expenses, baggage protection and trip cancellation.

**Imaway Multivacation** \- Imaway Multivacation insures all the trips you take in a year, with a limit of 45 days per single trip. It is the ideal policy if you want to save money without giving up health care and payment of medical expenses during your travels.

The 30% discount will be applied by accessing [www.imaway.it](http://www.imaway.it/) via the following [link](https://www.imaway.it/?promo=AMICI).

Fineco

È attiva una convenzione tra l’Università Bocconi e Fineco, che consente di accedere a condizioni agevolate per l’apertura di un conto Fineco.

In allegato è disponibile un leaflet informativo che riassume le principali caratteristiche dell’offerta e le modalità di adesione.

All’interno del documento è presente un QR code che consente di consultare ulteriori dettagli direttamente online.

È inoltre indicato il Fineco Center di riferimento, da contattare in caso di apertura del conto con l’assistenza di un consulente (necessaria solo per i clienti non residenti in Italia).

Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Organized by **Teatro Franco Parenti** in collaboration with **Associazione Pier Lombardo**.

_**Why “Break a Leg”?**_

In the British and American theatre tradition, actors are wished good luck before going on stage with the phrase “Break a leg!”. It may sound ominous — no one actually wants to break a leg while performing Shakespeare — but in theatre, it’s considered a lucky charm. Saying “good luck,” ironically, is thought to bring misfortune.

Theatre thrives on paradoxes, reversals, and sudden shifts in perspective. In this spirit, Break a Leg! is more than a superstition: it represents energy, wit, creativity, courage, and just a touch of madness — qualities essential to every true act of creation.

_**Workshop Overview**_

This English-language theatre workshop uses the tools and techniques of performance to develop expressive, communicative, and relational skills. Participants will strengthen their soft skills through play, creativity, and teamwork.

At the end of the program, students will collaborate on a final showcase performance, presented during the Campus Life open day, applying the theatre techniques they’ve learned. The course covers every phase of acting: group foundation, physical and vocal training, improvisation, text creation, rehearsal, and live performance.

_**When and where**_

- 11-18-25 February
- 4-11-25 March
- 15-22-29 April
- 6-8 May  (General rehearsals)
- 11 May: Closing night

The course will be held in the **Campus Life space, via Bocconi 12 at 6.30pm — 8.30pm.**

The activity is open also to Faculty and Staff Members, but priority will be given to students.

**Course registration fee: 50 €**

**Click on "Access as BOCCONI USER" > Campus Life > and select the product**

[REGISTER HERE](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en/customer/account/login/referer)

FIND OUT MORE ABOUT THE INSTRUCTORS

### Tobia Rossi e Claudia Grassi

**Tobia Rossi (1986)**– Playwright, screenwriter, and theatre educator, with works staged at leading Italian and international theatres. Winner of the Mario Fratti Award (NYC, 2018), his plays have been produced in Milan, London, and New York. Author of 'Cosa siamo nel buio' (Mondadori, 2024).

**Claudia Grassi (1996)**– Actress graduated from the Luca Ronconi School of Theatre at the Piccolo Teatro di Milano. Her experience in theatrical productions ranges from acting to assistant directing, collaborating with major Italian theatres and directors.

**Workshop Coordinator:** Susanna Brusa (Teatro Franco Parenti)

**BookCrossing** is a way to exchange books, share, connect with others, reduce waste and pollution, and encourage reading and opportunities for conviviality!

In collaboration with the student association **Bocconi d’Inchiostro**, **Egea** and the **Bocconi Library**, the **Campus Life office** is promoting a **BookCrossing area** to encourage the sharing of knowledge and cultural exchange, while at the same time promoting the reuse of paper resources, in line with the University’s sustainability plan.

The BookCrossing area is located on the **mezzanine floor of the Via Bocconi 12 building** and was  **inaugurated** **on 2** **6 September 2023.**

The initiative is **completely free and open to all members of the Bocconi community**: anyone can take one or more books and donate others.

All donated books will have a stamp indicating that they are not abandoned books but books 'on the move'.

You too can take care of this reality to ensure that books continue to circulate and thus keep the bookcrossing community alive.

In addition, throughout the year Bocconi d’Inchiostro organizes meetings and events in this area: [follow them to find out more!](https://www.instagram.com/bocconidinchiostro/)

And for all BookCrossing users there is a free Campus Life bookmark available in the area!

For additional information:

[campuslife@unibocconi.it](mailto:campuslife@unibocconi.it)

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Choral singing, understood as a musical instrument but also as a means of growth cohesion, is now widespread in most universities around the world.

The repertoire proposed draws on musical traditions from various eras and cultures, from African to Anglo-Saxon, from the Renaissance to the contemporary, from sacred to profane. It starts from monophonic pieces with the aim of reaching polyphonic singing.

Both choral singing accompanied by piano and a cappella pieces are planned.

The project also wants to allow participants to achieve a series of personal goals, such as: maturation of intonation and vocal emission, enhancement and involvement of the body in learning, also through gestures and signs, increasing musical critical ability, developing gradual control and mastery of one's own voice, sharing group activity.

Students with previous choral experience and singers with no experience are welcome to participate. Being able to read music is certainly an excellent requirement, but it is not absolutely necessary as the teaching methodology is imitative with the support of audio that can be created to memorize the parts better.

The lessons include an important warm-up (breathing, body activation), an ear training part, a moment dedicated to vocalizations and intonation, and then the study and revision of the repertoire. During the first meetings, the vocal registers of the individual singers will also be established to create the sections (Soprani, Alti, Tenori, Bassi). The last meetings will instead be dedicated to the concertation of the studied pieces.

At the end of this journey there will be the possibility to participate in a final recital.

Enrolled students will be provided with musical materials, including sheet music and other materials preparatory to choral activity.

#### **When and where**

- 12-19-26 February
- 5-12-26 March
- 2-16-23-30 April
- 7 - 8 May (General rehearsals)

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)

GuiDance

**"GuiDance"** is a dance course open to the whole Bocconi community: students, faculty, and staff. **Inclusiveness** is the key word for us. That's why GuiDance is proud to be a **judgement-free zone** where students can **discover the incredible art of Dance, on their own terms and time.**

This course could be defined as a dance experience with a ballet flair. The course is open to all people with little to almost no dance training.

Marco Pelle, international choreographer, and resident choreographer of the New York Theatre Ballet, with Gaia Maria Lorusso assistant choreographer, will take the students through a journey of fun learning and discovery.

The ballet-style warm up will be followed by combinations and choreographic phrases inspired by classical repertoire. Whereas ballet has been always confined to the theaters’ rehearsal studios, Gaia and Marco designed exercises, routines, and choreography that give everybody the chance to not only approach the beautiful art of ballet without feeling inadequate or left out but to also build strength, stamina and a new understanding of their own body and sense of rythm.

metadata.title: Dance Masterclass | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Dance Masterclass | Bocconi University

text:
#### **Calendar**

- 9 – 16 – 23 February
- 2 – 9 – 16 - 23 – 30 March
- 13 – 20 – 27 April
- 5 - 8 May (General rehearsals)
- 11 May: Closing night

Classes will be held from 6:15 to 8:15 pm in the Campus Life Space, via Bocconi 12, mezzanine floor.

#### **Program**

GuiDance is a course whose classes are divided according to an AAA system:

1) **Awakening**

2) **Alignment**

3) **Assertion**

**Awakening**: a short but powerful warm-up session to awaken our bodies and feel in touch with our muscles and elasticity.

**Alignment**: that's the part we are all crazy about: THE BARRE! At the barre dancers find the right alignment and help their bodies before going to the center and dance

**Assertion**: the actual dancing part. Dancers assert themselves on the center by performing short choreographic phrases that enhance stamina, sense of rhythm and interpretation.

The AAA System was created by Marco Pelle and Beatrice Carbone to take the students through a dance class feeling like true ballet dancers!

The whole course “travels” through different music genres and eras: the 60s, the 80s, the 90s, the 2000s, musical, opera, movie soundtracks etc etc… That’s because inclusiveness is a language that belongs not only to people but also to artistic forms and expressions.

The same goes for the students attire: students are encouraged to come to class wearing something connected to a specific era they like, if they want to.

You can come in full attire or just wearing an accessory! Of course, if you don't have anything related to an era, or you don’t feel like wearing something specifically connected to an era, it's just as perfect! There is no dress code required except for what you are most comfortable in and, we suggest, socks!

GuiDance aims to be a fun journey: together we learn, we improve our musicality, our muscular tonus and we have fun. But most importantly: TOGETHER WE DANCE!!!!"

**Course registration fee: 50 €**

**Click on "Access as BOCCONI USER" > Campus Life > and select the product "Guidance"**

Students can register simply by paying the registration fee. Faculty and staff should first check availability by writing an email to [campuslife@unibocconi.it](mailto:campuslife@unibocconi.it)

[REGISTER HERE](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en/customer/account/login/)

text:
### Marco Pelle


In collaboration with Gaia Maria Lorusso, assistant choreographer.

Marco Pelle is an internationally renowned choreographer. Recipient of the 2016 Primi Dieci USA Award as one of the ten most influential Italians in the USA, Mr. Pelle began his dance training in his Italy, continued in Monaco before moving to New York City to study with Merce Cunningham. He’s been one of New York Theatre Ballet’s resident choreographers since 2012. In 2013 Mr. Pelle also collaborated with great ballerina Alessandra Ferri on her comeback show "The Piano Upstairs". As an Opera choreographer he has worked extensively in the US and abroad. In Beijing, China, he has choreographed a total of four productions. In 2013 he choreographed "Passage", starring Mr. Bolle and Mrs. Semionova, a short movie directed by Fabrizio Ferri, with music by Fabrizio Ferri which opened Venice Film Festival. After many successful experiences in the US, in 2020, ballet star Roberto Bolle premiered Marco Pelle’s “LXIV” on the Italian national tv in his extremely successful tv show “Danza con Me”. In 2022, Mr. Pelle created two new projects sponsored by the Italian Culture Institute in New York and the italian Cultural Institute in Washington, DC, titled “AbunDance” and “Statuesque”. In 2022 he’s also directed and choreographed Javier Rodriguez’ opera “Frida” at Michigan Opera Theater.

More info abuot Pelle's carreer and projects available in the interview conducted by Tg2 and part of the program "Tg2 Storie — I racconti della settimana"; you can find the interview on Raiplay ( [press](https://www.rai.it/ufficiostampa/assets/template/us-articolo.html?ssiPath=/articoli/2022/10/Tg2-Storie---I-racconti-della-settimana--0ea60ce4-90fe-49ba-9c19-f429e6b9ba31-ssi.html)).

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


**13 NOVEMBER 2025: GUIDED TOUR AND WORKSHOP**

_**“M.C. Escher. Between Art and Science” at Mudec, Milan**_

_**“Paradox as a Critical Lens: Breaking Symmetry”**_

The exhibition **M.C. Escher. Between Art and Science**, at MUDEC in Milan from September 25 to February 8, 2026, brings back to the city, after a ten-year absence, one of the most fascinating and recognizable artists of the twentieth century, **Maurits Cornelis Escher** (Netherlands, 1898–1972).

Through a project entirely dedicated to him, the exhibition offers a fresh perspective on his artistic journey.

Known for his impossible architectures, optical illusions, tessellations, and metamorphoses, **M.C. Escher** created a unique visual language that bridges art and mathematics. A mathematics not made of the abstract reasoning of professional academics, but of long, meticulous, and passionate work, rooted in a more intuitive and perceptive approach.

In **Escher**, art and science merge into a vision that is rigorous, inventive, and deeply personal, a guiding thread that strongly defines the exhibition.

**The workshop**, inspired by Escher’s works such as _Circle Limit III_, _Order and Chaos_, and _Development_, invites students to reflect on order, symmetry, norms, and perception through a guided analysis of the exhibition.

During the workshop, students, divided into groups of four, create a visual map of social paradoxes. Starting from a regular graphic pattern representing order, they choose a social theme such as marginalization, gender stereotypes, inequalities, conflicts between conformity and identity, or discrimination, and express it by intentionally altering the pattern: interrupting, deforming, or contaminating it with colors, marks, and words—just as Escher moves from order to chaos.

The workshop concludes with a collective presentation, where each group shares their work and reflects on how change can challenge the apparent order we usually observe.

_**When:**_

_**13 November at 6:15pm**_

- **6:00 pm:** Meeting with the tour guide outside the building
- **6:15 pm:** Beginning of the guided tour followed by the workshop
- Language: English
- Available spots: 25
- Duration: about 90 minutes

The activity is open also to Faculty and Staff Members, but priority will be given to students.

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


#### Directing Between Prose and Poetry: a Pasolinian Approach to Cinema

#### Course program

This course explores film direction through the critical thought and cinematic practice of **Pier Paolo Pasolini**, focusing on **cinema as a form of language** rather than a purely technical or narrative medium. Students will examine the distinction between “ **cinema of prose**” and “ **cinema of poetry**” as two different ways of constructing meaning, perception, and point of view.

The course combines **theoretical analysis** with **hands-on exercises** to help students understand how form shapes content in film. Through film analysis, practical shooting exercises, and editing experiments, students will learn the fundamentals of camera grammar, point of view, time, montage, and sound. The second part of the course is dedicated to documentary practice, developed first through a conventional, prosaic approach and then through a poetic, non-explanatory one. By working on the same themes with different formal strategies, students will directly experience **how cinematic language transforms reality**.

The course concludes with the production and discussion of both **collective and individual documentary works**, providing students with a critical and practical framework for contemporary film direction.

#### When and where

- 24 February
- 3-10-30-31 March
- 27-28 April
- 5 May
- 11 May: Closing night

The course will be held **in English**, at **6.15pm — 8.15pm**, in **Room 42.**

On **30–31 March** lessons will be held in the music room at Via Bocconi 12 (entrance via the driveway).

**Course registration fee: 35 €**

**Click on "Access as BOCCONI USER" > Campus Life > and select the product**

[REGISTER HERE](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en/customer/account/login/)

FIND OUT MORE ABOUT THE INSTRUCTOR

### Tommaso Santambrogio


Tommaso Santambrogio is a young Italian director; he has lived between Milan, Paris, Rome, Florence, and Havana, and has collaborated with several internationally renowned filmmakers, such as Werner Herzog and Lav Diaz.

His first short films (Escena Final and Los Océanos Son Los Verdaderos Continentes) were both screened in 2019 at the Venice Film Festival and were then selected in many festivals around the world. L’Ultimo Spegne la Luce (2021), his latest short film, was screened in official competition at the 36th Venice Film Festival's Settimana della Critica, and then made it into the shortlist at the 2022 David di Donatello Awards. Taxibol (2023), his new docu-film, recently premiered at Visions du Réel. Oceans Are the Real Continents is his first feature film and will have its world premiere in competition at Venice Days during the 80th Venice Film Festival.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/arts-and-humanities/filmmaking#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


#### THE GREAT DISORDER

Since the beginning of this year President Trump has orchestrated a military assault in Venezuela, threatened to intervene in Colombia, Mexico, Cuba and again in Iran. He promised to annex Greenland, to downgrade Nato and EU. He dispatched thousands of federal agents to Minnesota and several other states and big cities, all ruled by Democrats; he launched, as a retribution, law suits agains the head of the Federal Reserve and other high ranking officials; he ignored the Congress, the Supreme Court and the Constitution.

Year 2026 was just at the end of its second month, when United Sates together with Israel (possibly the latter together with the former) started a war on Iran. The American democracy is in danger. And, accordingly, the world order.

_**Content and objectives**_

Our discussion will focus on:

- DONALD TRUMP – AN IMPERIAL FOREIGN POLICY
- MIDDLE EAST – THE FOREVER WARS
- UKRAINE & GREENLAND – EUROPE IN THE LABYRINTH
- ULTIMATE WEAPON – THE NUCLEAR ARMS RACE

The approach will be more journalistic than academic. Students are invited to participate actively in the course, whether in the form of answering the questions, interrupting with their own questions, or engaging in productive conversation, in order to:

- expand their knowledge on key geopolitical issues;
- develop their critical and analytical skills when reading the news;
- share their ideas and participate in active discussions.

_**Calendar**_

- **15-22-29 April 2026**
- **6 May 2026**

Classes will be held **online** at **6:15-7:30pm**

**HOW TO REGISTER?**

**Contact us via e-mail to** [**campuslife@unibocconi.it**](mailto:campuslife@unibocconi.it) **if you did not register before the first class.**

FIND OUT MORE ABOUT THE INSTRUCTOR

### Ugo Tramballi


ISPI Senior Advisor in charge of the Institute’s India Desk.

Ugo Tramballi is a veteran journalist, he joined the Metropolitan section of “Il Giornale” in 1976. He served as the newspaper’s Middle East correspondent in Beirut from 1983 to ’87 with war correspondences from Lebanon, Iran, Iraq, Afghanistan and extensive coverage in India and Pakistan. He was Moscow correspondent from 1987 to ’91 and then Global Correspondent and columnist with the Italian Economic newspaper “Il Sole 24 Ore”. He is a member of the Istituto Affari Internazionali, IAI, Rome, as well as a World Economic Forum’s Media Leader. On ISPI’s website, he publishes a weekly blog on international affairs, “Slow News”.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/arts-and-humanities/geopolitics-corner-understanding-world-around-you#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


_Big questions from Quantum Physics to Einstein’s Relativity to thrive in the world of tomorrow._

What are space, time and energy? What are the challenges that physicists are facing to understand fundamental phenomena in nature in the infinitely small, the immensely large or the utterly complex?  We shall discuss a selection of topics, from E=mcˆ2 to black holes and quantum entanglement, to stimulate curiosity and to provide basic knowledge that can help you comfortably gravitate our rapidly evolving world. We shall also touch upon how _science diplomacy_ can play a role in our quest for peace and sustainable development.

Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur and leader, a curious explorer of the universe, or simply a good citizen seeking a deeper understanding of reality around you, this course will ignite your curiosity and promote a strategic scientific mindset.

_**Structure**_

The course will be structured in 4 lectures that will include some virtual lab activity (RelativitApp, ScienceClic movies and other selected online resources).

_**Calendar**_

- Wednesday **29 October 6:15-7:30 p.m**. in presence **Room Manfredini**
- Wednesday **5-12-19 November 6:15-7:30 p.m**. **online on Blackboard**

Students who attend at least 3 out of 4 meetings will be issued an open badge.

**Registrations are closed**

FIND OUT MORE ABOUT THE INSTRUCTOR

### Anna Ceresole


INFN Sezione di Torino & Permanent Delegation of Italy to UNESCO, Paris Attachée, Expert in the Education Sector.

**Anna Ceresole** is Director of Research at the National Institute for Nuclear Physics (INFN) in Torino and collaborates with Bocconi University, where she teaches the interdisciplinary course "Gravitating the cosmos" since 2023. She holds a degree in Physics from the University of Torino and a PhD from the State University of New York at Stony Brook. She has held research positions at CALTECH, CERN, UCLA, and other leading institutions. Her scientific activity focuses on theoretical high energy physics and mathematical physics, with significant contributions in supergravity, superstring theory, black hole physics and quantum gravity. She has authored over 60 scientific publications and has played leading roles in national and international research projects, including EU networks and the ERC “Superfields” project. She has served on scientific advisory boards and evaluation panels across Europe and the US, and she is also active in science outreach and gender equity initiatives.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/arts-and-humanities/gravitating-cosmos#addtoany "Show all")

Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


**In via Bocconi 12** (floor -1 outside the Residence) a well-equipped and soundproof practice room will be available **only to Bocconi internal users (students, faculty and staff)**.

The room is open **from Monday to Sunday from 11 a.m. to 2 p.m. and from 5 p.m. to 10 p.m**.

To use the Music Room, please do the following:

- **Read the new** [**Regulations**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Regulations%20music%20room%202025_1.pdf?VersionId=ZB8985yaMt8DO5620Oy5hM0yz7bAX0dL) on the use of the Music Room and proper use of Equipment, **updated for the a. y. 2025/26**.
- **Pay a ‘one-off’ fee valid for the entire academic year (July-June) of 30.50 euros** to use the room by logging in with Bocconi User and selecting Campus Life > Music Room. **Important clarification: the fee is always valid until June of the academic year in which the payment was made, regardless of the month in which it was paid.**

**Practical examples:** If the fee is paid in **September**, the payment will be valid until June of the same academic year. From July, it will be necessary to renew the fee for the following year; if the fee is paid in **January**, it will still be valid until June and must be renewed from July.

- **Complete the dedicated booking procedure.**

Important: **Please note that the ‘ORDER ID’ is unique for the whole year and must always be entered in the booking platform, in the appropriate section**. You can see [**here**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Order%20ID%20ENG_1.pdf?VersionId=F6J58mgVwKq_Qjg.hycRb1dn_dMdqwZt) some examples of where to retrieve the Order ID.
- Bring **your university badge and a valid ID** with you when you go to the Music Room.

[1\\
\\
Read the new Regulations](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Regulations%20music%20room%202025_1.pdf?VersionId=ZB8985yaMt8DO5620Oy5hM0yz7bAX0dL "Download the regulations")

[2\\
\\
Pay the annual fee](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en/ "Click the link")

[3\\
\\
Book your slot](https://booking.unibocconi.eu/v2/ "click the link")

#### **Please note!**

- **It is possible to book a maximum of 2 sessions per day (each lasting 1 hour), whether consecutive or not, for a total of 2 hours of room use. In the case of bookings exceeding 2 daily sessions, any slot beyond the first 2 will be automatically cancelled by the system.**
- **If the reservation is not canceled and you fail to show up three times, the system will not allow you to make reservations for the next 15 days and reservations made on subsequent dates will be automatically canceled by the system.**
- The use of the Bocconi email address is recommended. **Please, note**: the reservation must be made **on behalf of the user of the Music Room**.

text:
## Sculpture course

**Presentation of the course and introduction of the participants and their creative skills**

The goal of the course is to stimulate imagination in order to foster creative action. _"REBUS"_, like the previous edition _"ICEBERG"_, offers a journey of exploration that investigates the visible and the imperceptible—those unexplored territories where creative thought is born.

The skies and the oceans, reflecting each other, conceal depths invisible to our eyes and hold mysterious secrets that leave us speechless.

Participants will begin to familiarize themselves with the proposed materials through an individual approach, yet always oriented toward collective action.

In the first meetings, intuitions, emotions, and mysteries will emerge, helping to shape the participants' creative projects.

### When and Where

- 3 November
- 10  November
- 17 November
- 24 November
- 1 December
- 4 December

The course will be held **in-person in the Campus Life Area in Via Bocconi 12, at 6:15pm- 8:15pm.**

The activity is open also to Faculty and Staff Members, but priority will be given to students.

**Participation fee: 12 €**

**Click on "ACCESS as BOCCONI USER" and select the product "Visual and creative arts".**

[REGISTER HERE!](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en/customer/account/login/)

FIND OUT MORE ABOUT THE INSTRUCTORS

### Vincenzo Cabiati and Francesco La Fosca

**Vincenzo Cabiati** is a multidisciplinary artist who draws inspiration from various sources, including art, cinema, and architecture. He works with a wide range of mediums, such as painting, sculpture, photography, and ceramics. Cabiati's works often explore themes of sequence and the square, and he has exhibited internationally at prestigious galleries and museums.

**Francesco La Fosca** is an artist who lives and works in Milan. His work is characterized by a focus on light, reflection, and the dichotomy between strength and fragility. La Fosca often uses multiple materials to create his pieces, including drawings, sculptures, installations, photography, and video. He has exhibited widely and his works are included in several public collections.

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/arts-and-humanities/visual-and-creative-arts#accordion-boxed-107773)

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


GUIDED TOUR

We are delighted to offer you this guided tour that matches the uniqueness of a world-renowned masterpiece like Leonardo da Vinci's Last Supper, protected by UNESCO.

The Last Supper occupies the entire northern wall of the refectory of the Dominican convent of Santa Maria delle Grazie, which served as the gathering place for friars to eat, pray, and meditate during meals. Since the 14th century, in the Tuscan tradition from which Leonardo drew inspiration, this place has commonly been referred to as the "Cenacolo," a term that from the early 16th century has also been used to denote the pictorial representation of the Last Supper that usually adorned such environments.

If you desire to witness this great masterpiece with your own eyes, we are thrilled to accompany you on an unforgettable guided tour.

**When: 24 September 2024 at 6:30 pm**

\> 6:15pm: Meeting with the tour guide outside the building

\> 6:30pm: Beginning of the guided tour

\> Language: ENG

\> Available spots: 25

The activity will be open also to Faculty and Staff Members, but priority will be given to students.

**Participation fee: 20 €**

For those who have bought tickets, please note that they will be handed out **on the day of the visit at the museum entrance.**

[The initiative is sold out](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/cenacolo-vinciano)

In collaboration with

### Ad Artem

**Ad Artem** is a Milanese company **active since 1993** in the sector of cultural heritage enhancement.

It supports cultural institutions, from museums to exhibition organizers, from public to private companies, in the realization of cultural projects: Picasso, Raphael, Michelangelo, Caravaggio, Leonardo are just some of the greats of art that they have made familiar to the non-expert public to work.

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/cenacolo-vinciano#accordion-boxed-106970)

Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


- In-person walk-ins on Mondays, Thursdays and Fridays from 3 to 7 p.m. at Room 3, Campus Life Space, via Bocconi 12, mezzanine floor
- E-mail: [counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it)
- Tel. 02 5836 2083 Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. and 2 to 7 p.m.
- Microsoft Teams Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. and 2 to 7 p.m. by writing to “counseling”

If you need support during days and hours not covered by the Bocconi Counseling Service or during holidays and University closures, you can contact International SOS at +33(0)155 633 155

Bocconi Counseling Service is not an emergency service.

If you don't know where to turn in an emergency or out of hours, here are a number of options.

**Emergency numbers**

- **112**: In case of a medical or psychological emergency, you can contact this number 24h/24, including to request an ambulance.
- **1522**: In cases of perceived violence, you can contact this number free of charge 24h/24. Specialized operators will take requests for help and support from victims of violence and stalking.

**Services**

- In case of medical or psychological emergency, it is possible to go to the local hospital emergency room free of charge without an appointment.
- In cases of perceived violence, in addition to contacting the 1522 number free of charge, in the main Italian cities you can also access the Anti-Violence Centers in person, where you will find specialized and trained staff for psychological interviews for help, not only in Italian.

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


The psychological Counseling Service offers students the chance to refer to a profession counselor on mental health topics to discuss personal, relational or academic difficulties that may interfere with their academic progress and fully realizing their potential. This service is free of charge.

Find out more about the different opportunities that are available to all Bocconi students.

[Notice Board\\
\\
On 5 and 6 February all counseling appointments will be held online.\\
\\
Please check also the Walk-in Notice Board](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/walk "Walk-in Notice Board")

[**Group work activities**\\
\\
Experience has taught us that **issues of concern** for students are **often common and recurring**, which is why we thought we would offer a series of **small group workshops** with specific focuses. Participating in these workshops may already provide you with a number of useful **tools and tips** for learning how to **deal with situations and emotions** that might characterize a period in your university career. The group sessions are moderated by a Bocconi counseling team psychologist as group facilitator.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/study-support-activities " ")

[**Walk-in**\\
\\
The walk-in service is available for an informal and confidential consultation during which you can better understand what to expect from an individual counseling session. The service is provided by phone and via teams Monday through Friday and in-person three afternoons a week. No appointment is necessary to access the service. Meetings are available for “one shot” counseling only.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/walk " ")

[**Individual counseling**\\
\\
Bocconi counselors are available to students for individual counseling sessions aimed at helping them manage the various challenges they may encounter during their university experience. Whether the issues are related to academics, adjustment to university life, or are interpersonal in nature, counseling offers students a supportive, confidential and objective space to work through and resolve situations that are causing distress.](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/individual-counseling " ")

#### Discover the team of counselors who collaborate with the Counseling service

metadata.title: Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Counseling | Bocconi University

text:
### Giulia Rancati

Cognitive and cognitive-behavioral psychologist and psychotherapist, she is a member of SITCC (Italian Society of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy). She has collaborated with Bocconi Counseling Service since 2007. She is also an expert in the treatment of eating disorders and obesity, and since 2006 has worked in a specialized department of nutritional rehabilitation. Member of SIDBT (Italian Society for Dialectical Behavior Therapy), she is specialized in dialectical behavior treatment for personality disorders, borderline disorder and self-injurious adolescents.

metadata.title: Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Counseling | Bocconi University > Giulia Rancati

text:
### Chiara De Falco

Bilingual psychologist and clinical sex therapist, currently a transcultural psychotherapy apprentice at GRT (Gruppo per le Relazioni Transculturali) in Milan. Since 2018, she has worked with young adults in multilingual clinical and socio-educational settings in Italy and abroad. Alongside her private practice, she delivers training in schools and social services on affectivity, DEA, and digital well-being, using interactive group-based methods and educational software. She is also an intern at CPS - Complex Operating Unit of Psychiatry 2, Department of Mental Health and Addictions, ASST Fatebenefratelli-Sacco.

metadata.title: Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Counseling | Bocconi University > Chiara De Falco

text:
### Alexandra Kraslavski

Clinical Psychologist and Psychotherapist, specialized in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy at the Milan school of Studi Cognitivi. An EMDR therapist and practitioner with expertise in psychotraumatology, she is trained in CBT-E for the treatment of eating disorders and obesity. She is a member of the EMDR Italy Association and the Italian Society of Cognitive and Behavioral Therapy (SITCC). Her clinical and research experience spans various settings, from hospital to outpatient care, integrating evidence-based protocols with a particular focus on the therapeutic relationship.

metadata.title: Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Counseling | Bocconi University > Alexandra Kraslavski

text:
### Antonio Danzi

Bilingual psychotherapist and counselor. Trained in London at the Tavistock and Portman NHS Trust, he is registered with professional bodies in the UK and Italy. He has extensive experience supporting young adults in universities, public mental health services, and the non-profit sector. His approach is relational, psychodynamic, trauma-informed and psychoeducational. At Bocconi, he offers a safe and confidential space for students to explore issues such as stress, academic pressure, anxiety, relationships, cultural adjustment, grief, and questions of identity or sexuality (LGBTQI+).

metadata.title: Counseling | Bocconi University

text:
### Milena Milani

Educational Psychologist and Psychotherapist. She is a Feuerstein mediator, a Theraplay practitioner and a member of the British Dyslexia Association. She has studied in both Italy and the US. Working mostly in English alongside a group of multilingual colleagues, she offers various services to the international and expat community living in Northern Italy and Ticino. She also teaches communication and bilingualism at several institutions.

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


In collaboration with the Lombardy Region and the relevant local Health Authority (ASST Santi Paolo e Carlo), starting in March 2026, Bocconi University will host a **free vaccination campaign against the Papilloma Virus (HPV),** with vaccinations administered on campus.

_**Why this matters**_

HPV infection is very common and is transmitted through sexual intercourse or contact. It is responsible for almost all cervical cancers and can also be associated with other HPV-related diseases (e.g. warts), involving both men and women. In addition, HPV-associated cancers do not only affect the uterine cervix: they can also affect the vulva, vagina, penis and anus. HPV vaccination is not mandatory, but strongly recommended.

The Lombardy Region has extended free access to the HPV vaccine up to the age of 26 (26 years and 364 days) and offers it to both women and men who hold a valid Italian health card.

Please be aware that the administration schedule requires 3 DOSES to be completed within a maximum of 1 year.

_**How it works at Bocconi**_

- **Where:** At dedicated spaces on campus
- **When: Starting in March** (detailed **calendar** and availability will be communicated at a later date)
- **Booking: A booking system** will be available **after the closing of pre-registrations** (links and instructions coming soon)
- **Reception and safety:** A **pre-vaccination triage** will be completed prior toadministration; in the event of concerns or precautions, the physician in charge of the vaccination session will intervene.

_**Administration schedule (in summary)**_

- 3 doses (recommended time between doses: 0+2+4 months, but NOT MORE THAN one year). Example: first dose in March, second dose in May, third dose in September.

You are eligible to participate if:

- you are under the expected age range (26 + 364 days) to receive the administration of the first dose in March 2026
- you have never received a vaccination against papilloma virus
- you hold an Italian National Health Service (SSN) health card
- you know you will be able to receive the other two doses (in May and September)

Please indicate your interest in participating in the vaccination campaign (PRE-REGISTRATION), by clicking on the link below **by 9 February.**

[_**Pre-Registration**_](https://forms.unibocconi.it/index.php?rif_quest=4135 "https://forms.unibocconi.it/index.php?rif_quest=4135")

Keep an eye on your yoU@B student diary! Everyone who has pre-registered will receive a **link to book the vaccine that you will be given on campus.**

Please feel free to write to [**vaccine.support@unibocconi.it**](mailto:vaccine.support@unibocconi.it) to request any other information, before and during the vaccination campaign.

Below is some useful information published on the Lombardy Region website: [Human Papilloma Virus (HPV)](https://www.regione.lombardia.it/wps/portal/istituzionale/HP/DettaglioRedazionale/servizi-e-informazioni/cittadini/salute-e-prevenzione/Prevenzione-e-benessere/papilloma-virus-hpv/papilloma-virus-hpv)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/free-anti-hpv-papilloma-virus-vaccination-campaign#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)

The service offers all Bocconi students and SDA students the opportunity to get professional psychological assistance for the development and achievement of their individual, academic and professional potential.

The psychological counseling supports students in managing the different challenges related to the years of university life.

This service provides a confidential support space to help students better understand and manage challenges they are facing related to academics, adjusting to university life in Milan, or personal and interpersonal dynamics.

#### **What to expect from Counseling**

The counseling sessions provide the opportunity to analyze your concerns, reflect upon the causes of your problems, and evaluate the possible related feelings and emotions; furthermore, the sessions aim to help you find different solutions to improve your individual situation, drawing on your personal resources and assisting you in discovering new and more effective strategies.

Counselling is structured with an **initial individual session**, which lasts up to one hour, followed, if necessary, by several **follow-up sessions**, which are usually shorter and are useful for supporting the implementation of the strategies provided, as well as addressing any difficulties or setbacks.

Counselling programs are medium-term and take place on a monthly basis (unless individual needs require a different arrangement, which can be discussed and agreed with the counsellor). The individual counselling service is, in fact, designed to offer **short-term, solution-focused support**, unlike psychotherapy, which usually involves a more in-depth and long-term process with regular and frequent sessions. If necessary, the university provides access to other public and/or private services.

A professional psychologist could help you gain a better understanding about your personal experience and contextualize it, supporting you in developing objectivity, the ability to switch perspective, and experience to face different kinds of problems. This could be useful to analyze complex issues from a different point of view, helping you feel better able to handle them.

It could be the case you are ready to face a long-standing conflict or concern from the past, or you are dealing with new challenges while living your university experience (e.g., complying with academic expectations, getting used to a different culture, or managing new social relationships): our counseling service could be the right solution for you.

We suggest that you avoid waiting too long, until the problem becomes even bigger but asking for an appointment while the situation still seems manageable, so that it is possible to solve it sooner.

#### **Counselors & Psychologists**

Bocconi University has been cooperating with a [team of professional counselors and psychologists](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/our-collaborators "Our Collaborators") for several years.

All of them are experts in supporting people from different backgrounds and cultures and have gained extensive knowledge of the specific features of the Bocconi university system.

Our Team adopts a **cognitive-behavioural** approach, a method that examines how our thoughts influence our behaviours, in order to identify targeted and specific strategies to address the personal and academic challenges.

metadata.title: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University

text:
Appointments are available throughout the year, except when the university is closed. They can be scheduled Mondays through Fridays between 8am and 4pm. Students can choose how they would like to attend the session:

- In person (Campus Life Offices at Via Bocconi 12 – access next to the Bocconi Residence Hall);
- Remote on Microsoft Teams.

To make your **first** appointment with a Bocconi counselor, you need to access the **dedicated online procedure**, where you can check the availability for the next 4 weeks and select an appointment based on the language you would like to use during the session (to make an appointment in French, please select Counseling ITA/ENG option).

Unless the online mode is specified, all appointments are intended as in-person mode. However, sessions can still be requested to be held online by specifying this via email to [counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it) after you have completed the booking procedure.

Once you have completed booking your first appointment, you will receive a confirmation email summarizing the details of your appointment.

**Please note**: This method can only be used to book your first appointment with a Bocconi counselor. Any subsequent appointments will be agreed upon and scheduled directly during the session with the counselor.

[Access the dedicated booking procedure](https://app.unibocconi.it/Appointments/Studente/indexcounseling)

If you need to **change** your appointment, you must cancel the one that has already been scheduled (at least 24 hours in advance) and book another slot among those available. A second session cannot be booked before canceling the first one.

If you are unable to attend, we ask you to **cancel** your appointment in a timely manner ( **at least 24 hours in advance**) so that other students can book that slot.

To cancel the appointment, you must log in to the booking system with your Bocconi account and go to "my reservations", select the appointment you intend to cancel and click "cancel".

If you are unable to cancel your appointment at least 24 hours in advance, we ask you to write to [counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it) as soon as possible. We will cancel your appointment for you. This will allow you to book a new first appointment.

In the event you do not notify us of your absence from an uncanceled booked session, you will be able book a new counseling appointment starting three weeks after the missed appointment.

If you would like to better understand what a counseling interview consists of, or you are waiting for a first appointment, you can contact the Walk-In service for an informal individual session (in person or by phone) without the need for an appointment.

We also encourage you to look into the other counseling services available:

- Workshops dedicated to specific topics (study methods, managing anxiety, time management, etc.)
- Group sessions, during which peers can discuss academic or personal problems

metadata.title: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University

text:
### FAQ: About Counseling

How do I understand if I need help?

The Counseling service aims to help you deal even with those problems which could seem insignificant.. If the issue interferes with your daily life and you do not feel comforted enough after talking to your friends and family, a professional counselor or psychologist could be helpful.

What kind of issues can I talk about?

The Counseling Service’s objective is to support students facing those issues related to university and/or which could interfere with your experience at Bocconi University and with the realization of your full academic and personal potential. You can discuss problems that either relate to your academic life or are outside of the academic scope.

What should I expect from counseling?

Your Counseling journey begins with a first session, where you and your counselor work together to elaborate on your personal experiences, reflect on your issues from a different point of view, and work on ways to overcome them. It might be necessary to schedule additional individual sessions after the first one: this could be the case if the issues are especially complex or if it takes longer to deal with them. , Regardless, we aim to guarantee an efficient and personalized service.

Will my counselor tell anyone about my issues?

The Counseling sessions are strictly confidential; consequently, the information you provide will not be shared with others, unless you give your consent. Exceptions will be applied for legal reporting obligations or dissemination statutory only, or in case of high risk for your personal health or for other people.

What if I need additional help besides the Counseling Service offers?

Sometimes, it might be necessary to consider an additional psychological support which goes beyond the counseling offered by Bocconi University. If your counselor believes you could benefit from a deeper level of professional support, this will be discussed with you by your counselor and you will be provided references of professional resources after a second-level Counseling session.

The second-level Counseling service aims to help students who have already taken part in first-level Counseling services and who have highlighted particularly difficult and significant situations of difficulty which interfere with their academic life and the realization of their potential. The second-level Counseling objective is to identify the main issue of the students and, specifically, to help them find the appropriate external support resources.

What is the difference between the university counseling services and individual therapy?

Bocconi University Counseling Service is meant to be a short-term support service focused on finding solutions to students’ issues, while individual therapy is often a more structured and long-term service, characterized by recurrent and frequent sessions.

What should I do if I think someone I know needs help?

If you worry about a friend/relative of yours or your partner, you may schedule a single counseling session to share your concerns and understand how you can provide help.

metadata.title: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University > FAQ: About Counseling

text:
### About your appointment

How long is the waiting time for an appointment?

This varies according to requests. We are generally able to give an initial appointment within a maximum of 15 days after the initial request. If you feel you have a particular urgency, you can let us know by email.

How should I get ready for my appointment?

There is no preparation needed, you simply need to come to the appointment. The individual session will develop naturally as you begin talking with your counselor.

metadata.title: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Individual Counseling | Bocconi University > About your appointment

text:
What if I need to reschedule or cancel my appointment?

- If you are unable to attend your first appointment, we ask you to cancel it in a timely manner (at least 24 hours in advance) so that other students can book that slot.

To cancel the appointment, you must log in to the booking system with your Bocconi account and go to "my reservations", select the appointment you intend to cancel and click "cancel".

If you are unable to cancel your appointment at least 24 hours in advance, we ask you to write to [counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it) as soon as possible. We will cancel your appointment for you. This will allow you to book a new first appointment. In the event you do not notify us of your absence from your first uncanceled booked session, you will be able book a new counseling appointment starting three weeks after the missed appointment.
- If you need to modify or cancel your non-first appointment, please send us an e-mail at least 24h before to cancel or reschedule it; this way, we will be able to assign your time-slot to another student.

Our counseling service is in high demand. Consequently, it is essential for students to inform us in advance if they will be no longer able to attend their appointment so that someone else can benefit from the counseling service as early as possible.

What if I miss my appointment?

- For first appointments: In the event you do not notify us of your absence from an uncanceled booked session, you will be able book a new counseling appointment starting three weeks after the missed appointment.
- For non-first appointments: If you did not inform the Counseling office in advance, your name will be put at the end of the waiting list in order for you to be considered for the next appointment available.

How long is the Counseling session and in what languages is the service delivered? How long can I benefit from the Counseling service?

Counseling is structured with an **initial interview** on an individual basis, which lasts for a maximum of one hour. This may be followed by a number of **follow-up sessions**, which are usually shorter, designed to support the implementation of the suggested strategies and address any potential difficulties or setbacks. The duration of a session can vary based on the personal needs of each student.

Even if the number of sessions is not set, the Individual Counseling is meant to be a short-term service with a frequency of 1 session/month almost; moreover, it should not be considered as a replacement for individual weekly psychotherapy service.

Individual counseling sessions are available in Italian or English.

Can I speak to a different counselor if I have already had some sessions?

Yes, at the end of your session, if for any reason you wish to make an appointment with another counselor, you can request it by email at [counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it)) or by calling 02/58362083.

Can I see the same counselor I saw in the past?

Yes, if you wish you can make a request by email at [counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it](mailto:counseling@unibocconi.it)) or by calling 02/58362083, specifying that you have already used the Counseling services in the past.

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Transforming opposition into mutual benefit

Discover how to interpret and navigate power dynamics, transforming opposition into mutual benefit.

This can be useful today at university, enhancing your relationships with teachers and classmates, and tomorrow in the workplace with colleagues, bosses, clients, and various types of stakeholders.

Drawing on the parallels between the values of martial arts and management skills (focus, self-awareness, agility, balance, determination), the course helps you build effective relationships, manage stress, analyze competitors, and select the best strategies for critical situations.

**Please note:** No knowledge of martial arts is required

The course is divided into two distinct parts: a practical session in the gym, where you will directly experiment with martial techniques, and a theoretical session in the classroom, which serves as a debriefing of the previous experience.

It offers three main takeaways:

- Understanding interlocutors
- Managing conflicts
- Identifying and overcoming different types of resistance

_**Calendar:**_

- 20 March at 6:15-8:15pm - practical experience
- 22 March at 9 am - 1pm - debriefing with method consolidation

Classes will take place in the Campus Life Space, via Bocconi 12

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


In an effort to support our wellbeing , to improve our own awareness of the relationship between mind and body and to achieve a psychophysical balance, members of the Bocconi community can access an **on campus Mindfulness Area.**

This area is meant to be a space for reflection, and individual mindfulness and meditation practices.

Mindfulness Area Guidelines

This area is available to any member of the Bocconi University Community who is seeking a quiet, simple place to reflect or meditate. You will have the chance to disconnect from the business of your day and reconnect with yourself.

This space is located in the **Campus Life Area, at Via Bocconi 12** (mezzanine floor) and it is **open from 7:30am to 9:30am and from 12:30pm to 2:30pm Mondays through Fridays.**

To access the area, reservations must be made using the dedicated online procedure.

You are also required to report to the residence hall Reception desk on the mezzanine floor with your Bocconi badge, to pick up the keys to access the meditation area.

If you don't know where to start with meditation or if you want to know more about it, have a look at the **dedicated self-help** **resources** that you can find [**here**](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_it/albero+di+navigazione/home/corsi+di+studio/studenti+iscritti/campus+life/counseling%2C+self-empowerment+e+wellbeing/counseling/self+help+resources_ita).

In order to allow everybody to make use of this area, users are required to follow these basic rules:

- Prior to entering the area, it is required to register at the reception desk at the Bocconi Residence Hall
- This area cannot be used as a lounge, study room, meeting room, or for other purposes
- No items are to be removed

- Users are asked to leave the area in the same condition as it was in prior to use
- Silence and mutual respect are expected at all times; music can be listened to through earphones or earbuds with any musical device (e.g., iPods, MP3 players, etc.)
- Food and beverages are not permitted
- Mobile phone use in the space is for emergency use only; mobile phones must be placed on silent upon entry
- Candles, burning oils, incense or any other flammable items are not permitted

The complete regulations are available in the "attachments" section.


[Book you slot in the mindfulness area!](https://booking.unibocconi.eu/v2/)

### Attachments

- [Mindfulness area regulations](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Regolamento%2BArea%2Bmindfulness%2Beng%2Bdef.pdf)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/counseling-self-empowerment-and-wellbeing/mindfulness-area#addtoany "Show all")
