text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


#### ON THE FIELD PROJECTS AND INTERNSHIPS AT SOCIAL ENTERPRISES AND IN THE THIRD SECTOR

Are you interested in working on a project you believe in while at the same time completing a meaningful personal and professional experience? Would you like to see your work make a tangible impact on the community?

Then **Making Sense of Profits is for you!**

This project is coordinated by the CERGAS Research Center. It offers students the opportunity to get first-hand professional experience, combining working in the field of economics with making a social impact.

**Dynamo Camp, Fondazione Cave Canem, ADMO, Fondazione Medici Senza Frontiere** are just some of the organizations involved in the upcoming edition.

The best projects will be recognized and, if they meet the requirements, may also be converted into an internship experience or be involved in the area of the [Social Impact Recognition](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/social-engagement/bocconi-social-impact-recognition "Bocconi Social Impact Recognition").

**Application period:** extended deadline **by 11 May 2026**

More details on the 15th edition are available on the CERGAS Research Centre website [**at this link**](https://cergas.unibocconi.eu/research/social-policy-and-social-innovation/dai-un-senso-al-profitto) .

For more information: [cergasociale@unibocconi.it](mailto:cergassociale@unibocconi.it) — [campuslife@unibocconi.it](mailto:campuslife@unibocconi.it)

Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


**Bocconi University recognizes social engagement and responsibility as values that are integral to its institutional identity**.

At Bocconi there are several opportunities made available for students to do some volunteer activities, in Milan and beyond. In addition to the opportunities reported through the network managed by CSV and conveyed through the Volunteer Desk, there are several opportunities both inside and outside the university to donate your time.

#### **Activities organized by the University**

- [**Making Sense of Profits**](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/campus+life/social+engagement/dai+un+senso+al+tuo+profitto) **project**
- [**Learning Needs Peer to Peer tutoring project**](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/campus+life/counseling%2C+self-empowerment+and+wellbeing/self-empowerment/learning+needs+peer+to+peer+tutoring+project+tutor+eng) **for SpLDs students**
- **Volunteering opportunities in collaboration with Bocconi Inclusion Service** (for more information [inclusion@unibocconi.it](mailto:inclusion@unibocconi.it))

#### **Students for Humanity**

This student association has been active in the university for many years and promotes various social engagement activities open to students. [Find out more on their website!](https://studentsforhumanity.it/)

#### **Other Activities and External Opportunities**

- **AVIS**: [Blood donation on campus](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/campus+life/social+engagement/blood+drive+with+avis+milano-eng)
- **Pane Quotidiano**: Pane Quotidiano is a nonreligious and nonpolitical non-profit organization located only 200 meters from the Bocconi campus. Every day, it supports the poorest segments of the population free of charge, providing them with food and clothing. For additional information please click [here](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/campus+life/social+engagement/pane+quotidiano+initiative).
- **Sottovoce — IEO and Monzino Milano:** Sottovoce is a volunteer association that aims at involving volunteers in various departments. For additional information please check the [Italian website](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_it/albero+di+navigazione/home/corsi+di+studio/studenti+iscritti/campus+life/impegno+sociale/impegno+sociale_altre+iniziative).

On the "on campus" dates, the Volunteer Desk is held **in presence from 10am to 5pm in Room 3**, via Bocconi 12 (mezzanine floor); **no appointment needed**.

When online, you will need to book an appointment via e-mail to [deskvolontariato@unibocconi.it](mailto:deskvolontariato@unibocconi.it)

#### **Types of activities**

The activities promoted by the volunteer desk are of different types and also involve varying commitment depending on the volunteer association of reference.

Some of the activities involve a commitment that is potentially compatible with the Bocconi Social Impact Recognition project. Others, on the other hand, require less time availability and therefore do not fall under the requirements of this project.

Thanks to the collaboration with CSV Milano and the associations in its network, you can find a wide range of occasional volunteering opportunities available both in Milan, throughout Italy and abroad. Discover all the occasional volunteering opportunities available for the upcoming weeks in this [webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/impegno-sociale/opportunita-di-volontariato-occasionale-aperte "Opportunità di volontariato occasionale aperte").

**Bocconi University recognizes social engagement and responsibility as values that are integral to its institutional identity**.

Because of this social commitment, the **Community and Social Engagement** project has been launched, with the aim of contributing to the personal development of the students, faculty and staff that make up the Bocconi community.

The University offers a number of ideas and suggestions on these issues, by creating not only moments for reflection, but also concrete opportunities for personal engagement.

text:
## Why volunteer?

The volunteer experience gives you the opportunity to give freely of your time, energy and skills to others and at the same time enrich yourself. But how?

- By gaining self-confidence and feeling useful by collaborating in a cause you care about;
- By putting your human and professional skills on the line. By donating your skills to receive more, experiencing new situations and opportunities for growth;
- By meeting new people who share the same goals with you;
- By enriching your resume with social impact activities;
- By improving your organizational, time management and communication skills.

In addition, starting from A.Y. 2023-2024, the university has also decided to formally recognize student engagement in social impact activities.


### Volunteer Desk

In collaboration with CSV Milano, to help you navigate the world of volunteering

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


The **Statute** is the **founding document of an association** (download mock statute in the attachment box below).

Because it allows associations to self-organize, Bocconi has not created a reference for statutes, but rather has indicated **principles** according to which statutes should be written.

**OBJECTIVES**

Remember that a student association may **not** have:

1. statute objectives in conflict with Italian law.
2. statute objectives in conflict with the university’s values and regulations.
3. aims and/or activities that overlap with the university’s institutional activities.
4. internal organizational processes that do not guarantee equal opportunities to all members.

**ATTENTION!** The Statute must contain and specify the following elements:

- Associations need to indicate a precise address on their statute but may not have their **registered office** on one of the Bocconi's campus addresses or on a generic location (i.e. in "Milan").
- If the name **“Bocconi”** is used in the name of the association, it must be accompanied by “students” or “studenti”. Name and Logo of the association must be coherent.
- **Objectives** and **scopes** of the association - that should not overlap with institutional activities — must be defined and presented in a clear and detailed manner.
- Elections of association’s bodies and heads (President, Vicepresident and any other body) must be held in a fully **democratic and transparent manner**. Heads of an association may not be presidents/vicepresidents in other associations.
- **Only Bocconi students** may be members of a Bocconi student associations. **Membership status and active positions within the association are forfeited upon graduation**. Members must have the same rights in terms of voting and standing for elections.
- **Transparent admission criteria** must aim to avoid any kind of discrimination. The association cannot select its members, rather it can only decline an application following a legitimate explanation.
- The association has to establish a **membership fee**. The amount of the fee does not have to be specified in the statute and can also be of symbolic value.
- The Statute must clearly state that the association **refrains from practicing any form of discrimination**(such as, for example, discrimination based on race, religion, sexual orientation, gender or disability).

### Attachments

- [Mock Statute](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/MOCK%2BSTATUTE%2BNUOVO%2BENG.pdf)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/association-statute#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Bocconi values students' active participation in making the life on campus richer and full of interesting opportunities. For this reason, the University promotes student initiatives and supports the organization of their activities and events with rooms, funds and means of communication.

All student activities are approved by the CASA Committee (or its delegates) that operates by principles collected and periodically adjourned.

CASA Committee composition

The Committee for the Coordination of Student and Group Activities ( **CASA**) is in charge for the evaluation of the student associations' activities.

The **CASA Committee** is representative of the whole Bocconi community, as its **8 members** are chosen from each component of the community. At the moment these are the members of the Committee:

- **faculty members**: prof. Paola Profeta (President).
- **staff members**: Paolo Cancelli, Anna Airoldi, Silvia Tracchi, Erika Zancan.
- **students**: Kevin Gjoni, Carlo Maria Pagliula, Sabino Andrea Rubino

The Committee meets **9 times per year**.

CASA Committee meeting schedule

The CASA Committee is in charge of evaluating the approval  of events and the requests of funding for activities organized by students and associations.

**CASA Committee Meetings and related deadlines for AY 2025-2026**

|
|

| Meeting | Deadline to submit requests |
| --- | --- |
| 24/09/2025 | 17/09/2025 |
| 15/10/2025 | 08/10/2025 |
| 12/11/2025 | 05/11/2025 |
| 26/01/2026 | 19/01/2026 |
| 18/02/2026 | 11/02/2026 |
| 18/03/2026 | 11/03/2026 |
| 8/04/2026 | 01/04/2026 |
| 20/05/2026 | 13/05/2026 |
| 7/07/2026 | 30/06/2026 |

In this webpage you can find the documents of reference, that are **FUNDAMENTAL** to know in order to plan your activities in an achievable way. It is the President's and Vice-president's duty to deeply know the content of such documents.

[**GENERAL REGULATIONS**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/CASA_GENERAL%20REGULATIONS_ENG%20220122025.pdf "GENERAL REGULATIONS")

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Selected by the **European Commission** as one of the pilot [European Universities](https://education.ec.europa.eu/education-levels/higher-education/european-universities-initiative) in 2019 and confirmed as successful alliance in 2022 under the Erasmus+ programme, [**CIVICA**](https://www.civica.eu/) brings together **10 leading European higher education institutions** in the social sciences, humanities, business management and public policy.

Under the leadership of SGH Warsaw School of Economics, CIVICA partners are developing a framework to **foster students’ engagement within the CIVICA alliance** while strengthening their **sense of belonging and** **shared identity**, deeply rooted in European values and heritage.

The [**CIVICA Student Engagement Fund (SEF)**](https://www.civica.eu/ambassadors/student-initiatives/student-engagement-fund) is introduced as one of the initiatives facilitating **bridge-building between nations, cultures, and languages**.

metadata.title: CIVICA Student Engagement Fund | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: CIVICA Student Engagement Fund | Bocconi University

text:
### Important information for Bocconi students and registered student associations

**The SEF is a** **mini-grant funding scheme aimed at supporting student-led joint initiatives within the CIVICA alliance.** It aims to foster **cross-campus collaboration among students** **and registered student associations** from different disciplines and backgrounds in tackling the important issues facing Europe today, and to further enhance the students’ experience within the alliance.

The SEF will support **up to 10 joint initiatives** per academic year across the alliance, to be implemented during the **Spring semester**, and foresees **one centralized call for application** in **Academic Year 2025/2026**.

_Before starting your application, please make sure **to read carefully** all information indicated in the **Call for Proposals AY 25/26** and in the **SEF Project selection and implementation rules:**_

- [_**Call for Proposals AY 25/26**_](https://www.civica.eu/files/user/Application_forms/SEF_Call_for_proposals_2025_26.pdf)

- [_**SEF Project selection and implementation rules**_](https://www.civica.eu/files/user/Application_forms/CIVICA_SEF_Regulations_2025_26.pdf)

metadata.title: CIVICA Student Engagement Fund | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: CIVICA Student Engagement Fund | Bocconi University > Important information for Bocconi students and registered student associations

text:
### Preparation of joint project proposals – process overview

The full process for Bocconi students is detailed here

- **STEP 1 - Form a project team and prepare the joint proposal:** team up with other CIVICA students/registered student associations, form a project team (and identify a team leader) and **prepare a detailed concept of a joint project/initiative** by completing the following **Application form template**:

  - [_**Application form**_](https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/view.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.civica.eu%2Ffiles%2Fuser%2FApplication_forms%2FSEF_Application_Form_2025.docx&wdOrigin=BROWSELINK) \- please read the instructions carefully
- **STEP 2 - Prepare the budget of the joint proposal:** prepare a cumulated budget for the joint project/initiative **(max total value 10,000 EUR)** and the partial budget **(max 3,000 EUR)** detailing the expenses to be covered by Bocconi and **fill out the Budget form template** here below:

  - [_**Budget form**_](https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/view.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.civica.eu%2Ffiles%2Fuser%2FApplication_forms%2FProject_Budget_Excel_SEF.xlsx&wdOrigin=BROWSELINK) \- please read the instructions carefully
- **STEP 3  - Obtain pre-approval on the initiative and budget by the CASA Committee @ Bocconi:** in order to obtain the required pre-approval by the CASA Committee, Bocconi students associations **must send via email to** [**civica@unibocconi.it**](mailto:civica@unibocconi.it) **AND** [**comitatocasa@unibocconi.it**](mailto:comitatocasa@unibocconi.it) the **Application form and the Budget form** duly filled **no later than 27 October 2025 at 1:00pm** (Milano time).

**- Please note: all proposals will be thoroughly discussed and formally evaluated during a dedicated extraordinary session of the CASA Committee scheduled for 3 November 2025.**

- **STEP 4 - Submit the joint proposal to the centralised SEF Committee:** after obtaining the official pre-approval of the CASA Committee, formally submit **the joint proposal (Application form and Budget form) AND proof of pre-approval** **by the CASA Committee** to the centralised SEF Committee **no later than 12 November 2025 at 1:00pm** as per the following instructions:

**a)** Access [**my.civica.eu**](https://my.civica.eu/) with the credentials from your home institution;

**b)** Open the link [**https://whaller.civica.eu/org/15773/forms/201**](https://whaller.civica.eu/org/15773/forms/201) still using the same browser and complete the online form.

text:
Only associations registered on the List may access the resources provided by the University, which include:
- **Spaces** on campus where activities can be organized
- **University** **funding** for events/activities organized by associations
- Several **communication tools** available to promote events/activities, including a **Bocconi e-mail account** created just for the association and a **personal page** on the University website.
#### **How to apply for AY 2025-2026?**

**Before starting the application for a new association, please make sure you have checked that an association with the same aims does not already exist.**

Requirements
- **Acknowledgment and acceptance of the Regulations**
- Presence of **at least 10 founder members**(Bocconi students): please download and fill out the [**PDF**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/founders%20and%20members%20new%20association.pdf) file that you will need to upload in the online renewal application;
- **Submission of all required documentation and information through the online form (STEP 1);**
- **Collection of at least 150 signatures in support** of the creation of the association (to be collected through the yoU@B online procedure) (STEP 2)
Deadlines and procedures
|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | When | What |
| 1st step | May 27th — June 3rd 2025 | Submit a registration request for the Associations List through the dedicated online form |
| 2nd step\* | June 6th-13th 2025 | Collect the signatures supporting the registration to the List, through the designated procedure in the yoU@B Diary. |
\*Please note! Only student associations that submitted the required online form by the deadline of the 1st step and that meet the minimum requirements (e.g. number of founders) will be able to continue with the 2nd step of the registration procedure.

Required documentation

In the online form you will need to upload the required documents, listed below, and declare some information regarding the association.

**Required documentation:**
- **Founders' List:** please download and fill out this [**PDF**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/founders%20and%20members%20new%20association.pdf) file

- **Statute**: in compliance with laws, codes of conducts and Bocconi University's principles and regulations, that includes admission criteria, manager's selection criteria, objectives and scopes and that states the absence of any kind of discrimination. For further information, click [**HERE**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/association-statute "Association Statute") **.**

- **Logo (if present):**it should be coherent with the name/acronym of the association as declared in the statute. Please find below the main indications for a compliant logo:

  - Using the colors and font of the university’s official logo is not allowed
  - If the full name of the association is used, then it must be complete
  - The acronym of the association may be used, provided it is unique
- **Motivational letter addressed to the CASA Committee**in which students need to indicate:

  - the main motivation that led them to found a new student association
  - the main differences from already existing associations that share similar objectives and aims
  - the added value the new association brings to the Bocconi student community
Step 1: Online application

On the online form, on behalf of the association, the president must:

1. Sign the regulations to attest their acknowledgement
2. Declare the following information:

- Contact information for the association’s president and vice president

- A brief report of the main and most significant activities that the association would like to organize during next academic year
- List of already existing partnerships and/or collaborations with student associations from other Universities and/or with institutional bodies that the association has already signed or would like to sign

- the online application must be completed **by one student only** for each association

Step 2: Signatures collection

Student associations that successfully completed the 1st step of the application, will need to collect the signatures supporting the registration to the List, through the designated procedure in the yoU@B Diary.

Each student will have the possibility to sign for up to 3 different associations.

Results

The requests will be evaluated by the CASA Committe on July 2nd 2025.

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)

#### **How to join a student association?**

Every year student associations have the possibility to introduce themselves to the Bocconi Student Community on the occasion of two dedicated events, called **Associations on Display,** in September and February.

Usually student associations start their **application campaigns** during Associations on Display, therefore this is the right moment if you want to join an association.

Check out the [**annual association list**](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/attivita-studentesche/elenco-delle-associazioni "Elenco delle associazioni") to find out the one that suits your interests!

#### **How to create a new association?**

If you think that none of the already existing student associations fits you, it could be interesting for you to know that any Bocconi student can create a Bocconi student association, provided that:

1\. Its statutory objectives are not in conflict with Italian law

2\. Its statutory objectives are not in conflict with the University's regulations and values

3\. Its aims do not overlap with the University’s institutional activities.

However, in order to support student associations and allocate the resources available, Bocconi has established the [**Association List.**](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/attivita-studentesche/elenco-delle-associazioni "Elenco delle associazioni")

Requirements and methods for applying for the registration of a new association are indicated in the [**General Regulations.**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/CASA_GENERAL%20REGULATIONS_ENG.pdf?VersionId=KOvIidUYJ28IzjZOxHA3B107BMYT9Xyv "CASA Committee General Regulations")

text:
Requirements
1. Submission of the **renewal application by the set deadlines and procedures** together with all the **required documentation**
2. **Acknowledgment and acceptance of the General Regulations and the Operational Regulations**
3. Presence of **at least 20 members** (Bocconi students)
**Please note:** **ALL members** must fill out a dedicated form in the agenda to attest membership.
4. Organization of **at least one event** during the academic year, or of an **activity held in collaboration with the University** or **communication activities approved by the CASA Committee** after the evaluation of the “communication plan”. Other types of activities approved by the CASA Committee may be taken into consideration, after verifying the commitment and involvement of the association.
Required  documentation

_**1\. Statute**:_

in compliance with laws, codes of conduct and Bocconi University's principles and regulations, that includes admission criteria, leadership selection criteria, objectives and scopes and that states the absence of any kind of discrimination.

During the renewal process the association must declare **whether the statute is exactly the same as the one previously submitted and approved or  whether the statute has been updated/amended and not yet submitted to the CASA Committee**.In the first case a copy of the current statute must be uploaded. In the latter case it is necessary to submit the updated statute **highlighting the differences** from the last one for which approval is requested for a.y. 2026-2027.

For further information about the structure of the statute, please [**CLICK HERE**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/campus-life/association-statute) **.**

_**2\. Logo** (if present)_

During the renewal procedure and before uploading the logo, the association must indicate whether the uploaded logo has remained the same as the one already approved and used or whether the uploaded logo is different from the one already approved and therefore the association wants to change/modify the current logo (in this case before using the new logo, the association will need to wait for its approval by the CASA Committee).

Please find below the main indications for a compliant logo:
- Using the colors and font of the university’s official logo is not allowed
- If the full name of the association is used, then it must be complete
- The acronym of the association may be used, provided it is unique
_**3\. A document attesting the organization of:**_
1. **At least one event during the academic year:** please attach the promotional material
OR
2. **Activities carried out in cooperation with the University:** pleaseattach a brief description of the initiative and the office involved
OR
3. **Communication activities approved by the CASA Committee** after the evaluation of the communication plan: please attach the email attesting the submission of the final output or other confirmation
In the absence of at least one of the three above-mentioned documents at point 3, it will be possible to request the evaluation of the renewal application by submitting documentation proving another type of initiative, approved by the CASA Committee, that demonstrates the Association's commitment and involvement.

When

_**April 29, 2026 to May 7, 2026**_

This timeline applies to:
- **completion of the online renewal application** by an association contact person
- **confirmation of membership to be made by each association member** through the **widget**"STUDENT ASSOCIATIONS' RENEWAL: CONFIRM YOUR MEMBERSHIP" **available in the homepage of their yoU@B (** _**desktop version only**_ **).**
How

To submit the renewal application, the **online form must be completed by 7 May 2026.**

On the online form, on behalf of the association, the president must:

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


All student activities are approved by the CASA Committee (or its delegates), that operates by principles collected and constantly adjourned in a document available here. It is **FUNDAMENTAL** to know its contents in order to plan your activities in an achievable way: here you can download, read and consult the [**CASA Committee Regulations.**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/casa-committee-and-regulations "The CASA Committee and the Regulations")

#### **ORGANIZING AN EVENT**

To present you request you have to fill in this [FORM](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/FORM-rich-spazi-x-EVENTI.pdf) \- which will be evaluated by the CASA Committee - and deliver it to Campus Life, the Technical Secretariat of the CASA Committee.

Pay attention: in order to realize your events you need to submit your request in time! Check in the Regulations the deadlines you need to follow; you can also check online the next meetings of the CASA Committee.

After the Committee's approval you can interact with all the Bocconi offices that are involved in the event organization. Campus Life is available for information at each stage of the process.

#### **GETTING FINANCIAL SUPPORT**

Each year Bocconi provides funding for initiatives organized by students for students.

The request for funding has to be presented together with the request for event approval by the CASA Committee by filling out this [FORM](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Domanda-di-finanziamento-delle-attivit%25C3%25A0-culturali-organizzate-dagli-studenti-2014.pdf) and delivering it to Campus Life paying attention to deadlines as above specified.

**Remember!**

- Consult the CASA Committee Regulations in order to verify the admissible costs
- Respect the deadlines for the presentation of the request to the CASA committee
- You cannot ask for funding after an event

**IN ORDER TO RECEIVE A FUNDING:**

When funding is granted, reimbursement of expenses will only be carried by submitting to the Campus Life office:

- proper invoices, headed to Università Bocconi:

Università Bocconi

via Sarfatti 25 - 20136 Milano

C.F. 80024610158

P. IVA 03628350153
- a detailed report of the activity carried out
- event poster; which needs to state that "The event is sponsored by Università Bocconi".

**ANY DOUBT?**

Contact Campus Life, the Technical Secretariat of the CASA Committee. It is available for information at each stage of the process, and it can help you to plan and organize your event.

### More info

- [Next meetings of the CASA Committee](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation%20tree/home/programs/current%20students/campus%20life/student%20activities/student%20associations/student%20association%20activities/the%20casa%20committee%20and%20the%20regulations)

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Students at Bocconi play a leading role in making life on campus more exciting with meetings, debates, events and recreation.

If you want to learn more about Bocconi Student Associations and participate in their activities, do not hesitate to contact them directly at the contact details that you can find in their social media profiles or websites.

You can find complete information about the registered student associations on the [**Annual List webpage.**](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/campus+life/student+activities/student+associations/annual+list)

Here below you can find the most common activities that are held on campus.

#### **Associations on Display**

Associations on Display is the perfect moment to meet all student associations on campus.

Every year in September and February you can participate in this fair where student associations introduce themselves to the Bocconi student community. You can also gather additional information on their activities and on how to join them.

**Don't miss the opportunity to know all student associations!**

#### **On Campus Events**

Throughout the academic year, student associations organize lots of events on campus focusing on a variety of topics. Their guests are Bocconi Professors and experts in different sectors. They are unique opportunities to learn more about specific topics.

These events usually take place in the late afternoon and are open to all students. Registration may be required.

Every month, all students receive a newsletter that includes all the events organized by student associations. Associations are very active on social media, therefore we suggest you follow their profiles in order to stay updated on their latest initiatives. Very often you will also find promotional materials on campus with upcoming appointments.

### Links

- [List of Bocconi Student Associations](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation%20tree/home/programs/current%20students/campus%20life/student%20activities/student%20associations/annual%20list)

- [The CASA Committee and the Regulations](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation%20tree/home/programs/current%20students/campus%20life/student%20activities/student%20associations/student%20association%20activities/the%20casa%20committee%20and%20the%20regulations)

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Università Bocconi promotes student participation in the governance of university life, giving space to active student participation in the University's collegial bodies.

In fact, according to its Statute and Regulations, elections are held every two years to elect the following representatives:

- 5 student representatives of the **Faculty Council**;
- 2 student representatives of **University sports**;
- 3 student representatives of the **ISU Bocconi Board**;
- 1 student representative for each **Department Council**;
- 3 student representatives for each **School Council** (Undergraduate, Graduate and Law).

During those same elections, the Terna is also elected. This triad is made up of the three candidates to the University Board who take the largest number of votes during the elections, and the Javotte Bocconi Institution traditionally selects one student representative from among them to participate in **University Board** proceedings **.**

Once a year, however, elections are held for student representatives in **degree program Committees** (four students for Undergraduate Bachelor programs and the Combined Bachelor and Master of Science program and two students for Graduate Master of Science programs) and **class representatives** (two representatives for each study program class group).

Three student representatives elected for the Faculty Council, selected by the Student Council, may also actively participate in the **Academic Board** proceedings.

Lastly, student representatives contribute to the University life through the **Student Council**, an advisory body which coordinates student representative activity.

### Links

- [B.LAB - Unilab Network](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/blab)
- [List of representatives in office](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/student-representation)
- [Regulations](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/regulations)

text:
Types of Activities
- **Event**: an on-campus or online initiative open to the Bocconi community, that involves external guest speakers

- **Bocconi Community Meeting:** an initiative open to the Bocconi community that does not involve external guest speakers (examples include debates, presentations of the association, new member admission sessions, simulations, etc.)

- **Initiatives with Companies/Institutions:** an initiative held off-campus or online, that provides for the involvement or contact with a company and/or institution (examples include: company visits, active participation in trade fairs or conferences, visits to institutions or organizations, networking initiatives, etc.). These initiatives are reserved to the members of the proposing association only.

- **Internal Meetings:** meeting for members of an association or representation group
Guidelines for the Organization of Activities

**Here below you can find the main guidelines that student associations need to consider when organizing an activity. For complete and detailed information, you should to refer to the** [**CASA Committee Regulations.**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/casa-committee-and-regulations "The CASA Committee and the Regulations")
- Student association activities cannot overlap with institutional activities
- Plurality of viewpoints and opinions (i.e. no single-business events)
- Presence of a [**core faculty member**](https://didattica.unibocconi.eu/docenti/proff_ord_alfa.php?tit=1) as moderator (for "events")
- Uphold the values of diversity and inclusion
How to Submit an Activity Request

| | | --- | | #### **TYPE OF ACTIVITY**
_**Event (on campus or online)**_
- Mode: Concept Event
- Deadline: One week before each CASA committee meeting.
- Documents needed:
- Detailed program
- Biographies/CVs of speakers
- Confirmation of internal core faculty moderator
- Promotional materials
- Application for funding (optional)
_**Initiatives with companies/institutions (online or off campus)**_
- Mode: Pre-verification via email with Dr. Anna Airoldi and then via Concept event
- Deadline: One week before each CASA committee meeting.
- Documents needed:
- For preverification: [Request for permission for initiatives with companies](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Richiesta%2BCompany%2BVisit.pdf)
- For concept event:
- detailed program
- promotional materials
- Screenshot of pre-approval email received from Dr. Airoldi
_**Bocconi Community Meeting (on campus)**_
- Mode: Concept Event
- Deadline: At least 8 working days before the scheduled date
- Documents needed: 
- Detailed program
- Promotional materials (if applicable)
- Funding application (optional)
_**Internal Meetings (on campus)**_
- Mode: send an email to [esami\_aule@unibocconi.it](mailto:esami_aule@unibocconi.it) and [comitatocasa@unibocconi.it](mailto:comitatocasa@unibocconi.it). The email request should specify day and time (after 6:15 pm), number of students expected, type of activity, association name
- Deadline: At least 2 working days in advance
_**Internal Meetings (in the Association Area in Isonzo)**_
- Mode: Fill out [this form](https://booking.unibocconi.eu/v2/)
- Deadline: At least 24 hours in advance
For specific cases, additional documents might be required. Please refer to the Regulations for complete and detailed information. | Available Spaces, Resources and Tools #### **SPACES** - **Associations Area in Isonzo** for internal meetings - **On campus rooms** - **A dedicated desk for Associations on Display** #### **RESOURCES** For specific activities, student associations can request up to €100 in funding from the CASA Committee. This funding is a reimbursement for promotional expenses. For complete and detailed information, please refer to the CASA Committee Regulations. #### **TOOLS** - **Institutional email address:** every student association is given an email address. All the communications addressed to Bocconi Student Associations, including the minutes of each CASA Committee meeting, are sent to this inbox that should be checked regularly. **Please note**: the password must be updated at least every 3 months through the online procedure available at [https://sspr.unibocconi.it](https://sspr.unibocconi.it/) In order to request a password reset for the association's account, please follow these steps: - **send the request via B in Touch "Student resources and tools" > "Webmail"** - **Indicate the username and e-mail address of the association in the text of the e-mail** Colleagues who will take care of your request will send you the temporary password to use in sspr.unibocconi.it to create the new password. Please always consider that the response will reach you within 2 working days, particularly close to deadlines for submitting requests for events or other activities.

text:
Campus Life

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life)


Students are active players in the life of the University.

They gather in **associations** having diverse purposes: sports, recreational, educational, cultural, socioeconomic and political. They organise events, debates, seminars and many other opportunities to enrich the cultural life in Bocconi.

Every two years, Bocconi Students elect their own **representatives** in the University's collegial bodies and take part in the governance of university life.

Students are also main actors in keeping the Bocconi community up-to-date: they organise and manage a **webradio**, a **videochannel** and a **newspaper**.

Following you will find further information about:

- [**Student representation**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/student-reps "Student Reps")
- [**Student associations**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/student-associations "Student Associations")
- [**Student Media Center**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/student-media-center "Student Media Center")

text:
Campus Life

Overview


Make the most of your time!

Campus life is the point of reference for **cultural and artistic activities**, as well as **volunteer** and **social engagement** activities. Campus Life also coordinates the activities of the **counseling** service, which offers psychological support to students, and provides various courses and activities on **self-empowerment and wellbeing**. **Student association** activities are also coordinated by Campus Life.

Find out more about all the opportunities available so you can make the most of your university experience!

text:
All students **regularly enrolled** in a regular year of a degree program, master's degree program, or PhD program who have the requirements indicated in the table below can use the canteen service at a reduced rate at one of the designated **[dining areas](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/funding/2023-24+dining+areas)**:
|     |     |     |

|  | Preferential rate meal | Application |
| ISU Bocconi eligible students | **V** | **X** |
| Residents in one of the Bocconi Residence Halls at preferential  rate | **V** | **X** |
| Students who request preferential  rate canteen service only\*. | **V** | **V** |
\\* Students who comply with the **[economic requirements](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/funding/2023-2024+economic+requirements)** and intend to request the preferential  rate canteen service **will** **have to submit the dedicated online application from** 19th to 29th

October 2023;

**[Preferential rate canteen service application](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=10976&ordType=)**

The preferential  rate meal assistance will be valid starting **on December 1, 2023 until November 30, 2024.**

Preferential rate meals

The student who benefits from the preferential  rate canteen service can purchase a full meal at the rates indicated below, usually consisting of first course, second course, side dish, bread, fruit or drink.

The specific types of meal that can be purchased at a preferential  rate must be checked at the point of sale.

Students will be able to buy n. 2 meals per day, one for lunch (from 11:45 to 14:30) and one for dinner (from 19:00 to 20:30) at preferential  rate using their Bocconi ID card.

Prices and purchases

The process provides that the student proceeds to purchase meals through the online tool [UBsStore](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en), active in in the following periods:

It should be noted that prices mentioned above refers to a.y. 2022-23.

**Starting from 1st decmber 2023 the following new prices will come into effect, increased on the basis of inflation:**

\> 10 meals in 1st ISU BOCCONI bracket: € 30.00;

\> 10 meals in 2nd ISU BOCCONI bracket: € 38.00;

Once the meals have been loaded onto the card, Meals can be used in a maximum of two per day, one for lunch and one for dinner (according to the periods foreseen for the individual stores) for the purchase of a complete meal at preferential  rate.

**Please note:** In the event of loss and simultaneous request for a new Bocconi badge, to access the canteen service at a reduced rate, you must send an email with the subject "Loss of Bocconi badge" to [funding@unibocconi.it](mailto:funding@unibocconi.it) to change the code of your own badge.

It is specified that following the **revocation** of the ISU Bocconi Scholarship, students who **meet** the economic requirements envisaged in the academic year following the one in which the revocation is incurred and intending to request **only the canteen service at a preferential rate**, they can submit a specific online **application** within the foreseen period, even if they are not eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship.

text:
## Students with Disabilities

Financial Aid

For students enrolled in [Bachelor and Law programs](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/funding/tuition-waiver-students-disabilities-equal-or-greater-66-ay-2026-27 "Tuition waiver for students with disabilities equal or greater than 66% - A.Y. 2026-27") or [Master of Science and MA programs](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/master-science-and-ma-programs/funding/tuition-waiver-students-disabilities-equal-or-greater-66-master-science-programs "Tuition waiver for students with disabilities equal or greater than 66% - Master of Science programs a.y. 2026-27"), a full tuition waiver is provided in case of 66% disability recognized throughout Italy or greater.

Housing

The University has provided accommodations in each [residence hall](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/life-bocconi-dorms-ay-2026-27 "Life in the Bocconi dorms a.y. 2026-27") designed to assist students with disabilities. Those with 66% disability or greater have priority in the allocation of housing over other students.

Teaching and Exams

The University provides technologies that support classroom learning and individual study, including [Sensus Access](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/services/sensus-access "Sensus Access") (for converting documents into accessible alternative formats) and the Blackboard Ally plug-in, in addition to textbooks in digital format.

Regarding exams, compensatory and dispensatory measures are assigned according to medical documentation, which may include extra time or the use of calculators or a computer.

For more information, see the _Compensatory/Dispensatory Measures During Exams: Initial Request, Types and Deadlines_ paragraph of the [Guides to the University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/guide-university "Guide all'Università") _._

Library Services

The Library offers [dedicated services](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/about-us/accessibility-and-inclusion "Accessibility and Inclusion") to guarantee access to information and to provide accessible spaces, as well as reserved priority seating. In addition, it is an institution authorized by the Italian Ministry of Culture to produce accessible digital copies of works protected by copyright for people with neurological, visual or motor disabilities.

Mobility Companion Service

A special [mobility companion service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/mobility-companion-service-people-disabilities "Mobility Companion Service for People with Disabilities") is available to help people with disabilities move around campus.

Home-University Transportation

A [transfer service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/home-university-transfer-people-motor-disabilities "Home-University Transfer for People with Motor Disabilities") provides home-university transport for students, staff and faculty with motor disabilities.

Service Animals

Properly trained [service animals](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/equal-opportunities-and-belonging/campus-access-service-animals "On-Campus Access of Service Animals") can accompany people with motor disabilities, sensory disabilities, psychiatric disorders or serious illnesses on campus.

International Exchange Programs

metadata.title: Services for Students with Disabilities, SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs - Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Students with Disabilities

text:
The Study Abroad office examines the specific needs of every student with disabilities who may be interested in participating in an [exchange program](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/international-mobility "International Mobility"), collaborating with partner universities to identify the available services in terms of accessibility and support.

Entering the Workforce

The [Disabilities and Work (DAL) Service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/career-services/career-guidance-approaching-job-market/career-guidance "Career Guidance") supports people with disabilities when looking for internship and job opportunities. The Service organizes one-on-one counseling meetings, monitors application processes, and is the point of contact for employers seeking candidates in this category.

For more information, please visit the FAQ section on yoU@B > B in Touch > 'Inclusion, Disability and Wellbeing', where you can submit your inquiry.

You may also contact the [Inclusion Service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/governance-people-and-policies/inclusion-service "Inclusion Service") office to request an interview and learn more about the available services.

text:
## Students with SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs

Teaching and Exams

The University provides technologies that support classroom learning and individual study, including [Sensus Access](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/services/sensus-access "Sensus Access") (for converting documents into accessible alternative formats) and the Blackboard Ally plug-in, in addition to textbooks in digital format.

Regarding exams, compensatory and dispensatory measures are assigned according to medical documentation, which may include extra time or the use of calculators or a computer.

For more information, see the _Compensatory/Dispensatory Measures During Exams: Initial Request, Types and Deadlines_ paragraph of the [Guides to the University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/guide-university "Guide all'Università") _._

Library Services

The Bocconi Library is an institution authorized by the Italian Ministry of Culture to [produce accessible copies](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/about-us/accessibility-and-inclusion) of works protected by copyright for people with SLDs.

Learning Needs Help Desk

The [Desk](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/learning-needs "Learning Needs") was established to provide every student with an SLD the chance to receive individual counseling on the best study strategies to tackle the university experience.

Peer to Peer Tutoring Project

This [service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/peer-peer-tutoring-project "Peer to Peer Tutoring Project") aims to improve and optimize study abilities through peer support, provided by student volunteers who have received specific training.

You may contact the Academic Specific Needs office via B in Touch > Exams > Disability and Specific Needs.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/inclusion-disabilities-and-learning-disorders/services-students-disabilities-slds-adhd-and-other-specific-needs#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Inclusion, Disabilities and Learning Disorders

Overview


The **Inclusion Service** is a point of reference that aims to guarantee the same opportunities to everyone: it collaborates with other offices on campus to create a connected and unified community where everyone – students, faculty, staff and alumni – can feel welcome and included.


### Students with Disabilities, SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs

[Find Out More](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/inclusion-disabilities-and-learning-disorders/services-students-disabilities-slds-adhd-and-other-specific-needs)


### Anti-Discrimination Service Desk

metadata.title: Inclusion, Disabilities and Learning Disorders | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Inclusion, Disabilities and Learning Disorders | Bocconi University

text:
## Multiculturalism

### Multifaith Space

A new Multifaith Space has opened, designed to ensure that all Bocconi community members have an appropriate place to pray, sit in silence or reflect.

The entrance is located at Piazza Sraffa 6 (door on the left, first floor.) The space is open from Monday through Saturday 7:30am–10pm, and on Sunday 9am–10pm.

### Student Associations

As of today, there are more than 100 student associations on campus, including ones representing various international communities.

[Associations](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/campus-life/student-activities/student-associations "Student Associations") offer an opportunity to engage with people from different backgrounds who share common interests, thus becoming a point of reference and mutual support.

### Ethical-Religious Meals

The offering of halal, kosher and vegan meals in dining areas underscores Bocconi's commitment to recognizing and supporting the dietary needs of the entire community.

Options may be related to religious, ethical and/or cultural beliefs. [Find out more about our dining areas](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/funding/dining-areas-0 "Dining areas").

## Contact Us

For general information check the FAQs at yoU@B> B in Touch> Inclusion, Disabilities and Wellbeing and submit your question.

The [Inclusion Service](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/diversity-equity-and-inclusion/governance-people-and-policies/inclusion-service "Inclusion Service") office is also available for information about accessibility, support for students with disabilities and other requirements, as well as other inclusion aspects.

from Elementary to Advanced | The same of the wtitten examination (HSK) | 3rd year students, who have already passed the exams of their Degree Program first three semesters | Students who wish to register Chinese certification in their study plan as additional exams (4 credits in addition to 120/180/300 credits) must pass both the written examination HSK and the associated oral examination HSKK. By the score for the written and the oral, you get the final mark out of thirty. The final score is calculated as follows: 75% written part + 25% oral part. When the result score is expressed to decimal places, the result is rounded up when the decimal place is greater or equal to 0,5. The written examination and oral examination correspond to two independent certifications provided separately. We remind you that the acceptance of the language certification is subject to the  fulfillment of the applied rules and to the possibility to verify online the authenticity of the certification on the date of the language certification recognition (complete and correct one) submission. **CONVERSION TABLES**:

metadata.heading_path: DIPLE level B2 | Bocconi University

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)


Bocconi University is accredited test center for international certification of Business French, namely **DFP** **Affaires B1 and B2 level** issued by **CCI PARIS ILE-DE-FRANCE.**

Registration is open to students from Bocconi and other Universities or Schools.

Exam sessions are generally scheduled twice per year (Spring and Autumn Session).

Registration fees:

- DFP level B1, € 120
- DFP level B2, € 140

metadata.title: DIPLÔME DE FRANÇAIS PROFESSIONNEL | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: DIPLÔME DE FRANÇAIS PROFESSIONNEL | Bocconi University

text:
## **How to register for the exam**

The applicant must:

1\. pay the registration fee through the online procedure by the deadline for “Enrollment and payment period” scheduled for the session; registration is accepted only after payment;

2\. subimit the application (via the online procedure) during the "Enrollment and payment period" scheduled for the session

The registration fee will not be refunded in the case of absence on the date of examination.

metadata.title: DIPLÔME DE FRANÇAIS PROFESSIONNEL | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **How to register for the exam**

text:
## **Exam format**

The exam consists of two parts :

- Written computer-based test (80’ for B1 level; 2 hours for B2 level)
- Oral test (Preparation: 20' for B1 level, 15' for B2 level; Presentation: 10’ for B1 level, 15' for B2 level)

[Further](https://www.lefrancaisdesaffaires.fr/tests-diplomes/diplomes-francais-professionnel-dfp/affaires/) [information](https://www.lefrancaisdesaffaires.fr/tests-diplomes/diplomes-francais-professionnel-dfp/affaires/)

metadata.title: DIPLÔME DE FRANÇAIS PROFESSIONNEL | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Exam format**

text:
## **Sessions**

Please note that the date of each session will be confirmed only upon reaching a minimum number of registrations. As soon as the number is reached, the session will be marked " **CONFIRMED**". Otherwise, it will not take place and the registration fee will be refunded to all registered candidates.

In addition, please note that **the registration fee will not be refunded in case of absence on the date of the exam**.

- [**PAYMENT**](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_en/)(available only during the enrollment period indicated)
- [**REGISTRATION FORM**](https://forms.unibocconi.it/index.php?rif_quest=798)(available only during the enrollment period indicated)

For further information, it is possible to contact us in the following mode:

- **Bocconi students**: via the **B in Touch** procedure available on the [yoU@B](mailto:yoU@B "mailto:you@b") Diary,  topic LANGUAGES>Discover language certificates>"Is the University test center for language certificates?"
- **Students from other Universities or Schools:** via the contact form available at this [link](https://forms.unibocconi.it/index.php?rif_quest=857)

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)

For all **students enrolled at Bocconi (including Master and PhD students)**, Bocconi University has entered the list of test venues for Cambridge Assessment English / British Council **IELTS  Academic** certification and Cambridge University / Anderson House **C1 Advanced and C2 Proficiency** certifications of general English.

Please note that the **test dates are defined in agreement with the British Council and Anderson House**.

**How to register for the exam**

It is possible to register directly on the [**yoU@B Student’s Diary**](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/)(Language Center widget) through the links to the British Council Milan and Anderson House registration platforms.

metadata.title: General rules for registration in student’s academic career | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: General rules for registration in student’s academic career | Bocconi University

text:
International certifications must be achieved and submitted for conversion by the study plan completion date relating to the selected Graduation session or, in any case, by the deadlines set to submit applications for the selected activities.

We remind that it is not possible to request the recognition of an international certificate if the curricular exam of the same language has been recorded in the academic career, or even if the written exam has been passed with a sufficient grade, until the end of the exam session, which ends after the oral exam and the grade recording.

When the result score of the International Certifying Agency is expressed to decimal places, the result is rounded up when the decimal place is greater than or equal to 5.

For curricular languages, an international certification whose level/typology (general, business, legal) is equal to the minimum level/typology required can be registered in student's academic career.

Furthermore, an international certification whose level/typology (general, business, legal) is higher than the minimum required can be registered in place of the minimum level/typology required (i.e. when the minimum level/typology required is B1 general, then a B1 business or B2 business or higher level/typology is allowed; when the minimum level/typology required is B1 business, then a B2 general or B2 business or higher level/typology is allowed).

Please note that the level/typology recorded in the academic curriculum is the level/typology stated by the Certifying Agency on the international certification.

Overlap between adjacent levels is allowed solely when the Certifying Agency explicitly provides for overlapping levels (for example: C1 scores recognizable as B2 scores and viceversa).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/general-rules-registration-students-academic-career#addtoany "Show all")

Chinese (as additional exam) | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Code** | **Certification Level** | **Level to be recorded in the study plan** | **Type of Students** | | 50168 | For HSK (written examination):     
from A1 general to C2
For HSKK (oral texamination):
from Elementary to Advanced | The same of the wtitten examination (HSK) | Students who have already passed the exams of the first 3 years of study | Students who wish to register Chinese certification in their study plan as additional exams (4 credits in addition to 120/180/300 credits) **must pass both the written examination HSK and the associated oral examination HSKK**. By the score for the written and the oral, you get the final mark out of thirty. The final score is calculated as follows: 75% written part + 25% oral part. When the result score is expressed to decimal places, the result is rounded up when the decimal place is greater or equal to 0,5. **The written examination and oral examination correspond to two independent certifications provided separately.** We remind you that the acceptance of the language certification is subject to the  fulfillment of the applied rules and to the possibility to verify online the authenticity of the certification on the date of the language certification recognition (complete and correct one) submission. **CONVERSION TABLES**:

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)


**Instructions to submit the request of recognition of a language certification for students enrolled in former cohorts** **(before the 2014-15 cohort in BSc Programs and CLMG and before 2018-19 cohort in MSc Programs):**

- Fill out entirely the **form** available at this [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/ING-form-registrazione-certificazioni-v2.pdf).
- Print the form filled out, sign it, specify the date and make a scan of it.
- Send a scan of it with date and original signature together with a scan of the certification, using the B in Touch procedure available in the yoU@B Diary, topic: "LANGUAGES > Discover language certifications".
- **Wait for the reply** by the Language Center which will indicate the outcome of the application submitted.

If the form has been completed correctly and the conversion request is accepted, the grade out of 30 resulting from the conversion will be recorded indicatively within two weeks than the request date.

If the form is completed incorrectly, the student will receive indication about it and he/she will have to submit a new request attaching again the form and scanned copy of the certification.

It is specified that the **date of submission of the complete and correct request** must be indicated on the form. In case of inconsistency, the certification will be recorded with the **date corresponding to the complete and correct request submission**.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/language-certification-recognition-0#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Language Certification Recognition | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Language Certification Recognition | Bocconi University

text:
1\. Verify that the certification to be recognized respects the **defined parameters**, specifically that:
- the certification is listed in the ones **recognized by Bocconi**(see list of certifications and conversion tables below)
- the certification can be verified online
- the certification is of a **level equal to or higher** than required level. Please check information on curricular languages ​​and levels in the “Foreign Languages” chapter of the [Guide to the University](https://www.unibocconi.eu/studentguide) of your Study Program and the rules available in the [**Rules**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/certificates/general-rules-registration-students-academic-career "General rules for registration in student’s academic career") section)
Moreover, the language certification recognition request must be submitted **within 3 years** after the date of the certification test ( **2 years** for TOEFL; **IELTS can be recognized from two years from the original test date even when it is an "IELTS One Skill retake" certification**; Goethe Zertifikat and OSD **can be recognized only if the four skills have been taken on one single date or across one year**. In any case, the certification recognition request **must be submitted within 3 years after the 1st module test sitting date**, in the event the certification has been completed in different dates).
2. **Check the grade** by consulting the tables available on below link:

- [Certifications recognized for the Bachelor of Science Programs](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/language+center/certificates/certificazioni+riconosciute+per+corsi+di+laurea+triennale+eng)
- [Certifications recognized for the Integrated Master of Arts in Law](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/language+center/certificates/certificazioni+riconosciute+per+corsi+di+laurea+magistrale+in+giurisprudenza+eng)
- [Certifications recognized for the Master of Science Programs](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/language+center/certificates/certificazioni+riconosciute+per+corsi+di+laurea+magistrale+eng)
3\. **Submit the language certification recognition request**:

exams can be recorded on the transcript starting in the enrollment in the year of studies the language subject of recognition is positioned in by filling out the application via the “ **Language certification recognition” procedure** available on the yoU@B “Language Center” widget.

In the language certification recognition procedure, it is possible to download (in pdf format, no larger than 2MB):
- a scan of the certificate, or
- the statement of results, or
- a screenshot of the online results, also including student's name and surname, certification test date and certification reference code.

4. **Wait for the reply** by the Language Center which will indicate the outcome of the application submitted through the above-mentioned procedure.

metadata.title: Language Certification Recognition | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Language Certification Recognition | Bocconi University

text:
It is specified that **the student has the responsibility** to carefully check the recognized certifications and rules for conversion, before submitting certification request of recognition.

Submitting a certification request of conversion DOES NOT IMPLY that the application will be accepted by the Language Center.

If the conversion request via the “Language certification recognition” procedure has been completed correctly and has been accepted, the grade out of 30 (or PASS for the Professional English Seminars) resulting from the conversion will be recorded indicatively within 10 working days than the request date (the date of recording will correspond to the date of submission of the complete and correct request).

With reference to any institutional or application deadlines (for which students must request information to the relative Services), **we suggest to submit the request of recognition of the language certification with the adequate time of 10 working days before the deadline for registering exams to be considered for the relating application.**

[**Instructions to submit the request of recognition of a language certification for students enrolled in former cohorts** (before the 2014-15 cohort in BSc Programs and CLMG and before 2018-19 cohort in MSc Programs)](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/language+center/certificates/certification+recognition+for+former+cohorts)

from A1 general to C2
For HSKK (oral texamination):
from Elementary to Advanced | The same of the wtitten examination (HSK) | 3rd year students, who have already passed the exams of their Degree Program first three semesters | Students who wish to register Chinese certification in their study plan as additional exams (4 credits in addition to 120/180/300 credits) **must pass both the written examination HSK and the associated oral examination HSKK**. By the score for the written and the oral, you get the final mark out of thirty. The final score is calculated as follows: 75% written part + 25% oral part. When the result score is expressed to decimal places, the result is rounded up when the decimal place is greater or equal to 0,5. **The written examination and oral examination correspond to two independent certifications provided separately.** We remind you that the acceptance of the language certification is subject to the  fulfillment of the applied rules and to the possibility to verify online the authenticity of the certification on the date of the language certification recognition (complete and correct one) submission. **CONVERSION TABLES**: - The final score is calculated as follows: 3/4 mark out of thirties of the written part + 1/4 mark out of thirties of the oral part (the result is rounded up for decimal places ≥ 0,5). text:
## **Who can participate in the Language Exchange?**

The initiative is open to students enrolled in Bachelor, Master of Science, Integrated Master of Arts in Law programs and Specialized Master Programs, Exchange incoming students who already have **at least an elementary level (A2)** in the language which they would like to improve. They must be at least B2 level or mother tongue for the other exchange language.

metadata.title: Language Exchange | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Who can participate in the Language Exchange?**

text:
## **When and how is possible to take part in the Language Exchange?**

The Language Exchange is offered at the beginning of every semester. It will be possible to fill in the registration form on the yoU@B Student Diary, in the Language Center widget.

For 2nd semester 2025-26, registrations will be open **from January 26th to February 16th 2026**.

metadata.title: Language Exchange | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **When and how is possible to take part in the Language Exchange?**

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)


**From 26 August 2024**, the Language Lab only hosts specific initiatives, including tutoring sessions reserved for students enrolled in the curricular language preparatory courses (except for English) and international certification exams.

Tools and resources for language self-study are available for Bocconi students in the yoU@B Diary ("Language Center" menu) and on the BBoard pages, including the ones for students behind normal academic progress.

text:
Language Center [Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center) The Bocconi Language Center presents a **film festival** showcasing movies in their **original-language versions**. This event offers a fantastic opportunity to enhance your language skills, explore diverse cultures, and enjoy a meaningful and engaging experience. For each film, attendees will be able to download a brief synopsis and glossary once the registration opens (available in the "About the Movies" section below). Event structure: - Teacher's introduction (15 min.) - Film - Q&A Entrance is **reserved to the Bocconi community** and admission is free **.** To join a screening, registration is required via the **online form**, accessible either through the Postmaster email or the link provided below each film title. | | | --- | | **Program - Spring Semester 2025-2026 a. y.** | | **25 February 2026 (Room Franceschi, at 6:30pm)** | | **"The Phoenician Scheme" directed by Wes Anderson** (2025)
In English with English subtitles. | | **11 March 2026 (Room Franceschi, at 6:30pm)** | | **"The Last Showgirl" directed by Gia Coppola** (2025)
In English with English subtitles. | | **20 April 2026 (Room Franceschi, at 6:30pm)** | | **"Familia" directed by Francesco Costabile** (2024)
In Italian with Italian subtitles. | | **12 May 2026 (Room Franceschi, at 6:30pm)** | | **"El maestro que prometió el mar"** **directed by Patricia Font** (2023)
In Spanish with Spanish subtitles.
**Registration open from 04/28 to 05/12. Click** [**here**](https://events.unibocconi.eu/index.php?key=ev20260512ml) **to register.**

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)


The Bocconi Language Center organizes a **film festival** devoted to movies **in original- language versions**. The festival is a good chance to broaden your language horizons, get to know other cultures, and enjoy yourself productively.

Viewers can read brief synopsis cards. **You can download synopses** from the Language Center website.

**Program**

Spring semester 2024-2025 a.y. Movies & Languages Film Festival program will be available in January 2025.

Event structure:

- Teacher's introduction (15 min.)
- Film
- Q&A

Entrance is **reserved to the Bocconi community** upon registration, according to the methods indicated for the single screening.

text:
## **Tutoring for all curricular languages**

The tutoring consists in **group sessions** of exercises on specific basic language topics.

It is addressed to students enrolled in curricular language preparatory courses (except for English) with no previous knowledge of the language or a low level.

Currently, some of the tutoring sessions are held in presence and some are online via Microsoft Teams.

Tutoring sessions are optional and students are admitted only upon reservation (via the “Appointments” procedure available on the yoU@B Diary).

Further information and the tutoring calendar will be available in the "Content" section of the preparatory course BBoard page.

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)


The Language Center offers a short intensive Italian language course in distance to help foreign students interact during their first days in Italy and facilitate their integration at Bocconi.

This course is addressed to 1st year students (BSc Programs and MSc Programs), University Master students and incoming Double Degree students.

It is entirely online asynchronous and is made up of five units, organized with explanatory videos and a series of related linguistic activities. It offers basic language skills and an introduction to Italian culture.

As the course is available online in a self-learning mode, **no attendance certificate is provided**.

text:
Language Center

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center)


The **Language Center** offers free courses for Bocconi students, including:

- Language and culture courses
- Introductory language courses
- Courses to prepare for international certifications, available for both curricular and extracurricular languages
- Workshops aimed at enhancing students’ academic and professional skills in English (Soft Skills Workshops)
- A short online course covering the basics of the Italian language (Italian Survival Course)

metadata.title: Supplementary Activities | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Supplementary Activities | Bocconi University

text:
## **Enrollment**

**Courses** are **open to** a limited number of Bocconi students of **all Programs**, University specialized Masters, SDA Masters and PhDs, **except for the "Italian Survival Course"** online **,** which is open to first-year students (BSc, MSc, Masters) and Double Degree Incoming students with unlimited places available.

Students can **enroll exclusively through the you@B Diary**, in the **“sign-up for various activities” box**. Before registering, students are strongly advised to **carefully read the complete course calendar** to avoid possible overlaps with other classes or activities.

Please note that **cancellations** can only be made through the yoU@B Student Diary **within**(and not after) **the deadline indicated for the registration**. No other type of cancellation request will be accepted.

Students who have not properly completed the registration process cannot be admitted to the courses.

metadata.title: Supplementary Activities | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Enrollment**

text:
## **Attending and obtaining the open badge**

Courses **do not grant academic credits** or additional points for graduation. **There is no final exam**.

Students who attend **at least 75%** of the academic hours of the course **\*** receive an **Open Badge of Attendance** approximately 30 days after the course ends.

**Attendance** is recorded at the beginning of each class through a **numeric code** provided by the instructor. **The code is valid for 10 minutes only**. **Any attendance recorded after this time will not be considered valid** and will count as an absence. Students are not allowed to leave the classroom before the end of the lesson. Any improper conduct will be sanctioned in accordance with the Università Bocconi Honor Code.

**\*Exception**: theItalian Survival Course (delivered online in asynchronous mode) does not include attendance tracking and therefore does not issue an Open Badge of Attendance.

metadata.title: Supplementary Activities | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Attending and obtaining the open badge**

text:
## **Exclusion list**

If you are registered for a course but you **do not attend at least 25%** of the scheduled hours, you will not be able to register to any other Language Center extracurricular activity during the same semester and the following one.

**Being placed on the Exclusion List twice** leads to **permanent exclusion** from Language Center supplementary activities.

\* **Exception**: there is no exclusion list for the "Italian Survival Course".

[**Calendar and Details**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities-0)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/language-center/supplementary-activities/supplementary-activities#addtoany "Show all")
