Availability of:

- 2 Reserved seats, exclusively for persons with disabilities
- 4 Priority seats, for non-exclusive use: available to all, upon reservation, but to be vacated if requested by persons with disabilities
- 1 dedicated workstation with a lowered desk dedicated to wheelchair users for online catalogue browsing
- Online delivery of accessible digital material for entitled users (users with disabilities or specific learning disorders) thanks to the fact that Bocconi University is recognized as “Entità autorizzata” by the Ministry of Culture under Law 633/1941 and the DPCM July 6, 2020. Here is a [list](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Catalogo%20inclusion_marzo%202026.pdf?VersionId=XyBjZp1x6oQKJh0RIO2Vwe4nr6.hUqHN "inclusion catalogue") of texts in accessible format from other “Entità autorizzate” and available by request
- [Sensus Access](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/campus+and+services/services+for/sensus+access): application for converting documents and web pages into accessible formats
- Campus Guide, created in partnership with Istituto dei Ciechi di Milano specifically for users with visual impairments, available at the Library Main Entrance
- Bocconi campus tactile model, available at the entrance to Via Gobbi 5 building

For other services offered by the University, please visit the following page [Services for Students with Disabilities, SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/inclusion-disabilities-and-learning-disorders/services-students-disabilities-slds-adhd-and-other-specific-needs "Services for Students with Disabilities, SLDs, ADHD and Other Specific Needs").

Here are also our Libguides:

- [Accessibilty and health libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/accessibility): a selection of relevant resources and tools, available through the Library or freely accessible online, related to accessibility, learning disabilities and mental health
- [Diversity and inclusion libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/diversity): related to gender, LGBTQIA+, geographic and ethnic background

If you need to get browsing from your device more accessible and easier, you can choose one of the free add-ons offered for [Google Chrome](https://chrome.google.com/webstore/category/collection/3p_accessibility_extensions) and [Mozilla Firefox](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/search/?q=accessibility&type=extension). You can choose them also using the Library's computers in the reading rooms.

For further information and assistance: [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it)

Library & Archives

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)


The majority of electronic resources (databases, e-journals, e-books) made available through the Bocconi Library are governed by license agreements between the Library and publishers or vendors. These define specific terms and conditions of use, i.e. authorized users, permissions and restrictions.

**Access to the licensed online resources is for educational and research use only.**

Here you can find a list of usually permitted and not permitted uses:

- **Displaying, searching, downloading, printing and storing the licensed material** for scholarly research, educational and personal use only

- **Creating a temporary link** to a journal article or a book/book chapter for use in electronic course packs or learning management systems provided proper reference to the author and source is given, and with the authorization of the Library (only authorized users can access it)

- **Sharing your own IDs and password** with third parties, nor to enable anyone other than authorized user to use the licensed material
- **Distributing or transmitting** (by whatever means, e.g. fax, e-mail, social networks, etc.) the licensed material to anyone other than authorized users
- **Systematically downloading, printing and storing** an excessive portion of the licensed materials (e.g. an entire journal issue or e- book), also by use of specific software
- **Exporting and using the content in artificial intelligence tools** is not permitted unless such use is explicitly authorized by the resource’s terms of use or falls within the limits of copyright exceptions, in particular the personal-use reproduction of non-substantial parts of a work (typically up to 15% of the work, by analogy with the provisions that apply to analogue reproductions)
- **Removing, obscuring or modifying any copyright** author attribution or other notices included in the licensed material
- Making **any use** **for commercial purposes**
- Making any use that exceeds or violates the terms and conditions of use **without the prior written permission of Licensor**

You can read the full license details of a specific resource searching for a specific database in [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/) and clicking on “Show terms of use”:


Consequences of misuse

Publishers monitor the usage of electronic resources to verify compliance with licensing agreements.

**Misuse or violation of license restrictions may result in the termination of the license agreement and in the withdrawal of any access rights to the electronic resource**

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/about-us/responsible-use-online-resources#addtoany "Show all")

text:
You can explore dissertations in the [Collections](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/collectionDiscovery?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1) tab, where you can find:
- [**Bocconi University Dissertations (Online)**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/collectionDiscovery?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&collectionId=8199465570007991&lang=en), all dissertations discussed at Bocconi University since 1977, divided between [**Dissertations 1988-2000**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/collectionDiscovery?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&collectionId=8199465560007991&lang=en) and [**Dissertations 2000-**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/collectionDiscovery?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&collectionId=8199465530007991&lang=en). Within each group you will find two boxes, one for the theses for which the author has authorized consultation ( **Authorized Dissertations**), another for those for which consultation has not been authorized ( **Unauthorized Dissertations**). In the latter case you will be able to see details about the dissertations (title, author, advisor, year), but not read the content.
- [**Bocconi University Historical Dissertations**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/collectionDiscovery?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&collectionId=8199465730007991&lang=en), that contains the texts of all the theses discussed at the University from 1906 to 1977. The dissertations are displayed according to a timeline that accompanies significant moments in the history of the University and its community. Historical dissertations are undergoing an important process of restoration and digitization for preservation purposes. This project is currently in progress and has been made possible thanks to the support of generous donors.
- [**Bocconi University PhD dissertations 1995-2002**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/collectionDiscovery?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&collectionId=81108487060007991&lang=en "Bocconi University PhD dissertations 1995-2002"), that collects PhD dissertations discussed at Bocconi University between 1995 and 2002.

metadata.title: Consulting a Thesis | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Consulting a Thesis | Bocconi University

text:
#### **How do I search for a dissertation?**

Use the search bar in each section or scroll through the titles and choose the ones that interest you most. Either way, we suggest you to start with the sign in. You can also search for dissertations in [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en) by author (student name or supervisor name), title or keywords within the title, but you will only be able to read dissertations for which the author has given permission for consultation. We suggest you to consult the Libguide [Consulting Dissertations](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/consulting-dissertations) to best set up your research strategies.

#### **Can I read dissertations in the Library?**

Sure! Theses can be consulted in the Library from the dedicated workstation in Data Room 016, located on the Library ground floor.

To consult print dissertations, you may request them through [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en "SearchLib"); once your request is submitted, we will contact you to schedule an appointment for assisted consultation.

#### **What if I am off campus?**

If you are a student or Faculty member, you can consult the theses even when you are off campus at any time. Simply sign in and ... enjoy your reading!

Please note: all theses are subject to current legislation on [copyright and intellectual property rights](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/copyright "copyright and intellectual property rights").

text:
### [Rodolfo Banfi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/catalogobanfi.pdf "Rodolfo Banfi Collection")

The collection contains about 300 works and it was donated to the Library between 2005 and 2009. Rodolfo Banfi (1919 – 1992) worked at Banca Commerciale Italiana led by Raffaele Mattioli from 1946 to 1978, directing the COMIT’s Research Department from 1972 to 1978. In 1979, he became Chairman of Medio Credito Centrale. A board member of ISTAO, the R. Mattioli Foundation, Basinvest, ICE and the Corrente Foundation, he became Chairman of ATM Milano in December 1990.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Rodolfo Banfi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/catalogobanfi.pdf "Rodolfo Banfi Collection")

text:
### [Ferruccio Bolchini Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoBolchini.pdf "Ferruccio Bolchini Collection")

The collection contains 440 works edited between the late 19th century and 1940, all of which focus on law.

Ferruccio Bolchini (1875 - 1931), Professor of Civil Law at the University of Pavia from 1903 to 1925, he was Rector of Bocconi University from 1926 to 1930.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Ferruccio Bolchini Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoBolchini.pdf "Ferruccio Bolchini Collection")

text:
### [Ugo Caprara Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoCaprara.pdf "Ugo Caprara Collection")

The collection contains 94 works edited between the late 19th century and 1940, which focus on business economics, accounting and business.

Caprara (1894 - 1990) graduated from Bocconi in 1921 began his university career a year later as assistant to the Chair of Accounting and Business Management held by Professor Zappa. In later years, he was Professor of Business Management, retiring from academics in 1969. During his university career, he was asked to join the University Board, contributing to Bocconi’s continuous development.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Ugo Caprara Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoCaprara.pdf "Ugo Caprara Collection")

text:
### [Enrico Cernuschi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoCernuschi.pdf "Enrico Cernuschi Collection")

The collection contains around 720 works edited between the late 16th century and the late 19th century. Enrico Cernuschi (1821 – 1896) was a banker and Italian patriot, and a naturalized French citizen. As a banker, he is best known for his publications on finance issues, specifically related to the monetary system known as bimetallism.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Enrico Cernuschi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoCernuschi.pdf "Enrico Cernuschi Collection")

text:
### [Giordano Dell’Amore Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoDellAmore.pdf "Giordano Dell’Amore Collection")

The collection contains more than 4000 works, edited between the early 19th century and 1980. Dell’Amore (1902 - 1981) graduated from Bocconi in 1923, he was Professor of Banking at Cattolica University in Milan. Professor of Management at Bocconi and Rector from 1967 to 1973, he was also Chairman of CARIPLO and the Lombardy Provincial Government.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Giordano Dell’Amore Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoDellAmore.pdf "Giordano Dell’Amore Collection")

text:
### [Eugenio Greco Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoGreco.pdf "Eugenio Greco Collection")

The collection contains around 3200 works edited between the late 18th century and 1950. In 1902, the year Bocconi University was founded, Professor Maglione asked Greco (1878 - 1959), to be his assistant to the Chair in General and Applied Accounting. He taught continuously until 1938. He held the Chairs in Business Management (1918 - 1920), Finance (1920 - 1925) and Accounting (1925 - 1938).

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Eugenio Greco Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoGreco.pdf "Eugenio Greco Collection")

text:
### [Giuseppe Luraghi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoLuraghi.pdf "Giuseppe Luraghi Collection")

The collection, donated to the University by the family in 2000, contains about 260 monographs and 53 periodicals. Giuseppe Luraghi (1905–1991) graduated from Bocconi with a degree in Economics in 1926. As a corporate manager, he had executive roles at Pirelli, Finmeccanica and IRI Group. In 1960 he was appointed Chairman of Alfa Romeo.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Giuseppe Luraghi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoLuraghi.pdf "Giuseppe Luraghi Collection")

text:
### [Luigi Majno Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoMajno.pdf "Luigi Majno Collection")

The collection contains 130 works focusing on the evolution of socialist theories.

Jurist Luigi Majno (1852 - 1915), taught Law and Criminal Procedure at the University of Pavia from 1889 to 1894. He was Chairman of Fondazione Umanitaria until 1898 and a Member of Parliament representing Milan during the 21st legislature. He was also Rector of Bocconi University from 1914 to 1915.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Luigi Majno Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoMajno.pdf "Luigi Majno Collection")

text:
### [Cesare Pedrazzi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoPedrazzi.pdf "Cesare Pedrazzi Collection")

The collection contains about 500 monographs and periodicals focusing on criminal law. Cesare Pedrazzi (1927 – 2005) began his university career as an assistant in Urbino, later on he held the Chair in Criminal Law at Universities in Trieste, Pavia, and then Milan (both the University of Milan and Bocconi). Editor-in-chief of Rivista di Diritto e procedura penale, at the end of his career he was Professor Emeritus of Corporate Criminal Law at Bocconi.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Cesare Pedrazzi Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoPedrazzi.pdf "Cesare Pedrazzi Collection")

text:
### [Ugo Pisa Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoPisa.pdf "Ugo Pisa Collection")

The collection contains about 250 documents edited between the late 18th century and 1940.

Ugo Pisa (1845-1910) graduated from the University of Pavia with a degree in Law. He was first board member and then Chairman of the Milan Chamber of Commerce. He was also Senator of Italy in the Liberal party during the 20th legislature (1898-1910).

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Ugo Pisa Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoPisa.pdf "Ugo Pisa Collection")

text:
### [Angelo Sraffa Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoSraffa.pdf "Angelo Sraffa Collection")

The collection contains about 835 works edited between the mid 16th century and 1937.

Sraffa (1865 - 1937) graduated from Pisa in 1888, then, starting in 1893, taught Commercial Law in Pisa, Macerata, Messina and Parma until 1903, when he was asked to hold the Chair in Business and Commercial Law at the then newly founded Bocconi University. He was Rector of Bocconi from 1918 to 1926. In 1924, after teaching at the University of Turin, he was part of a group of professors who were given the task of creating the Faculty of Law for the University of Milan.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Angelo Sraffa Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoSraffa.pdf "Angelo Sraffa Collection")

text:
### [Ariberto Mignoli Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoMignoli.pdf "Ariberto Mignoli Collection")

The collection includes about 2300 monographs and periodicals focusing on commercial law.

Ariberto Mignoli (1920-2003) graduated from Cattolica University in Milan in 1942 with a degree in Law. Starting in 1956, he was Professor of Commercial Law at Bocconi and he was Director of the Angelo Sraffa Institute of Comparative Law. He founded Rivista delle società with Tullio Ascarelli.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Ariberto Mignoli Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoMignoli.pdf "Ariberto Mignoli Collection")

text:
### [Gino Zappa Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoZappa.pdf "Gino Zappa Collection")

The collection contains about 2200 works, edited between the mid 19th century and 1950.

Zappa (1879 - 1960) graduated in 1904 from University Ca' Foscari in Venice, where he studied under Fabio Besta. He was known as the founder of Business Economics in Italy. A tenured Professor at Bocconi from 1929 to 1934 in Business Mathematics and General and Applied Accounting, he was Director of the Private Economics Laboratory, then the Institute of Business Research and Accounting.

metadata.title: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Catalogues of Donations | Bocconi University > [Gino Zappa Collection](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/lea-CatalogoZappa.pdf "Gino Zappa Collection")

This catalogue contains bibliographic records of the lecture notes held at the University from 1902 to 1945.

text:
Library & Archives

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

Before the establishment of the Borsa Italiana joint-stock company, headquartered in Milan in 1998, Italy was home to ten different stock exchanges located in Bologna, Florence, Genoa, Milan, Naples, Palermo, Rome, Turin, Trieste, and Venice. Each of these was established in different years during the 19th century, often reviving local civic traditions of institutions devoted to the trading of securities and goods. **Regulated first by the commercial codes of the 1800s and later by a comprehensive law passed in 1913**, the history of stock exchanges in Italy reflects the evolving financial geography of the country. Among them, the Milan Stock Exchange - operating since 1808 - gradually emerged as the most important, followed by Rome. This trend became even more pronounced in the 1970s, when many banks began to adopt centralized order management systems, channeling most operations through Milan and relegating the other nine exchanges to handling retail clients and small local banks.

**The "Open Outcry" System**

Frenetic and theatrical, **stock trading in Italy relied on the "open outcry" method: the law required that buy and sell offers be shouted aloud by authorized brokers to ensure transparency and public access to trading information**. The trading floor, known as the “ _recinto delle grida_” (literally “shouting rings”) or “ _corbeille_” in the French tradition, served as the stage for stockbrokers and their assistants, intent on executing buy and sell orders. The continuous noise and confusion made verbal communication with other rooms and telephone clients difficult and unreliable. Therefore, **shouting was supplemented by a codified system of hand signals**, used to indicate different kinds of information: numerical signals for quantities and prices, alphabetic signs for company names, and symbolic gestures to represent business activities, product qualities, or general market conditions.

**The Milan Stock Exchange**

**This world came to an end in the 1990s with a series of reforms that replaced the open outcry system with an electronic trading platform**, a transition completed between 1992 and 1994. These reforms also ended the brokers’ monopoly over stock exchange contracts, led to the creation of investment firms known as "SIMs" ( _Società di Intermediazione Mobiliare_), and finally transferred management of the exchange from public bodies, such as the _Consigli di Borsa_, to a private entity: the joint-stock company Borsa Italiana.

**Historical Documents at Bocconi Library**

**Bocconi Library preserves a significant collection of resources on the activities of historical stock exchanges**, beginning with **the most complete collection in Italy of price lists from all exchanges**, comprising over a thousand volumes. The library also preserves various publications, including _**L’osservatorio della borsa**_, _**La rivista della Borsa**_ and **annual reports curated by the** _**Comitato Direttivo degli Agenti di Cambio della Borsa di Milano**_. Finally, the Library stores the rare _**Il termometro mercantile e d’industria**_, which published the quotations of four public debt securities traded on the Milan Stock Exchange in the 1830s.

_The Stock Exchange in a humorous cartoon by Giovanni Manca (Source: Biblioteca Comunale Centrale di Milano)_

_Price List of the Borsa Valori di Milano (Source: Bocconi Library)_

_Price List of the Borsa Valori di Firenze (Source: Bocconi Library)_

text:
Library & Archives

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

**After the armistice of September 8, 1943, many Italian military students—as well as civilians—sought refuge in Switzerland to avoid surrendering to the Nazi army, which was occupying Italy.** They were not considered prisoners of war or formal internees but rather refugees fleeing a collapsing nation. Initially referred to as military refugees by the Swiss Federal Council in an attempt to facilitate their legal expulsion, they were eventually accepted into Switzerland, due to the impossibility of controlling border crossings and the growing solidarity of the Swiss population. In October and November 1943, a questionnaire distributed to the refugees revealed the presence of around **1,000 students and 120 university graduates**. Like earlier cases involving refugees from Poland (1940) and France (1942), **the decision was made to offer these students the opportunity to continue their university studies**.

**The Organization of the University Camps**

To support this major educational and humanitarian effort, a Comité d’Aide aux Universitaires Italiens en Suisse was established in Lausanne, with the support of the _Fonds Européen de Secours aux Étudiants_ (FESE). The committee was led by Plinio Bolla, Vice President of the Swiss Federal Tribunal in Lausanne, and **Gustavo Colonnetti**, **a former professor at the Polytechnic University of Turin who had also taken refuge in Switzerland**.

**Given the difficulty of enrolling the students directly in existing universities, the decision was made to create university camps with a paramilitary structure near universities in French-speaking Switzerland**, as French was the foreign language most widely known among Italian students. A special commission composed of Swiss and Italian professors selected students through oral exams based on their academic curricula. Starting in January 1944, university camps were opened in Geneva (Economics and Law), Lausanne (Engineering and Architecture), Fribourg (Humanities) and Neuchâtel (various disciplines).

Each camp operated under the supervision of Lieutenant Colonel Max Zeller, who appointed an academic rector, a university faculty coordinator, and a military commander ( _chef des études_) \- a hierarchical structure typical of military organizations. Roughly half of the 1,000 applicants were admitted, and additional camps were later opened in the Bernese Oberland (notably in Huttwil and Mürren) to accommodate many of those initially excluded.

The **Italian University Camp in Lausanne** became the most prominent, thanks to the leadership of **Colonnetti**, **who served as its rector**. He recruited outstanding faculty, organized lectures and cultural events, and founded the Centre for Studies on Italian Reconstruction, which he co-chaired with Luigi Einaudi.

Despite the challenges posed by the students’ diverse academic backgrounds, there was a clear effort to uphold academic standards and ensure that **exams would be recognized once students returned to Italy**. To this end, a Swiss-Italian examining board was created: an Italian professor sat on each board to verify program equivalencies and translate Swiss grades into the Italian system. This allowed for the official **r** ecognition of coursework after the university camps closed between May and June 1945.

**Documents in Bocconi Library and Historical Archives**

metadata.title: The University in Exile: The University Internment Camps in Switzerland, 1943-1945 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: The University in Exile: The University Internment Camps in Switzerland, 1943-1945 | Bocconi University

text:
At Bocconi University Library, there are **34 pamphlets published by FESE, most of which relate to courses held at the Geneva university camp, attended by 200 students and supported by prominent jurists**, including Francesco Carnelutti (who had taught Industrial Law at Bocconi from 1909 to 1912), Donato Donati, Alessandro Levi, Piero Sacerdoti and Vittorio Tedeschi, **and renowned economists, especially Luigi Einaudi** **(himself a civil refugee in Switzerland from September 26, 1943, and in Geneva from October 1944)**. The collection also includes Gustavo Del Vecchio, who had been a professor and rector at Bocconi before being removed due to the racial laws, and Amintore Fanfani. Ariberto Mignoli, who would later become a long-time professor at Bocconi and founder of the _Rivista delle Società_, wrote two pamphlets during his time as an assistant at the Geneva university camp. One was co-authored with Pietro Chiovenda and Vittorio Gattinara based on the course by Alessandro Levi, supplemented with other texts, while the other was based on the course by Georges Sauser-Hall, also supported by additional texts. Other assistants who wrote pamphlets were Vittorio Paretti on the lessons of Del Vecchio and Giuseppe Salto (a Bocconi graduate with Libero Lenti in 1940/41) based on the lessons of Édouard Folliet.

In Bocconi Historical Archives (ASBOC), there is also **documentation on the exams taken by Bocconi students at the camps of Huttwil and Mürren** ( _Fondo Buste AB_ _,_ [Fascicolo Esami Svizzeri convalidati sostenuti da nostri studenti residenti provvisoriamente in Svizzera durante gli eventi bellici](https://asboc.unibocconi.it/oggetti/10023-esami-svizzeri-convalidati-sostenuti-da-nostri-studenti-residenti-provvisoriamente-in-svizzera-durante-gli-eventi-bellici/)). In addition to the correspondence between the Italian university camp at Huttwil, Bocconi University, and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs from 1944 to 1945, the file contains a list of names of students temporarily residing in Switzerland during that period.

**After the war**, with the restoration of democracy and the return of the students, the Academic Council was tasked with determining the validity of exams taken in Switzerland. After extensive debate, **the Faculty Council decided to assign individual professors the task of verifying the actual preparation of students who had attended the Swiss university camps through oral exams**.

_Gustavo Colonnetti (Source: Wikimedia)_

_Handout of Luigi Einaudi's lectures (Source: Bocconi Library)_

_Detail from the record of recognition of examinations taken (Source: ASBOC)_

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/historical-archives-and-special-collections/university-exile-university-internment-camps-switzerland-1943-1945#addtoany "Show all")

text:
News and Events Hub

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


17 Jul 2025, by Library & Archives

A virtual exhibition exploring a central figure in Bocconi’s history

Bocconi Library and Archives are pleased to present a new virtual exhibition dedicated to **Angelo Sraffa**, distinguished jurist, professor, and key figure in the life of the University.

Through archival documents and bibliographic sources, the exhibition retraces the deep connection that bound Sraffa to “his” university for nearly his entire professional life: from his appointment in 1902 as Professor of Commercial Law, to his tenure as Rector, and later as a member of the University Board, from which he resigned in 1934.

The exhibition also offers a unique glimpse into a lesser-known side of his personality: not only a prominent scholar and respected professional, but also a refined bibliophile.

The core sections of the exhibition focus on a selection from his extensive personal library, which was partly donated to the University by his family after his death. Special attention is given to several particularly valuable groups: ancient legal texts, a _Miscellanea_ personally compiled by Sraffa, and a number of significant volumes from the humanistic segment of his collection.

The result is a portrait of a man of profound humanistic culture and wide-ranging intellectual interests, whose legacy continues to resonate with the history and identity of the University.

[**Visit the virtual exhibition now**](https://virtualexhibitions.unibocconi.it/sraffa/en/1/home)

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/news/angelo-sraffa#addtoany "Show all")

13 Jan 2026, by Library & Archives

We have a workshop to boost your research career

Starting this year, you can include the **Open Science for Researchers workshop** in your [study plan](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/phd/phd-business-administration-and-management/courses-and-requirements).

**When:** January 29, 6:00–7:30pm

**Where:** Online on Blackboard

**Assessment:** A short final quiz will certify attendance

**What you will learn**

- The fundamentals of **Open Science** and **Open Access**, essential tools for anyone pursuing an academic career.
- How Open Science practices promote **transparency** and **accessibility** in research.
- How to use **Creative Commons licences** to share your work while retaining intellectual property rights.
- How to identify and avoid **predatory publishers** that exploit researchers and their work.
- How to **publish in Open Access at no extra cost** with Elsevier, Springer, and other participating academic publishers.

**Registration**

Register through **yoU@B → Appointments**.

**Programme**

Check the [**workshop syllabus**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Open%20Science%20for%20Researchers%20Workshop%20aa%202025_2026.pdf?VersionId=YrLxWQCotwSvNgFomFtNS40HOcW36AES).

30 Oct 2025, by Library & Archives

Get Certified with Bloomberg, LSEG, and FactSet

**Relevance for finance students**

Leading financial data providers such as Bloomberg LSEG and FactSet offer certification programmes that help students build practical skills in market data, as well as prove their knowledge of financial databases to prospective employers.

**Recognition in the job market**

Your proficiency will be proven by a printable certificate and a badge that you can add to your LinkedIn profile.

**Flexible**

The interactive learning modules are available online with no live sessions or strict deadlines, allowing you to stop and resume the course, or review concepts, at your own pace.

**No charge**

All certification programmes are free to take for Bocconi students.

**How do I enroll?** You can register with your Bocconi e-mail and/or access the certification module within the database. For details, please check the Training & Certification tab of the relevant libguide:

[Bloomberg libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/bloomberg)

[LSEG Workspace libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/refinitiv)

[FactSet libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/factset)

Please note: certifications are available in **English only**.

Don’t miss this opportunity to demonstrate your knowledge and skills with the most popular financial analysis products! For further information, please write to [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto: infobiblio@unibocconi.it) or contact us via B in Touch – Library.

26 Mar 2025, by Library & Archives

Bocconi Historical Archives opens the door to history by applying AI: thanks to this new project, it is now possible to easily read hand-written texts and perform word searches on the oldest economic documents preserved at Bocconi.

### Which documents are readable with AI?

We have selected seventeenth-century mercantile letters that tell the story of trade between the Tuscan Saminiati family and major Italian and European cities in the period between 1627 and 1630.


### How do I get access?

The project on the Saminiati Mercantile Correspondence can be accessed on the [dedicated portal](https://app.transkribus.org/sites/saminiati).


### What can I do?

On the portal dedicated to the project you will find the image of the original text alongside its transcription, and you can do word searches by locating words in the original text.


### What tool did we use?

We used Transkribus, a platform created in the framework of the European project Horizon 2020, involving the collaboration of a group of European universities.

For more information, write to: [archivistorici@unibocconi.it](mailto:archivistorici@unibocconi.it)

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

This is just the beginning: new videos about Library & Archives spaces, services and resources will be coming soon.

02 Oct 2025, by Library & Archives

Finding your way through the financial identifiers maze

Library & Archives has just published a new library [guide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/identifiers) to help navigate the complex landscape of company and equity identifiers. The guide, available to the entire Bocconi community, is designed especially for students engaged in empirical research involving the use of financial and accounting databases. Mastering identifiers is a key skill for downloading data effectively.

The [guide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/identifiers) provides basic information on the main identifiers, along with **step-by-step instructions on how to obtain and use them in the Library databases**. To better understand the examples provided, it is helpful to already have some basic knowledge of financial databases. For a quick refresher, you can consult the [dedicated libguides](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/?group_id=15883 "https://unibocconi.libguides.com/?group_id=15883").

We hope you will find the guide useful and [ask a librarian](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/ask-librarian "https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/ask-librarian") should you need further support.

16 Sep 2025, by Library & Archives

The Library welcomes students for the new academic year, reminding everyone to follow simple rules that ensure a respectful, focused, and shared study environment.

As the new academic year begins, the Library once again welcomes students, offering study and research spaces designed to foster concentration, exchange, and personal growth.

To ensure a calm and efficient environment for everyone, it is important to keep in mind a few simple rules of coexistence:

- **Always book your study seat**, to guarantee fair access to spaces and services for all.
- **Keep your voice low**, to support both concentration and mutual respect.
- **Take care of the spaces and facilities**, as a sign of responsibility towards the whole community.

Please also note that any damage to University premises or facilities constitutes serious misconduct and may result in disciplinary action.

Respecting these simple essential rules helps improve everyone’s experience and makes the Library a welcoming place where we can study and grow together.

text:
What’s there for you?
1. _**Access the Library 24/7**_
Bocconi Library is located in via Gobbi 5, but you can explore its collections and services directly from the Library website. On the [**Library homepage**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives) you will find [**SearchLib**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en) **to** **access books, articles and more**, and the [**A-Z Databases list**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/az.php?q=) **, the quickest way to browse and access databases**. The Library offers also access to leading international [**newspapers**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/online-news-sources) and magazines, such as the Financial Times, The Economist and Il Sole 24 Ore, some of which are also available via **app**.

[**Libguides**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/) are a great way to help your students find relevant resources for your discipline. They provide a selection of key databases, journals and books recommended for different subjects, topics (for example, how to use [financial databases](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/az.php?q=) or find specific data) and services.

Fill in the dedicated [form](https://lib-unibocconi.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/discovery/blankIll?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1) for purchase and Inter-Library Loan requests: we shape our collections to match your research needs.
2. _**Course Reserves and teaching materials**_
The Library team can help you make required readings accessible to your students through the **Library Course Reserves** tool, integrated into your Blackboard course home page. Find out more about this [**service**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/course-reserves-textbooks).
3. _**Get tailored support**_
Faculty members can get help and assistance from the Library team directly at the [**Information Desk**](https://bocconi.stackmap.com/explore/1?c=97) in the Library, or by scheduling an **online meeting** via MS Teams or Zoom. To schedule a Library meeting, please write to [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it).

Each semester, the Library offers a series or [**workshops**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/library-workshops) on databases, research strategies, managing citations, and much more. As a faculty member, you can write to us to request **on-demand workshops** designed specifically for your class or research needs.

Need to **purchase data for a research project** using your personal research funds? Contact the Library. We will support you during the whole acquisition process, from getting the quotation through the final agreement with the vendor.
4. _**Publish in Open Access**_

metadata.title: Library Services for Faculty | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Library Services for Faculty | Bocconi University

text:
Bocconi University promotes **Open Access** as a key practice for making research freely available to all.  **Open Access publishing** is supported through **transformative agreements** with academic publishers, allowing Bocconi authors to publish in Open Access **at zero costs**. Discover more in our [**libguide dedicated to Open Access**](https://eur03.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Funibocconi.libguides.com%2Fc.php%3Fg%3D722521%26p%3D5248977&data=05%7C02%7Cfrancesca.bagaglia%40unibocconi.it%7Cd8b5ff1169a041bd4fc408dde475dd8a%7C6bf3b57a9fb447c29ada51156518f52f%7C1%7C0%7C638917917081075201%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJFbXB0eU1hcGkiOnRydWUsIlYiOiIwLjAuMDAwMCIsIlAiOiJXaW4zMiIsIkFOIjoiTWFpbCIsIldUIjoyfQ%3D%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=GO3EX381W40nSm3A2NNbB%2FnVYhdTUSIkYwSq1vioKo0%3D&reserved=0).

To find out further details about the Library services available to Bocconi Faculty, please consult the Library [**Service Charter**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/servicecharter).

For any questions or further assistance, feel free to contact us writing to [infobiblio@unibocconi.it.](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it)

11 Mar 2025, by Library & Archives

Discover what has changed

With an image-focused design and a mobile-first approach, the new website offers a refreshed browsing experience - simple, intuitive, and perfectly integrated with the University's content. Explore the tools, services, and resources of Library & Archives: not just a website, but a tool always available to you anytime and anywhere.


#### Need books and articles?

[**SearchLib**](https://library.unibocconi.it/): Start here to search for books, articles, and much more. Enter a title or keywords to begin your search.

#### Need a database?

[**A-Z Database List**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/az.php): Check which databases are available and access them quickly from here.

#### Need immediate support to find resources or use a database?

[**Libguides**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/) **:** Browse over 40 guides on specific topics (such as startups and artificial intelligence), disciplines, and specific databases (like Bloomberg and LSEG Workspace).

#### Want to check the reading lists for your courses?

**Course Reserves:** Find your course reading list from the course homepage in Blackboard to get access to articles, case studies and adopted textbooks. If you need help, take a look at this [libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/coursereserves).

19 Feb 2026, by Library & Archives

Bocconi Library & Archives is participating again this year in MuseoCity, an event featuring special openings, guided tours, workshops, meetings, and conferences promoted by 124 institutions across the city of Milan.

The [2026 edition](https://www.museocity.it/en/milano-museocity-2026) is entitled “Le imprese della cultura” (Cultural Enterprises) and the Bocconi Historical Archives have organized three guided tours to present, through documents, collections, and photographs, the history of the Bocconi Library as a cultural enterprise fostering knowledge.

We look forward to welcoming you on:

- **Saturday, 28 February** at 11:00am
- **Thursday, 5 March** at 1:00pm
- **Saturday, 7 March** at 11:00am

Reserve your place by writing to: [archivistorici@unibocconi.it](mailto:archivistorici@unibocconi.it)

Starting in January 2026, two reserved seats will be introduced in the Library’s reading rooms.

Thanks to booking through the **yoU@B PRO** app, access will be simple and immediate, ensuring maximum convenience for those who are entitled to use them.

In addition to these, five priority seats will be available on a non-exclusive basis: they can be booked and used by anyone, but must be vacated if requested by a person with disabilities. This is a simple act of consideration that fosters cooperation and mutual respect.

text:
News and Events Hub

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


24 Sep 2025, by Library & Archives

As part of Open Access Week 2025 Bocconi Library organizes, in partnership with Hertie School Library (Berlin), an event to explore the evolving relationship between Open Access (OA), copyright, and artificial intelligence (AI) in scholarly communication.

The workshop will address both the opportunities and the legal and ethical challenges of AI in academic research, with a focus on access to literature, copyright compliance in AI training, and sustainable solutions that support both academic integrity and technological innovation.

**When**: Thursday, 16 October 2025 2:00-5:00pm

**Where**: online

**How**: the event is free and it is open to all, [**registration**](https://events.unibocconi.eu/index.php?key=ev2025070081) is required

**Programme**

2:00 – 2:15pm Greetings and introduction

2:15 – 2:45pm “AI and open access: transforming the way we search for, analyse and publish academic literature” Christopher Landes (Hertie School)

2:45 – 3:15pm "Understanding copyright in the age of AI: considerations for academic researchers" Christine Daoutis (University College London)

3:15 – 3:45pm “Open Access and the Illusion of Lawful Use: Copyright Challenges in AI Training” Nicola Lucchi (Pompeu Fabra University)

3:45 – 4:00pm Break

4:00 – 5:00pm Round table: Nicolò Cavalli (Bocconi University), Joanna Bryson (Hertie School), Paola Corti (SPARC Europe)

The event is organized in collaboration with the network of universities of the [European Project CIVICA](https://www.civica.eu/).

For more information, please contact [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it).

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/news/Open-Access-%26-Copyright-in-the-Age-of-AI#addtoany "Show all")

13 Nov 2025, by Library & Archives

A workshop to boost your research career

**Are you a PhD student in your second year or beyond?**

Starting this year, you can include the Open Science for Researchers workshop in your [study plan](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/phd/phd-business-administration-and-management/courses-and-requirements).

**When:** November 27, 6:15–7:30 p.m.

**Where:** Online on Blackboard

**Assessment:** A short final quiz will certify attendance

**What you will learn**

- The fundamentals of **Open Science** and **Open Access**, essential tools for anyone pursuing an academic career.
- How Open Science practices promote **transparency** and **accessibility** in research.
- How to use **Creative Commons licences** to share your work while retaining intellectual property rights.
- How to identify and avoid **predatory publishers** that exploit researchers and their work.
- How to **publish in Open Access at no extra cost** with Elsevier, Springer, and other participating academic publishers.

**Registration**

Register through **yoU@B → Appointments**.

**Programme**

Check the [**workshop syllabus**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Open%20Science%20for%20Researchers%20Workshop%20aa%202025_2026.pdf?VersionId=YrLxWQCotwSvNgFomFtNS40HOcW36AES).

**Contacts**

For more information: [**infobiblio@unibocconi.it**](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it).

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


03 Apr 2025, by Library & Archives

Register for our online Open Science for Researchers workshop: learn the basics of Open Access agreements, predatory publishers and creative common licenses.

- What do you know about **Open Access** and how it drives transparency and accessibility in research?
- Did you know that with **Creative Commons licenses**, you can retain ownership of your work while sharing it widely?
- Are you aware of the risks posed by **predatory publishers** who might exploit researchers for profit?
- Did you know that you can **publish in Open Access at zero costs** with Elsevier, Springer and many more academic publishers?

Discover all of this and much more at our [_**Open Science for Researchers**_](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/library-workshops "Library Workshops") workshop, book now for **15 April** or **27 May** (6.15 – 7.30 pm).

_**How to book?**_

Through your [yoU@B student diary](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/), “Appointments” section. Bookings open 4 weeks prior to each event.

For more information, write to [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it)


Source: [UNESCO (2022). Understanding open science — Factsheet — SC-PBS-STIP/2022/OST/1. Paris, France: UNESCO](https://unesdoc.unesco.org/ark:/48223/pf0000383323)

text:
News and Events Hub

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


09 Dec 2025, by Library & Archives

A new tool integrated into SearchLib that enhances Interlibrary Loan and Document Delivery services

Starting today, accessing materials not available in the Bocconi Library collections is easier and faster thanks to Rapido, the new tool integrated into [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en) that enhances Interlibrary Loan and Document Delivery services.

### _**How does it work?**_

1. Start by searching in [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en).
2. If you can’t find the document you need, click the banner at the top of the results to “expand your search beyond the library’s collections.” You will then access a centralized index that includes Open Access content and the holdings of libraries participating in the network.
3. To request a document, click on “Get it for me from other libraries”. If you're requesting a book chapter, make sure to provide as many details as possible.
4. If you requested a digital document, you will receive a secure download link directly to your Bocconi e-mail address. Delivery times range from just a few hours to 1–2 business days, made possible by a system that automatically routes your request to the most suitable library based on location and availability.

If you requested a physical item, you’ll receive an e-mail notification when it becomes available for pickup at the Information Desk. Please note: delivery times depend on shipping, and loan periods are set by the lending library.
5. You can track the status of your requests in your personal area, under My Library Account - Requests.

If you still can’t find what you need, you can access the traditional request form by clicking the banner again.

### _**Why use Rapido?**_

- **More resources**: access materials not held by Bocconi Library directly from [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en).
- **Integrated experience**: no external forms or platforms. Search, request, and manage everything in one place.
- **Faster digital delivery**: thanks to automated processes and a global library network, articles and chapters arrive in record time.
- **A more sustainable choice**: the digital format for articles and chapters helps reduce paper use, transportation, and the overall environmental impact.
- **Global collaboration**: Bocconi’s collections are also shared within the Rapido network, contributing to international academic exchange.

The service is free of charge and available to all students, faculty, and staff of Bocconi University.

What are you waiting for? Try it now!

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/news/rapido#addtoany "Show all")

Explore Digital Newspapers and Magazines from Bocconi Library

Studying social sciences means developing strong critical thinking skills, also being up-to-date on what’s happening in the world.

Did you know you can read top publications like Il Sole 24 Ore, Financial Times, The Economist, Harvard Business Review and The New York Times every day in digital format, wherever you are?

Whether you’re researching global market trends, looking for real-world cases for an assignment, or simply trying to understand today’s headlines, the Library’s resources are there for you.

You can read **digital newspapers** and **magazines** from your laptop or smartphone, **on campus** or **remotely**. Some of them are also available via **app**. Access is free for Bocconi students, faculty and staff!

**Here are some titles:**


[Corriere della Sera](https://library.unibocconi.it/permalink/39UCLB_INST/chtij2/alma991005398654307991)

Textbooks in the Library are a shared resource: for you today, and for others tomorrow.

Textbooks in the Library are a shared resource: for you today, and for others tomorrow.

- **Do not highlight**
- **Do not underline**
- **Do not annotate**

Marked or damaged textbooks can become unreadable, especially for those who need clear and accessible reading. Preserving them intact is a matter of respect, responsibility, and inclusion.

📌In case of improper use (e.g., underlining, highlighting, writing...), **you may be required to reimburse the damaged item**.

💡 Library books belong to the whole community: keeping them intact is a simple gesture with great value.

🤝 Help us preserve them as a shared resource.

**Respect the books, respect those who use them.**

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


02 Feb 2026, by Library & Archives

A bibliographic guide to the Olympic Games

In preparation for the Milan–Cortina 2026 Winter Olympic Games, the Library has prepared a **bibliographic guide** dedicated to the event, with the aim of supporting multidisciplinary academic research on the topic.

The guide provides a curated selection of books, reports, journals, and websites that examine the Olympic Games primarily from an **organizational** and **management perspective**, while also considering their **historical evolution** and the **legacy** that this mega-event leaves on host territories.

The guide is freely available online and includes both resources subscribed by the Library and open access material open to everyone.

[**Access the guide**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/olympicgames)

**Did you already know our libguides?**

Over the last years, Library & Archives have published over 50 library guides ( _libguides_) to help our users navigate our resources and services. We have developed subject guides (like [Accounting](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/accounting)), topic guides ( [Artificial Intelligence](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/artificialintelligence)), and guides providing instructions on how to use databases ( [LSEG Workspace](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/refinitiv)), find data ( [Financial Statements](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/financialstatements)), and write your final work ( [Writing your dissertation / thesis](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/dissertation)).

**Explore all our** [**libguides**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/) **.**

text:
News and Events Hub

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


27 Jun 2025, by Library & Archives

A journey through the history of an avant-garde magazine, among advertising, rationalization and business culture

Bocconi Library & Archives is pleased to announce that the new virtual exhibition path dedicated to [L'Ufficio Moderno](https://virtualexhibitions.unibocconi.it/ufficio-moderno/en/1/l-ufficio-moderno-1926-1985?pageId=1), the magazine that marked an epoch in the Italian publishing and cultural landscape, is now available online. Through archive materials and historical images, the exhibition traces the entire parabola of the magazine: from its birth to its advertising campaigns - including the innovative collective initiative to promote Italian beer - to the meetings of the Gruppo Amici della Razionalizzazione in the 1930s.

The exhibition also takes an in-depth look at the editorial activity of L'Ufficio Moderno in the 1950s and 1960s, with particular attention to the **National Advertising Award** and the many cultural and entrepreneurial initiatives that made it a point of reference for the world of work, communication and business organisation.

The exhibition concludes with the decline and subsequent closure of the title, offering a critical look at the transformation of the role of specialist publishing after World War II.

[_**Visit the online itinerary now**_](https://virtualexhibitions.unibocconi.it/ufficio-moderno/en/1/l-ufficio-moderno-1926-1985?pageId=1)

An unmissable opportunity to discover - or rediscover - a fundamental page of 20th-century Italian work culture and innovation.

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/news/ufficio-moderno#addtoany "Show all")

The Library will be closed for [winter break](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/opening-hours "Opening Hours") from Sunday, 21 December to Sunday, 4 January included. On Saturday 20 December, Monday 5 and Tuesday 6 January, it will be open from 8.30am to 4pm (closing procedures start 30 minutes earlier).

Here’s what you can still do during the period in which the Library is closed for the holidays:

- _**Access e-books, e-journals, and databases**_

Most resources are available online 24/7. Search for them through our [**SearchLib**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&lang=en) discovery tool and access them directly.

**E-books and e-journals:** look for the item you need in SearchLib and click the link to access the full text. Want to learn how to optimize your search? Check the [**SearchLib guide**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/searchlib)!

**Databases:** did you know you can access most of our databases from home? Connect straight from our [**A–Z Databases**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/az.php?q=) page. Some databases require special access methods: always read the instructions provided below for each database name. Not sure which database fits your needs? Select your subject from the drop-down “subjects” menu and explore the recommended databases.

**Newspapers:** stay up to date with [**digital newspapers**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/online-news-sources).

- _**Borrowed books**_

If you have borrowed books, don’t worry: during the holiday period loans do not expire. You can return them after the break.

- _**Working on your dissertation?**_

To get started, you can take inspiration from the dissertations discussed over the years at our University: read the [**Consulting dissertations**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/consulting-dissertations) guide to learn how to access the dissertations archive from home.

Also consult our guide [**Writing your Dissertation/Thesis**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/dissertation) for suggestions on how to start gathering material for your thesis.

- _**Need help while the Library is closed?**_

Check our [**LibGuides**](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/): they point you to the main resources depending on your subject area (marketing, finance, law, etc.) or on a specific topic of interest (artificial intelligence, sustainability, …). Other LibGuides are more practical, offering step-by-step instructions on how to use specific resources.

Visit **B in Touch** as well: you’ll find an [**entire section dedicated to the Library**](https://bit.unibocconi.it/hc/en-us/categories/4405709230354-Library) **,** with the most frequently asked questions (and answers!). Remember to sign in.

See you on Monday, 5 January at 8.30!

_**Happy holidays!**_

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/news/winter-break-2025#addtoany "Show all")

text:
News and Events Hub

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)


18 Mar 2026, by Library & Archives

A new Library channel to help you keep track of due dates in a simple and integrated way

From now on, loan due dates are integrated into the yoU@B student diary, making them easier to manage.

Specifically:

- in the **“Deadlines” section of the Calendar**, you will find automatic updates of the due dates for your current loans;
- you will receive a **notification on the actual due date**.

Please note that, thanks to the automatic renewal system, due dates may change over time. Books are automatically renewed for up to a maximum of 6 months in total, except in the following cases:

- the item is an **exam textbook** (only one automatic one-week renewal is allowed);
- the item has been **requested by another user**;
- the **maximum loan period** has been reached.

If you receive a due date notification, this means that no further renewals are possible.

You may return books at any time using the Book return boxes (also through a delegated person), by mailing them or by contacting the Library team at the Information Desk. If there are no reservations or blocks due to overdue items, you may borrow the book again and start a new loan period.

You can always check the updated status of your loans by accessing [**My Library Account**](https://lib-unibocconi.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/discovery/account?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1&section=overview&lang=en). For more information, please visit the dedicated webpage [Borrow, Renew and Return Books](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/using-library/borrow-renew-and-return-books).

This new channel has been created to make communication about due dates more immediate and effective, supporting responsible loan management. Respecting due dates helps ensure fair access to the Library’s collections for the entire Bocconi community.

[Home Library & Archives](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)

text:
## Newspapers

Here's a list of the most popular newspapers that you can access online. Some of them require to create an account, here you can find all the instructions:

- [**Corriere della Sera (Archivio Pro)**](https://go.openathens.net/redirector/unibocconi.eu?url=https%3A%2F%2Farchivio.corriere.it)

Includes all articles published on Corriere della Sera from 1876 up to now. The current edition is available from around 11 am (click on _Ricerca ultimi 7 giorni_)

- [**Il Sole 24 Ore**](https://du.ilsole24ore.com/utenti/authfiles/loginaziende.aspx?caller_id=CRUI.MYDESK&user_uniqueid=crui)

Access restricted to current Bocconi University and SDA members.

Enter _Universita Bocconi (OpenAthens)_ as the organization's name and login with your Bocconi/SDA credentials.

Scroll down the page and click on _Quotidiani_ within the section _I miei prodotti_. Registration required once a year through the dedicated form.

- [**Financial Times**](https://ft.com/Bocconi)

Access restricted to current Bocconi University and SDA members.

Once you've registered, you can also access the newspaper through the app.

Share links to articles exclusively by using any [sharing tools](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qPjJH4CRVng "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qpjjh4crvng") made available by FT.

- [**The New York Times**](http://www.nytimes.com/)

Access restricted to current Bocconi University and SDA members.

You need a **NYT account** (academic pass) to access. Visit [accessnyt.com](http://accessnyt.com/), type in **Bocconi** as school name, click on the institution, and create your account to claim and activate your **one-year pass**. You must use your Bocconi/SDA e-mail address. Once your pass is active, you will be able to access nytimes.com from anywhere.

Once you've registered, you can also access the newspaper through the NYT app.

- [**The Wall Street Journal**](http://www.wsj.com/)

Access restricted to current Bocconi University and SDA members.

You need a **WSJ account** to access. [Register](https://go.openathens.net/redirector/unibocconi.eu?url=https%3A%2F%2Fpartner.wsj.com%2Fenter-redemption-code%2FUCLB8qrudjag) with your Bocconi/SDA e-mail address. Once your account is active, you will be able to access WSJ.com from anywhere. Access is valid for one year. After the expiration, you can [re-activate it](https://partner.wsj.com/enter-redemption-code/UCLB8qrudjag). **ATTENTION!** You must be **on-campus** to re-activate it.

Once you've registered, you can also access the newspaper through the WJS app.

- [**The Economist**](https://myaccount.economist.com/s/institution-login?entity=https://idp.unibocconi.eu/entity)

Access restricted to current Bocconi University and SDA members.

You can also access the newspaper through the app: once you have downloaded it, click on “Log In” in the top right corner, then sign in using your Bocconi/SDA email address and selecting “Università Bocconi (OpenAthens)".

text:
## Let us know what you think!

We work every day to ensure that Bocconi library users are satisfied with the services offered. Our aim is to provide a quality experience for all users of our services and we welcome feedback, whether positive or negative. Together we want to build the Library of today and tomorrow, one that increasingly meets your dreams and desires.

Would you like to tell us what you think?

If you would like to send **suggestions, comments and ideas** on how to improve services or complaints to help us improve, you can fill in the [Feedback form](https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=erXza7Sfwkea2lEVZRj1L6bDyXFMNdxOi2DioP2q8_NUMk80NVNVMDNKNFJQV0JaOVk4UFVSQUxCUi4u)

If you would like to **propose** the **purchase** of a title that you cannot find among the Library's collections, you can share all the details of your proposal by carefully filling in the [Can't find it?](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/blankIll?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1) form.

Thank you, we look forward to hearing from you!

text:
Library & Archives

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)


Teaching and Research Support

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support)

Ask a Librarian



#### In-Person Support (Ask a Librarian!)

If you're in the Library and need assistance, you can rely on the in-person reference service: Ask a Librarian! Visit the Information Desk in the reading rooms during the following hours:

\- Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM.

At the Information Desk, our specialized staff is available to assist you with:

\- Databases – Guidance and support for navigating online databases and using the Data Room 015

\- Bibliographic Information – Helping you find all the print and online resources you need, including textbooks and other Course Reserves

\- Loans – Borrowing and consulting Library resources, as well as requesting documents through Interlibrary Loan (ILL)


#### Online support

If you're off-campus and need assistance, you can always reach out to the Library staff by emailing infobiblio@unibocconi.it or contacting us via B-in-Touch. You will receive an answer within 3 working days.

Our team can help with:

\- Guidance on using databases and support with bibliographic citation tools

\- Fixing access issues for Library resources

\- Finding print and online materials, including textbooks and other Course Reserves

\- Orientation on Library services

\- Purchase requests or document supply through Interlibrary Loan (ILL) and Document Delivery (DD)

External users are also welcome to contact the Library at infobiblio@unibocconi.it. However, priority will be given to requests from members of the Bocconi Community.

Please note that the Library staff does not:

\- Conduct bibliographic searches or perform database queries on behalf of users

\- Provide complete subject-specific bibliographies or repertories


#### Assistance by appointment (Library meetings)

Bocconi students and Faculty members can request one-to-one support through a Library Meeting.

Appointments are available in person at the Information Desk or online via Teams, depending on availability. Each session lasts 30 minutes.

\- Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM

To schedule a Library Meeting, email infobiblio@unibocconi.it, specifying your research needs.

text:
## RefWorks

RefWorks is the bibliographic management tool ( _citation manager_) supported by the Library. It is web-based, thus allowing you to access your references from any web connected device, with no need to install any specific software.

RefWorks will help you save time and maximize your efforts in collecting and track your sources.

You will be able to:

- create a personal database of references collected from different kind of sources, such as databases, web, and the like
- organize, annotate, and tag your references
- automatically build and format a bibliography in a wide range of citation styles
- add references directly to your paper as you write

metadata.title: Bibliographic Management Tool | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: RefWorks

text:
The law allows to make a single printed copy of an item protected by copyright, without any authorization from the rights holder, for individual study and in line with the following criteria:
- journals: one article, in any case within a maximum limit of 15% of the total number of pages of the issue
- books: one chapter, in any case within a maximum limit of 15% of the book’s total number of pages
These are actually exceptions. Infact, for any reproduction (even partial) of a protected item, it would be necessary to obtain a specific authorization from the copyright holder. To allow photocopies of protected items (within the above-mentioned limits) on one hand and to remunerate copyright holders on the other, Universities have made an agreement with SIAE and authors’ and publishers’ associations under which Universities pay a “fair compensation”, distributed to authors and publishers.

Scanning is different from photocopying and thus does not fall within the exceptions mentioned above. To scan material, you must have the explicit authorization from the copyright holders (author and/or publisher), granted personally or through a copyright clearance service. This is the procedure followed by Bocconi Library to reproduce parts of textbooks in digital format.

Receiving photocopies and scans from the Library

In the framework of Interlibrary Loan and Intercampus Delivery services, the Library delivers photocopies or digital documents from other libraries or from the Library physical collections to students and faculty members. In both cases the material is shared solely for personal use, for the purpose of study or research. It is therefore not permitted to reproduce these items or to send them to others by any means (fax, e-mail, sharing on social networks). Nor is it permitted to distribute copies during a lesson. If you need to share material for a course, please contact the Library ( [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@uniboccon.it)) to request reuse permission from the publisher.

Studying and teaching with digital documents

The Library delivers book chapters and journal articles in electronic format specified by faculty members for the preparation of exams. The documents are organised by subject. In order to grant this service the Library has requested authorizations from the legitimate copyright holders and paid for reproduction licenses. The course reserve section is password protected, therefore accessible only by the faculty members in charge of the courses and enrolled students. The texts are either downloadable or available online only, depending on license terms. However, it is important to remember that only an exclusively personal use is permitted, limited to the duration of the course. Sending documents to others or sharing them on social networks and external websites is not allowed.

Consulting a thesis

Theses are both administrative documents, as they attest the regularity of a course of study, and scholarly documents, as they are intellectual works. They come under copyright law precisely because they are the result of graduates’ creative activity. Any reproduction constitutes copyright infringement and consultation is only possible if the author granted permission when submitting the thesis. The author can allow or suspend permission at any time. Both printed and digital theses are stored in the Library. Consultation is allowed, but full or partial reproduction is strictly forbidden.

Online content

All digital content and objects are also protected by copyright: use of web pages and softwares must be made in the respect of the intellectual property rights of the authors. Please remember that freely accessible material is still generally subject to a license for reuse. It may, therefore, be forbidden to:
- copy or modify online content
- send it to third parties
- share it on social media
- publish it on other websites

metadata.title: Copyright | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Copyright | Bocconi University

text:
Artificial Intelligence Platforms

Copyright regulations and intellectual property laws fully apply to the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools and platforms.

The protection of intellectual works remains fully in force, regardless of the digital or technological medium used.

Unless such use falls within the exceptions provided by copyright law, it is not permitted to upload, input or use copyrighted material (such as thesis, full-text articles, images, e-books) on AI services without first obtaining permission from the rights holders, or if the terms of use of the databases from which the material was retrieved do not expressly allow it.

Open Access

It is important to make an informed choice about how to publish your research. Assigning the rights to publish one's work to a publisher on an exclusive basis is not always the best solution. The Bocconi Library promotes Open Access to scholarly literature by organizing training initiatives and offering advice to faculty on how to publish in open access, in line with European Commission guidelines that support this mode of publication.

Bocconi Library adheres on behalf of the University to transformative agreements signed through the CARE-CRUI consortium to enable Bocconi faculty, researchers and students to publish in Open Access in hybrid journals. For more information on the contracts to which Bocconi University adheres, please see the [Open Access](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/open-access) page.

Citing and referencing

Citing documents is above all an act of intellectual honesty and a form of respect to the author (moral right). Universities and University libraries, as part of their mission to develop scholarly research, are responsible for disseminating appropriate citation methods. Bocconi Library regularly offers workshops on citation methods and standards.

Use of images

Images (e.g. photos, drawings, pictorial works, photographic reproductions of artistic work, graphs, tables reproduced as images) are works protected by copyright law, even when they are reproduced in a book, in another document or on the Internet. Partial reproduction of images may be allowed for purposes of criticism and discussion, always citing the source. If reproduction occurs for research or teaching purposes, "the use must be for illustrative and non-commercial purposes", provided that the reproduction "does not compete with the work reproduced" (art. 70 of the Italian Copyright Act). If an illustration is excerpted from a book, it is necessary to cite both the book and the original source of the image.
