text:
### For Students

Access your reading list directly from your course page on [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/). In each list, you will find:

- Books and eBooks available at the Library
- Articles, case studies and book chapters in full text

Why use it?

- No need to search on SearchLib: everything is ready to use. Each list includes direct links to Library-subscribed online resources, along with details on the location and number of available copies for printed materials
- Easy access from any device, on campus and from home

For more information, take a look at our [dedicated libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/coursereserves) or watch the videos below.

**Where can I find textbooks and other print course materials required for exams?**

If online versions of required resources cannot be purchased, the Library will acquire print copies and make them available in a dedicated open-shelf area: the Course Reserves (Textbooks) Collection, located near the Information Desk.

**How long can I borrow course materials?**

To make course materials easy to find, we use a color-coded system and special call numbers:

- Green sticker: borrowable for 2 weeks, renewable once for 1 additional week.
- Red sticker: not loanable, Library-use only.

**Will I find all essential readings in the Course Reserves Collection?**

The Library works actively to ensure that all required textbooks are available in the Course Reserves (Textbooks) Collection. We review and update the collection at the beginning of each academic year, checking both availability and physical condition of materials.

But if you notice something is missing, please get in touch at [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it) so we can look into it.

**How can I check if a required reading is available at the Library?**

Just follow these steps:

1\. Login to [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/) with your Bocconi credentials and access one of your courses

2\. Click on the Course Reserves tool

You’ll be able to:

- Browse your class’ reading list
- Download or read online resources
- Check the availability of print copies and request them

**Need help?**

If you need help, come visit us at the Information Desk during [opening hours](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/opening-hours "Opening Hours") or contact us at [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it). For full details, check our [dedicated libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/coursereserves).

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/course-reserves-textbooks#accordion-boxed-126381)

metadata.title: Course Reserves (Textbooks) | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Course Reserves (Textbooks) | Bocconi University > For Students

text:
### For Faculty

The Library Course Reserves tool helps you manage, update and share course reading lists in just a few steps. With Course Reserves, you can:

- Build and edit lists with books, eBooks, articles, and web resources
- Submit purchase and licensing requests to the Library
- Ask for digitization services in compliance with IP regulations

For more information, take a look at our [dedicated libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/coursereserves) or watch the videos below.

**How to get started:**

You can access the Library Course Reserves through your course page on [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/). The Library is here to support you at every step: please refer to the dedicated Libguide for full details.

To schedule a training session or for further assistance, contact: [coursereserves@unibocconi.it](mailto:coursereserves@unibocconi.it).

**How it works:**

1\. The Library creates reading lists for all courses offered in the Undergraduate, Graduate, and Law programs.

2\. Instructors can enrich the lists by adding resources from the Library collections or importing citations from RefWorks.

3\. Instructors may also request new acquisitions or licensing for additional relevant materials.

4\. The Library makes all course materials available through the reading list, including online and print resources.

5\. Reading lists are published and integrated into each course’s [Blackboard](https://blackboard.unibocconi.it/) page.

6\. Students access and use materials from any device, both on campus and remotely.

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/course-reserves-textbooks#accordion-boxed-126426)

Marked or damaged textbooks can become unreadable, especially for those who need clear and accessible reading. Preserving them intact is a matter of respect, responsibility, and inclusion.

In case of improper use (e.g., underlining, highlighting, writing...), **you may be required to reimburse the damaged item**.

European Documentation Centre (EDC)

[Open Access](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/open-access) [Bibliographic Management Tool](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/bibliographic-management-tool) [Copyright](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/copyright)

[_**State of the Union Speech 2025**_](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/L%26A_Statodell%27Unione2025.pdf "State of the Union 2025")

The European Documentation Centre (EDC) of the Library was established in 1983 based on an agreement between Bocconi University and European Commission. It is part of a [network](https://ec.europa.eu/europedirect/visit_us/edc/index_en.htm) which includes 600 centres across the world, mostly in universities, research centres and national libraries, with 400 of them in the Member States and 51 in Italy.

The Bocconi EDC is member of the Editor's Group of [**abCDEuropa**](http://wikicde.cnr.it/index.php/Pagina_principale), the wiki guide of the Italian EDCs, and of cdeita, the national website of the [Italian EDCs network](https://cdeita.it/).

Free publications and booklets by the European Union are available at the EDC Reading Room (Room 111) on the first floor of the Library. Reference and research assistance on the European Union, EU databases and EU documents is available for the Bocconi community and external users by appointment. Please send an e-mail to [cde@unibocconi.it](mailto:cde@unibocconi.it), or call +39 02 58365147.

Here you can find a selection of resources curated by the EDC:

EU web resources

The EDC of Bocconi Library has selected the lists of official websites of EU Institutions, Agencies, Organisations and their Representations in Italy and a list of dissertations about the European Union discussed in Bocconi University:

[EU policies](https://commission.europa.eu/index_en)

[EU Institutions](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/eu-institutions "EU Institutions")

[EU Agencies](https://european-union.europa.eu/institutions-law-budget/institutions-and-bodies_en)

Library Workshops


Need to find academic articles, books and data for your research?

Doubts on writing a bibliography for your thesis?

Are you a faculty member interested in organising a class session on specific resources?

Bocconi Library offers a **program of** **scheduled workshops** and is available for organising **on demand workshops** upon request from Faculty members.

metadata.title: Library Workshops | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Library Workshops | Bocconi University

text:
## Our workshops

Library tools

Don’t miss this workshop to discover all the tools available through the Library to support you during your courses, assignments and exams! At the end of this workshop, you will know where to find all the resources and data you need and get the most out of the Library.

This workshop is particularly recommended for new students, students who are starting working on their final work and for everyone who wants to get an overview on Library services and resources.

**Library workshop season is over! See you at the beginning of the next Academic Year!**

Databases

The workshops are aimed at exploring different features and the add-ins of some of the databases subscribed by Bocconi Library.

#### Upcoming Workshops

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **TITLE** | **DATE** | **TIMETABLE** | **LANGUAGE** | **LOCATION** |
| **APIs with LSEG data: explore LSEG Workspace Codebook** | 15 MAY | 2.30-3.30pm | ENGLISH | ONLINE (Blackboard) |

Citations and bibliography

The workshops are aimed at providing the basic skills needed to correctly cite bibliographic sources when writing academic texts (final work, papers, class assignments) and to use the citation manager software RefWorks that can help you creating citations and bibliographies automatically!

**Library workshop season is over! See you at the beginning of the next Academic Year!**

Law

The Library offers the following workshops targeted to law students' specific needs:

• **Library Tools**: to get an overview of the search tools to browse Library Law collections and explore legal databases.

• **Citation**: to master citations and bibliography for your written academic texts.

**Library workshop season is over! See you at the beginning of the next Academic Year!**

_**Where do workshops take place?**_

Online workshops take place on Blackboard. In-person workshops take place in Library room 111, on the first floor of the Library (Gobbi 5), or in the Financial Markets Data Room (Via Sarfatti 25, basement).

_**How to book?**_

Through your [yoU@B student diary](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/), “Appointments” section. Workshops are confirmed with a minimum of 4 participants. You will receive a confirmation email the day before the workshop.

text:
## Welcome!

Library & Archives supports the Bocconi community and works every day to make information accessible and to offer services for teaching, learning, research, and inspiration.

The Library is open from [Monday to Sunday](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/opening-hours). You can discover the world of the online Library 24 hours a day also off-campus and access all the services dedicated to you, entering into your personal area [sign in](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/login?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1).

metadata.title: Access to Bocconi Library & Archives | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Welcome!

text:
## Library:

- collections (print and digital): books, periodicals, statistics and research databases focusing on economics, law and social sciences
- individual study seats in the reading rooms
- Phone Booths: soundproof booths dedicated to online calls and meetings. For more details, you can refer to the [**Rules for utilizing Phone Booths in the Library**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Phone_booths_ENG_251125.pdf "RULES FOR UTILIZING PHONE BOOTHS IN THE LIBRARY")

From the [yoU@B student diary](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/) you can:

1. generate a temporary QrCode to access, as an alternative to the Bocconi ID Card
2. view the [**Rules for utilizing study seats in the Library rooms and conduct to be observed**](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Rules%20Seat%20management%20_27-03-25.pdf?VersionId=1pE.Aom6eTIkLayJ9qgLL9ZCaQ4N26Cr): their subscription is necessary to access the Library; you must comply with these rules throughout your stay
3. reserve your study seat or a Phone Booth through the **Study Places/Desk Finder** section: reservation is **mandatory**

- dedicated services [(Accessibility and inclusion)](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/accessibility-and-inclusion)
- [ask a librarian!](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/ask-librarian)

### Booking a study seat in the Library

Watch video


Learn how to book your study seat in the Library using the yoU@B Pro app with this AI-assisted video. You will also discover tips to manage your reservations and to solve the most common issues.

Remember: **booking a study seat in the Library is mandatory for the entire duration of your stay**.

Please note: if you can't see the video, click on the green lock in the bottom right corner and accept all cookies.

[Transcript](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/media/attachments/Booking%20a%20study%20seat%20video%20transcript.pdf "Booking a study seat video transcript")

### Data Room

In the Data Room you can find the following databases, available upon reservation:

- Bloomberg
- LSEG Workspace

[Reserve a slot](https://libdatabases.unibocconi.it/)

[Read the reservation policy](https://libdatabases.unibocconi.it/modules/policies.php)

All other databases are accessible [online](https://lib.unibocconi.it/), including [off-campus](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/using-library/using-online-resources).

metadata.title: Borrow, Renew and Return Books | Bocconi University

text:
## Borrowing books

If you’re a member of the Bocconi Community (students, faculty, Bocconi Alumni Community, staff), you can borrow most items from the Library collections.

You just need:

- your Bocconi ID card or the QRcode created through the [yoU@B student Diary](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/)
- the books you’ve picked up from the shelves

If you need help, [ask a Librarian](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/teaching-and-research-support/ask-librarian) at the Information Desk.

The external community can contact the Library team by writing to [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it) for information about books.

### For how long can I keep a book on loan?

You can borrow books for **4 weeks**, except for textbooks, which can be borrowed for **2 weeks** (more information in the [dedicated libguide](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/coursereserves)).

metadata.title: Borrow, Renew and Return Books | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Borrowing books

text:
## Renewing books

We automatically renew loaned books for up to 6 months, except for these specific cases:

- the book is a textbook: in this case, there is one automatic renewal of 1 week
- item has been requested by someone else and must be returned
- the due date would be after the expiration date of your Bocconi account

If we need you to return a book, we will e-mail you with a revised due date.

metadata.title: Borrow, Renew and Return Books | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Renewing books

text:
## Returning books

Return books using the self-service machines on the ground floor of the Library and put them in the Book return boxes.

If you don't want to enter the Library, place your books into the Book return box at the Library entrance.

We also accept postal returns to:

**Università Bocconi. Biblioteca e Archivi. Via Ulisse Gobbi, 5 - 20136 Milano (Italia)**

Please retain proof of posting until items are discharged from your Library account.

metadata.title: Borrow, Renew and Return Books | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Returning books

text:
## Request books out on loan

To ensure the fair circulation of material we have a process where Bocconi Library users can request a book that is on loan to another reader.

When a book you have searched for is on loan it will show on [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/) as: On loan until ...

To place a request on the book:

1. Sign in
2. Search for a book
3. Click on the book to display the record of the item
4. In the 'Get it' section of the record click the request option
5. Fill in any relevant information in the short form
6. Submit your request by clicking the green request option

We will email you when the item is ready for you to collect.

### Access material not available: Can’t find it? service

Bocconi students and faculty can use the [Can't find it?](https://lib-unibocconi.primo.exlibrisgroup.com/discovery/blankIll?vid=39UCLB_INST%3AVU1) service to get access to material not available at Bocconi Library. Through Interlibrary Loan, it is possible to expand the search to other libraries’ collections and request the resources needed.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/using-library/borrow-renew-and-return-books#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Borrow, Renew and Return Books | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Request books out on loan

text:
## How it works

Interlibrary Loan (ILL) is a service that allows libraries to borrow and share documents. Books or articles not owned by the Bocconi Library can be requested from a network of partner libraries.

To use the service:

1. **Search in** [**SearchLib**](https://library.unibocconi.it/discovery/search?vid=39UCLB_INST:VU1).

If the document is not available in the Bocconi collections, click on the banner at the top of the results page to “expand your search beyond the library’s collections”. This will give you access to a central index that includes Open Access resources and materials from partner libraries.
2. **Request the document.**

Select “Get it for me from other libraries” to submit your request:

- for digital documents, you will receive a secure link in your institutional e-mail;
- for print documents, you will receive an e-mail notification when they are ready for pick-up at the Information Desk.

3. **Still haven’t found what you need?**

You can access the traditional Can’t find it? form by clicking the banner again.

All requests are reviewed by the Library Team, also with a view to potential collection development.

metadata.title: Can't Find It? (Interlibrary Loan) | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: How it works

text:
## Interlending services for external libraries

External libraries can [submit requests via email.](mailto:gila@unibocconi.it)

The supply of books (Interlibrary Loan) and articles or book chapters (Document Delivery) complies with current copyright regulations, the licensing terms of electronic resources, and the preservation status of the documents.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/using-library/cant-find-it#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Can't Find It? (Interlibrary Loan) | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Interlending services for external libraries

text:
## Accessing online resources

The Library website serves as access point to our resources. At the center of the homepage, you will find all our search tools: **SearchLib** and **A-Z Database List**.

[SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/) is our discovery tool to browse our print and electronic collections, and beyond. SearchLib is particularly recommended for browsing academic material and request books.

If you’re looking for statistics, reports or other specialistic content, you might need to look into specific databases. To do that, check out our [A-Z Database List](https://unibocconi.libguides.com/az.php?q=). This alphabetical list of all our bibliographic and numeric databases is particularly useful to check what’s available through the Library and quickly access the platforms. Next to the resource titles, you will find icons explaining access information.

metadata.title: Use Online Resources | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Accessing online resources

text:
## Off-campus access

Even if you are off-campus you can access a number of databases, e-journals, e-books and other online resources of the Library. In order to access online material, you will need to authenticate: the authentication system is called OpenAthens.

Who is entitled to off-campus access? Regularly enrolled undergraduate, Master’s and PhD students, authorized faculty members, Bocconi University and SDA staff.

For information and assistance write to [infobiblio@unibocconi.it](mailto:infobiblio@unibocconi.it)

metadata.title: Use Online Resources | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Off-campus access

text:
## Responsible use of online resources

The majority of electronic resources (databases, e-journals, e-books) made available through the Bocconi Library are governed by license agreements between the Library and publishers or vendors. These define specific terms and conditions of use, i.e. authorized users, permissions and restrictions.

**Access to the licensed online resources is for educational and research use only.**

Here you can find a list of usually permitted and not permitted uses:

- **Displaying, searching, downloading, printing and storing the licensed material** for scholarly research, educational and personal use only

- **Creating a temporary link** to a journal article or a book/book chapter for use in electronic course packs or learning management systems provided proper reference to the author and source is given, and with the authorization of the Library (only authorized users can access it)

- **Sharing your own IDs and password** with third parties, nor to enable anyone other than authorized user to use the licensed material
- **Distributing or transmitting** (by whatever means, e.g. fax, e-mail, social networks, etc.) the licensed material to anyone other than authorized users
- **Systematically downloading, printing and storing** an excessive portion of the licensed materials (e.g. an entire journal issue or e- book), also by use of specific software
- **Exporting and using the content in artificial intelligence tools** is not permitted unless such use is explicitly authorized by the resource’s terms of use or falls within the limits of copyright exceptions, in particular the personal-use reproduction of non-substantial parts of a work (typically up to 15% of the work, by analogy with the provisions that apply to analogue reproductions)
- **Removing, obscuring or modifying any copyright** author attribution or other notices included in the licensed material
- Making **any use** **for commercial purposes**
- Making any use that exceeds or violates the terms and conditions of use **without the prior written permission of Licensor**

You can read the full license details of a specific resource searching for a specific database in [SearchLib](https://library.unibocconi.it/) and clicking on “Show terms of use”:


Consequences of misuse

Publishers monitor the usage of electronic resources to verify compliance with licensing agreements.

**Misuse or violation of license restrictions may result in the termination of the license agreement and in the withdrawal of any access rights to the electronic resource**

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/using-library/use-online-resources#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Use Online Resources | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Responsible use of online resources

text:
Library & Archives

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives)



Founded in 1902 by Ferdinando Bocconi as “Institute of Higher Education in Economics”, Bocconi University was the **first Italian University in the field**. From the original location in via Statuto, in 1941 the University moved to via Sarfatti, which at the time was outskirts of the city. The first building in via Sarfatti 25 was designed by **Giuseppe Pagano** and represents a **perfect example of rationalist architecture**.

In the following years, **Giovanni Muzio** together with his son Lorenzo designed the Student House, followed by the Library. In the framework of the Strategic Plan “Bocconi 2000”, Bocconi University decided to further expand the campus and the first SDA School of Management was built by **Vittore Cerretti**, followed by the velodrome by **Ignazio Gardella** and the **Via Roentgen** building, designed by Grafton Studio and inaugurated in 2008.

Finally, in 2019 Bocconi campus expanded to an area previously occupied by a **milk processing plant** through a project of urban requalification. Designed by Studio Sanaa, the new campus comprises the buildings of the new SDA School of Management, the Castiglioni student house and a new sport center.

Bocconi University offers guided tours, open to the general public, of its urban campus in downtown Milan. Tour guides will tell stories about the people and architecture of the University, as well as share interesting facts about the campus and the history of Bocconi.

Two types of tours are available, both in Italian and English:

- **Full tour**, lasting about two hours, with access to several buildings
- **Short tour**, lasting about one hour, outdoors only

[More information on upcoming dates and bookings](https://www.unibocconi.eu/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/campus+and+services/bocconi+urban+campus/campus+tour "campus tour")

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/whats/bocconi-campus-tour#addtoany "Show all")

text:
The Bocconi Library is a place that metaphorically represents the meeting point between all generations of students at the University. For this reason, the initiative of Bocconi Donors who have chosen over the years to philanthropically name one of its spaces, to associate their own name or the name of a loved one with a place of great symbolic value, is particularly significant. The named spaces are recognisable by the presence of a plaque at the entrance, bearing the name of the Donors and the dedication - in memory or dedicated to - of those whose name or memory is associated with the room.

text:
## A better and more sustainable world for all

### The 2020 UN Agenda for Sustainable Development

The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, provides a shared blueprint for peace and prosperity for people and the planet, now and into the future. The core are the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), which are an urgent call for action by all countries in a global partnership.


### Bocconi and the UN Agenda 2030 for sustainable development

Bocconi’s most direct contribution to the United Nations 2030 Agenda for sustainable development is in research and teaching, and in particular Quality Education (SDG 4), Gender Equality (SDG 5), Decent Work and Economic Growth (SDG 8), Reduced Inequalities (SDG 10), Sustainable Cities and Communities (SDG 11) and Climate Action (SDG 13). Bocconi University also contributes to the development of multiple fields of study that address, in a multidisciplinary manner, many other SDGs.

metadata.title: Pick your Goal! | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: A better and more sustainable world for all

text:
## Pick your goal!

### Bocconi Library and the UN Agenda 2030 for sustainable development

The Library promotes the 17 goals of the United Nations 2030 Agenda by ensuring inclusive services and environments in a stimulating climate that meets the needs of learning, teaching, research and interaction. Now the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are even closer and visible among the collections in the reading rooms. All that it takes is to discover them together. Only the broadest and most crosscutting exchange enables the advancement of scientific research and understanding of the world: are you ready to go?

### Want to find out more about the 17 goals of the United Nations?

Click on the icon to discover each goal.

You will find:

- A brief **description** of the goal
- **Keywords** for searching relevant materials in [**SearchLib**](https://library.unibocconi.it/ "SearchLib")
- The **shelves** where the books are located

### Need help?

Ask the **Library team!**



[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/library-archives/whats/pick-your-goal#addtoany "Show all")

Technology and Information Services

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/technology-and-information-services)


(Service only available for Teachers and Technical-administrative staff)

A **VPN**, **Virtual Private Network**, is a private network activated between individuals, using the Internet as a public means of communication. Thanks to a VPN access server, a communication channel is created through the Internet, which carries encrypted and thus secure information.

The user can therefore use the internal network and it's services from home or from any internet station as he were physically operating from the Campus.

In addition, users that access Bocconi email through client applications (eg: Microsoft Outlook) can set the SMTP Bocconi server (smtp.unibocconi.it) as the default outgoing mail server when authenticating in the **VPN**.

To use this service, users need to have the necessary credentials (username and password).

Authentication is made by the LDAP protocol.

**The service is available only for Teachers and Technical-administrative staff**. The new VPN client software and the installation instructions are available by the [Professor's Diary](https://agenda.unibocconi.it/ "PROFESSOR'S AGENDA").

For any problems or for further information, please send an email to the following address: [noc@unibocconi.it](mailto:noc@unibocconi.it)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/technology-and-information-services/access-bocconi-network-through-vpn#addtoany "Show all")

Technology and Information Services

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/technology-and-information-services)


**LTCC** (the Learning Technology Competence Centre) is the technology research and services office established by ICT in 1999 to support and spread the use of e-learning platforms. **LTCC** also contributes to the implementation of multimedia teaching projects of general interest to the University.

**LTCC** makes its skills and technological knowhow available to both the **Faculty**'s courses and **SDA Bocconi** initiatives. It also supports the continuous training of personnel organized by **Human Resources**.

This office provides **support** services to students and participants via email. It assists faculty members through **personalized training** on the technological potential of platforms. It is also in charge of activities related to the scheduling and management of access to online courses.

Technology and Information Services

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/technology-and-information-services)


Bocconi University has joined the [IDEM Federation](https://www.idem.garr.it/en/) (IDEntity Management per l’accesso federato), the Italian Authentication and Authorization infrastructures federation for the GAAR Network, the italian network for Research and Education. GAAR coordinates the various activities and provides the central infrastructure, the services and manages the applications.

IDEM is the first italian federation of Authentication and Authorization (AAI). Its goal is to allow the members of the scientific and academic community to easily access services and online content that's made available by various organizations.


Joining IDEM provides users at Università Bocconi with the convenience of using institutional login information to access all the resources available through the Federation.

Services that can be reached through the Federation are made available by [IDEM's partners and members](https://www.idem.garr.it/en/join/participants).

For any issues and information regarding the IDEM Federation, send an email to: [idem@unibocconi.it](mailto:idem@unibocconi.it).

[Notes about user attributes release for federated authentication IDEM-GARR-AAI](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/notes-about-user-attributes-release-federated-authentication-idem-garr-aai)

**High Performance Computing**

Bocconi University provides access to **High Performance Computing (HPC)** resources to all faculty members, supporting the research community for those computations that cannot be performed on a local/personal machines.

The facility is currently built with 24 nodes interconnected with a special high-speed Infiniband network, with 752cores, 16 Nvidia Ampere A100 GPUs, 8 Nvidia H100 GPUs, 16 Nvidia H200 GPUs, 20TB of RAM and about 250TB of storage

| nodes | RAM Gb | cores | tot. RAM | tot cores | tot. GPU |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 4 | 512 | 40 | 2048 | 160 |  |
| 4 | 512 | 24 | 2048 | 96 |  |
| 2 | 512 | 24 | 2048 | 48 | 8 |
| 2 | 1024 | 32 | 2048 | 64 | 8 |
| 4 | 1024 | 32 | 4096 | 128 | 8 |
| 8 | 1024 | 32 | 8192 | 256 | 16 |
| **24** |  |  | **20480** | **752** | **40** |

Two nodes, each equipped with four NVIDIA A100 GPUs (80 GB VRAM each) and 512 GB of system RAM, are configured with MIG (Multi-Instance GPU) and dedicated to students.

The Technology team provides training workshops and support for researchers.

Access is granted by simple email request ( [hpc@unibocconi.it](mailto:hpc@unibocconi.it "mailto:hpc@unibocconi.it")).

Technology and Information Services

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/technology-and-information-services)


**Hardware Requirements** TECHNOLOGY recommends the following device features for accessing wi-fi:

**_Laptop_**

Wireless card compatible with the standard 802.11ac or 802.11a/b/g/n (but with a lower performance level).

If your laptop has not a wireless card with these features, you can buy Wireless USB cards at a moderate price.

**_Smartphone and Tablet:_**

The same requirements are for smartphone and tablet as well as for laptop: preferably compatible with standard 802.11ac, or otherwise with 802.11a/b/g/n.

We do not recommend the use of laptop, smartphone or tablet compatible  with the only old standard 802.11b/g

and 802.11b/g/n because they can only work using a frequency of 2.4 GHz and a speed of 54 Mbps.

Wi-Fi technology is now widespread, the best infrastructure to be able to access Internet services without a cable and logistic spaces (plugs, laboratories PC).

Bocconi offers a large list of applications available through Internet: undifferentiated browsing, email, administrative processes management, eLearning. Wi-Fi technology makes the use of computing resources and innovative educational models easier and increases work areas.

All the buildings are covered by wi-fi (standard 802.11ac).

text:
For Employers and Partners

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/employers-and-partners)


Services offered by Bocconi dedicated to head hunters are the following:

- **Access to CV Book** Bocconi’s CV Book is a summary of the personal data, academic background, work experience and language knowledge of Bocconi recent graduates. CVs are published after each graduation session and are available for one year.
- **Promotion of internship and placement opportunities** Bocconi University carefully checks the quality of opportunities posted on our portal and may opt not to offer job posting services to all those requesting it. In this regard, please consult our [Employer policies](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/employer-e-partner/employer/recruitment-online/employer-policies) which applies to both Head Hunters and its Client Companies.

Head Hunters may promote placement offers both for themselves and for their client companies, but may only promote internship opportunities for themselves. In this case, internships are to be formalized through Bocconi University.

Head Hunting agencies are allowed to promote up to:

- 12 offers a year;
- 2 offers at the same time.

**Please note** that such restrictions do not include senior placement offers posted by Head Hunters.

**[Access to our services](https://jobgate.unibocconi.it/home.php?lingua=eng)**

For any further detail, please refer to:

text:
09 Feb 2026 - 13 Feb 2026

10:00am - 6:30pm

Leonardo Del Vecchio Building

Bocconi student associations are welcoming our community!

Join us for the ultimate gathering of university student associations! Whether you're looking to meet like-minded peers, explore new interests, or get involved in campus life, this event is your gateway to a world of opportunities. Discover student-led organizations across culture, finance, economics, social impact, and more **below** and explore more details on the associations [**here**](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/attivita-studentesche/elenco-delle-associazioni).

**Don’t miss out—come explore, connect, and take the first step toward something great!**

In addition to the student associations, you can also discover at thr fair the **European University of Social Sciences (CIVICA)** an alliance of ten leading institutions with the goal to create an inter-university campus that fosters collaboration in education, research, and innovation.

It aspires to shape the future of social sciences while promoting inclusivity, sustainability, and societal impact.

You will also find a desk dedicated to all the Campus Life activities organized for the second semester.

**Swing by our desk and find out more!**

21 May 2026 - 22 May 2026

9:00am - 6:30pm CET

Room N01, Leonardo Del Vecchio Building, Ground floor

Conferences - International Arbitration Law

Paid admission

Angelo Sraffa Department of Legal Studies

International law plays a crucial role in addressing post-conflict disputes, from arbitration tribunals to the enforcement of sanctions and legal advocacy in war-torn regions. It shapes accountability mechanisms, facilitates restitution efforts, and navigates the complexities of state and investor arbitration. Legal frameworks must balance justice with the pragmatic realities of diplomacy and conflict resolution. Understanding these dynamics is essential for ensuring fair outcomes and sustainable peace in an increasingly volatile world.

[Click here for the event's main website](https://ius.unibocconi.eu/ethics-international-arbitration)

Founding member of the jazz-fusion group Snarky Puppy—with whom he has achieved worldwide fame and won five Grammy Awards—Bill Laurance arrives at Isole Sonore to present his highly personal solo piano journey.

Composer, pianist, and keyboardist, Bill Laurance explored jazz, funk, and drum’n’bass alongside classical music from a young age. He has collaborated with artists such as Morcheeba, Salif Keita, Terence Blanchard, Susana Baca, Lalah Hathaway, Laura Mvula, Jacob Collier, Musiq Soulchild, Khalid Sansi, Chris Potter, Lionel Loueke, and Carlos Malta, and has written numerous ballets and film scores.

text:
**Focus: Online simulation of the admissions test**

You can take an online simulation of the admissions test **on 20, 21 and 22 May 2026**.

If you're interested, you must select the admissions test option when completing your **registration for the Open Day event** and **choose your preferred day** among the three days. On Monday 18th May you'll receive an email with a **link** to access the simulation, along with your **log-in information**.

**Choose how you want to attend** and register!

**Don't wait**: a limited number of spots is available.

You can **change your selections** at any time by accessing the [**event platform**](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=programma) with the email and password entered during registration.

ONLINE

You will receive a confirmation email after registration. If you change your mind and would like to attend on campus, you can always change your registration - based on availability.

ON CAMPUS

When you register for the event, remember to select the Welcome Speech you would like to attend, the activities you're interested in participating in and whether you will be attending with a guest. **Access to presentation rooms is restricted** to participants who have reserved that activity. Only some activities on the day of the event will be open to everyone.

After completing registration, you will receive your personalized program with a **personal QR code** to access all the activities at the email address indicated on the form.

You can change your selections at any time by accessing the [**event platform**](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=programma). On 16 May you can also use the [**platform**](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=programma) to see your schedule, get your QR code and participate in the chat rooms.

NB: Each student can **attend the on-campus event with only one guest**(parent or other guest). Therefore, when completing the registration form, make sure you enter your guest's contact information so they receive an invitation to register for the event. They will then receive **their personal QR code** and be able to attend the activities you select for 16 May.

[How to register and participate](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/events/open-day#accordion-boxed-123999)

text:
### A Sustainable Bocconi

Bocconi's foundation in 1902 was based on a set of ethical and responsible core values that have guided it to a robust and lasting focus on sustainability issues. This commitment is fueled through teaching and research, to best prepare our students and contribute to scientific and social progress. Bocconi also undertakes direct initiatives in many areas, including energy efficiency, saving resources, reducing the use of materials such as paper and plastic, and recycling waste.

We therefore aim to reduce the impact of the event on campus in the following ways:

- **Less paper**: Brochures and other materials will not be printed but rather available online or accessible through QR codes
- **Less plastic**: Water dispensers are available on campus where you can fill your water bottle at no cost
- **Less pollution**: We encourage you to come to campus using public transportation when possible.

metadata.title: Open Day | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Open Day | Bocconi University > A Sustainable Bocconi

text:
11 May 2026

6:00pm - 11:30pm

Via Roentgen 1

#### An evening entirely devoted to performing arts at Bocconi!

A journey through the performing arts as a shared community experience, where every voice, every body, every image is a thread that binds us together.

Student participating in **theater, singing, dance and filmmaking** courses organized by Campus Life, together with students from the Association **Gruppo Teatro Studenti Bocconi,** will perform in an evening celebrating the performing arts on campus.

This will be followed by **the musical "Tonight We Gotta Footloose!" by BLPSA.**

The evening is organized by Campus Life **in collaboration with Teatro Franco Parenti** and coordinated byProf. **Alex Turrini**, Arts & Humanities Committee with responsibility for performing arts.

The Bocconi University community reveals itself through art as a living network of connections, passions, and presences.

metadata.title: Performing Arts Night 2026 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Performing Arts Night 2026 | Bocconi University

text:
### Performing Arts Showcase

The evening celebrates the journey of the workshops and student initiatives promoted by Campus Life: through distributed stages, video installations, and performance spaces, featuring theater, singing, and dance.

At the beginning and at the end of the evening, the Gruppo Teatro Studenti Bocconi - directed by Marco Brambini - will present readings from the works of Stefano Benni.

Each performance thus becomes a thread cast into the shared space of the community, capable of connecting stage and audience, performers and spectators, students and the public, making visible through art the network of bonds that already unites us.

The workshops were led by:

- **Theater**: Tobia Rossi  in collaboration with Claudia Grassi, artistic collaborator.
- **Choral singing**: Aleksander Zielinski
- **Dance**: Marco Pelle in collaboration with Gaia Maria Lorusso, artistic collaborator.
- **Filmmaking**: Tommaso Santambrogio, in collaboration with Astrid Ardenti, artistic collaborator.

_Free event, registration required._

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/events/performing-arts-night-2026#accordion-boxed-142973)


Don't miss the

Performing Arts Showcase

[Register here!](https://events.unibocconi.eu/index.php?key=ev20260511pan)

9 PM, Aula Magna Roentgen

metadata.title: Performing Arts Night 2026 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Performing Arts Night 2026 | Bocconi University > Performing Arts Showcase

text:
### Musical "Tonight We Gotta Footloose!"

Get ready to dance, sing, and let yourself be swept away by the most infectious music ever. BLPSA is proud to present a rousing revival of one of the most beloved shows in the history of musical theater.

A show entirely produced by students, directed by Altea Russo, Resident Director of productions such as Sister Act and Pretty Woman at the Teatro Nazionale, and choreographed by GinevraGrossi, a Musical Performer and Dancer with experience in major international productions such as Così Fan Tutte at La Scala, West Side Story at the Rome Opera and Tootsie & Matilda the Musical with Peeparrow Entertainment.

The show will be held in Italian.

The Bocconi Live Performance Students Association is a student organization, active since 2014 and dedicated to live performances and staging a musical on campus every year.

The musical is an initiative funded by Bocconi University, organized as part of the Arts and Humanities activities of Campus Life.

_Paid event._

[Read more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/events/performing-arts-night-2026#accordion-boxed-142971)


BLPSA presents

Tonight We Gotta Footloose!

text:
News and Events Hub

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/hub-news-and-events)

[University](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/news-categories/university) [Welcome days](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/argomento-news/welcome-days)

23 Aug 2024, by Pietro Masotti, translated by Jenna Walker

Associations recognized by the University range from groups related to volunteering and social work, to groups that bring together national communities; from sports to literary associations, as well as groups focusing on diplomacy and international cooperation. With over 100 official student associations, they are a great way to meet people who share your interests and put yourself to the test

These days, Students for Humanity’s group chat is full of photos of a construction site. They are from a site in Wasa, Tanzania, where some of the members of the Bocconi student association are following the renovation of a school that inspired the Working for Wasa project. At the same time, more than 13 years ago, it also inspired the creation of a student group that would deal with social issues at Bocconi. Today the school hosts 70 students and represents one of the main projects that Students for Humanity (SforH) supports as a volunteer organization recognized by the Lombardy Region. "I myself was in Tanzania two years ago, helping register the facility with the local government," recalls **Anna Artese**, in her second year of the Master of Science in International Politics and Government and president of SforH. Since its inception in 2011, SforH has also grown as a Bocconi association with over 100 members and is now a point of reference among student groups focusing on social-oriented initiatives: it organizes events, promotes fundraising, participates in awareness campaigns and supports the projects of other NGOs. "But most of all we try to encourage our classmates to actively participate in the various activities," adds Anna. "And we act as an intermediary between associations that need volunteers and students who have expressed their availability. In recent years we have participated in lots of one-off initiatives, from collecting medicine for children to prison visits. More consolidated collaborations are also created when we are able to guarantee adequate and ongoing participation."

"There are about 110 student associations active at Bocconi today," says **Paola Profeta**, Dean for Diversity, Inclusion and Sustainability and President of the CASA Committee that brings together the leadership of the associations but also members of the staff, academics and the Campus Life offices. "Some groups are active on general issues, others have specific interests, regarding individual countries, economic matters or social issues. It is a very interesting area that makes life on campus more exciting. It has grown over the years in terms of numbers, perhaps because the younger generations are more inclined to develop extracurricular interests and look for other people at the university they can share them with. In this sense, they can be considered part of student education, understood not only as teaching or research, but creating a context where students can put themselves to the test in developing and implementing ideas by creating a professional network and a community of people. At Bocconi we try to make sure they do not become closed, exclusive circles, but that they remain as inclusive as possible."

metadata.title: Student Associations: 110 Ways to Experience the University | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Student Associations: 110 Ways to Experience the University | Bocconi University

text:
This is a topic that **Angelo Hu** – newly elected head of the Bocconi Chinese Student Association (BCSA), founded in 2010 with the aim of extending a bridge to Chinese heritage – understands well. "It is therefore not an association only for Chinese students, but it is open to anyone interested in the country and its culture," Angelo points out. For this reason, activities more related to business aspects are interspersed throughout the year with others of a more recreational nature. "For example, this year we hosted some senior members of the Alibaba group who shared with us the strategies of their success in international and online commerce, but we also celebrated several typical holidays such as the Moon Cake Festival, and of course the Chinese New Year with a Dumpling Event, during which we prepare dumplings by hand and get to eat them together." The most eagerly awaited event on the BCSA calendar is the Welcome Dinner, a dinner attended by over 200 people and which includes speeches, dance and singing performances, as well as ice breaking games. Activities also include volleyball and badminton sports challenges with other universities and winter sports, confirming that the main objective of the association is to promote cohesion and the creation of strong bonds between members. "Above all, we would like to reach out more to international students, who today represent 15% of currently enrolled students," continues Angelo. "Chinese students in particular are more likely to get to know us as soon as they arrive at Bocconi because we offer a dedicated tutoring, orientation and academic support program for them. We have also created a page on the Chinese social network Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book) that shows university residences, lecture buildings and study spaces, with vlogs that explain a typical day at Bocconi."

Every year there are changes to the panorama of student associations, with some closing shop and others being founded. "Students who would like to propose a new association must submit a list with a certain number of interested classmates," explains Dean Profeta. "We evaluate their purpose, we look at whether there are other similar groups present, possibly suggesting a merger, we approve the calendar of events and we monitor that they remain active over time, i.e. that they are organizing activities. And we also promote special events, such as the Associations Fair, in which the different groups can introduce themselves on campus and to other students."

It was during one of these initiatives last year that **Lea Casucci** came across a presentation by Bocconi Students for Diplomacy and International Cooperation, an organization where she is now the outgoing president. "It was my first year, but I was immediately impressed by the initiative because I already had an interest in international cooperation and it seemed like an opportunity that I shouldn’t miss." The association is active in the organization of events and conferences on the subject and it promotes contact between students and international organizations. Today there are about 70 members. Recruiting sessions are held twice a year to assess which new members should join, evaluating their motivations and skills to understand what activities they can contribute to. "But events are not reserved, many are open to all students," Lea points out. "Last year, for example, we organized a day in memory of Luca Attanasio, the Italian ambassador and former Bocconi student killed in 2021 in the Democratic Republic of Congo. There was a commemoration with Attanasio's family, followed by a panel on the impact of humanitarian aid in Central Africa with very high-profile speakers." The projects in the pipeline for the new year include organizing an exchange with SDA Bocconi on the topic of space diplomacy, and learning more about embassies and consulates in the local area."

metadata.title: Student Associations: 110 Ways to Experience the University | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Student Associations: 110 Ways to Experience the University | Bocconi University

text:
There is also no shortage of initiatives scheduled by ALBI, the Bocconi d'Inchiostro Literary Student Association. One of the oldest groups at the University, it was co-founded in 2012 by Gerardo Masuccio, now editor at Utopia. Since then, the group has been responsible for suggesting literary insights to the entire university community, promoting events with writers, poets, publishers and translators, and raising awareness of reading with various initiatives. An example of this is Bookcrossing, created in collaboration with the university: an area located near the entrance to the Bocconi Residence Hall and intended for a book exchange. In just a few months, more than 120 books have already been shared on its shelves. "We try to organize at least three or four events a month, some reserved for our internal Book Club and others also open to the whole city, such as those during BookCity," explains president **Valentina Benassi**. "As well as the event organized on the Bagutta Prize, which was held here because the historical archive of the Bagutta trattoria’s works and its literary prize is preserved at Bocconi." Bocconi d'Inchiostro also awards a prize, the Dante d'Oro, which for several years was given as a lifetime achievement, but now is intended for unpublished poems. "The selections are in the final stages. This summer we will discuss the poems and in September we will have the awards ceremony." The association's activities have even inspired a new cultural magazine, 'Turbine'. Though it was founded at Bocconi, it is now maintained on the initiative of the individual founding students.

[Discover here all the students associations](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/campus-life/attivita-studentesche/elenco-delle-associazioni)

## **Bocconi4Access to Education a.y. 2025-26**

Bocconi has always been committed to making higher education available to all motivated and talented students through a financial aid system, based on both merit and need.

In the section below, **funding opportunities for a.y. 2025-26** are summarized. These opportunities offer a different kind of support to families and, in most cases, take into consideration the economic and asset situation of students' families.

Benefits are different based to the degree course to which you belong.

text:
Gli studenti in possesso dei [**requisiti economici**](https://www.unibocconi.it/it/studenti-iscritti/agevolazioni/requisiti-economici-isu-bocconi-aa-2025-26 "Requisiti economici ISU Bocconi - a.a. 2025-26") e intenzionati a richiedere il solo servizio mensa a tariffa agevolata, **dovranno presentare apposita "Domanda di servizio mensa a tariffa agevolata"** nel periodo 14 ottobre - 21 ottobre 2025 **:**

**Domanda di servizio mensa a tariffa agevolata**

Il beneficio del pasto a tariffa agevolata avrà validità **dal 1 dicembre 2025 al 30 novembre 2026**.

Non saranno effettuati rimborsi per eventuali importi residui non utilizzati. Invitiamo gli studenti a verificarne l'entità prima di effettuare l'acquisto soprattutto nelle ultime finestre temporali di ciascun anno accademico (ottobre e novembre).

Il pasto a tariffa agevolata

Lo studente che beneficia del servizio mensa a tariffa agevolata può acquistare alle tariffe più avanti indicate un pasto completo costituito, di norma da primo, secondo, contorno, pane, frutta o bevanda.

Le specifiche tipologie di pasto acquistabile a tariffa agevolata vanno verificate presso il punto vendita.

In ogni caso lo studente può acquistare massimo due pasti a tariffa agevolata al giorno, uno a pranzo (dalle 11:45 alle 14:30) e uno a cena (dalle 19:00 alle 20:30), secondo i periodi di apertura previsti per i singoli punti vendita. Se verranno riscontrate anomalie e/o comportamenti impropri, l’Università si riserva di effettuare le opportune valutazioni e di intervenire anche con l’eventuale revoca della possibilità di acquisto dei pasti a tariffa agevolata.

Tariffe e acquisto

I pasti a tariffa agevolata possono essere acquistati, nei punti ristoro previsti, esclusivamente utilizzando il tesserino Bocconi, sul quale vanno preventivamente caricati. Si ricorda che il badge è personale e non cedibile e che anche i pasti in esso caricati sono esclusivamente ad uso personale e non possono essere ceduti.

Il processo prevede che lo studente proceda all’acquisto dei pasti attraverso lo strumento on-line [UbsStore](https://uni.bocconistore.it/buy_it), attivo nelle seguenti finestre temporali:

- 10 pasti in seconda fascia ISU Bocconi € 38;
- 10 pasti in terza fascia ISU Bocconi € 46.
Una volta caricati i pasti sul tesserino questi possono esser utilizzati in numero massimo di due al giorno, uno a pranzo (dalle 11:45 alle 14:30) e uno a cena (dalle 19:00 alle 20:30), secondo i periodi di apertura previsti per i singoli punti vendita per l’acquisto di un pasto completo a tariffa agevolata. Se verranno riscontrate anomalie e/o comportamenti impropri, l’Università si riserva di effettuare le opportune valutazioni e di intervenire anche con l’eventuale revoca della possibilità di acquisto dei pasti a tariffa agevolata.

**\*** Gli importi possono subire variazioni legate all'inflazione e/o cambiamenti nel servizio offerto.

**Attenzione:** In caso di smarrimento e contestuale richiesta di un nuovo badge Bocconi, per accedere al servizio mensa a tariffa agevolata, è necessario inviare una mail con oggetto “Smarrimento badge Bocconi” all’indirizzo [funding@unibocconi.it](mailto:funding@unibocconi.it) per modificare la stringa del proprio badge.

Si specifica che a seguito di **revoca** della Borsa di Studio ISU Bocconi, gli studenti in possesso dei requisiti economici previsti nell'a.a. successivo a quello in cui incorre la revoca e intenzionati a richiedere il solo servizio mensa a tariffa agevolata, possono inoltrare apposita domanda online nel periodo previsto, anche se non idonei alla Borsa di Studio ISU Bocconi.

\\*\\* Prima finestra d'acquisto disponibile per gli studenti immatricolati al primo anno 2025/2026

Eduroam

**Eduroam** ( **Edu** cation **Roam** ing) è un servizio che offre un accesso wireless sicuro alla rete.

Permette a **Docenti** e **Personale tecnico amministrativo** della Bocconi che visitano un ente/istituzione che aderisce all'iniziativa, di utilizzarne la rete locale wireless (WLAN) in modo sicuro, autenticandosi con credenziali Bocconi, senza la necessità di espletare ulteriori formalità presso l'istituzione ospitante.

Analogamente, tutti gli utenti provenienti da enti/istituzioni che aderiscono all'iniziativa, possono accedere alla rete wireless Bocconi, utilizzando le proprie credenziali, senza la necessità di alcuna registrazione.

L'Università Bocconi aderisce alla **Federazione Italiana Eduroam**, coordinata dal **GARR**, che la rappresenta presso le altre federazioni e confederazioni. Eduroam e' un marchio registrato di [TERENA](https://www.terena.org/ "TERENA").

Eduroam è un'infrastruttura basata su una rete di server **RADIUS** che utilizza il protocollo 802.1x. Unico requisito tecnico per accedere alla rete eduroam è che il proprio dispositivo di accesso sia in grado di supportare l'autenticazione 802.1X con crittografia WPA2 / TKIP.

Il nome di rete WiFi (SSID) alla quale accedere per usufruire del servizio Eduroam è: **eduroam**

metadata.title: Eduroam - Università Bocconi
metadata.heading_path: Eduroam - Università Bocconi

text:
L'accesso alla rete Eduroam e' vincolato al rispetto del Regolamento della Federazione Italiana Eduroam. Per maggiori informazioni relative alla rete Eduroam e alla sua copertura nazionale ed internazionale si rimanda al sito della [Federazione Italiana Eduroam](https://www.eduroam.it/ "Federazione Italiana Eduroam") e al sito della [Confederazione Europea Eduroam](https://www.eduroam.org/ "Confederazione Europea Eduroam").

#### EDUROAM

Per richiedere informazioni o l'accesso al servizio inviare un email all'indirizzo

[**noc@unibocconi.it**](mailto:helpwifi@unibocconi.it)

## Check out all the museums you can visit for free

[Museums and Galleries](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-museums-milano-definitive-guide#) [Attractions](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-museums-milano-definitive-guide#)


Find here the guide to all the **museums in Milano** that always offer **free entry** plus those that offer free admission on the **first Sunday of the month**, or only on **specific days and times**, and those that offer free entry for **children and youngsters**.

Make the most of these opportunities to get your fill of cultural beauty.

- [Museums always free in Milano](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-museums-milano-definitive-guide#blocco-redazionale-tab-10543)
- [Free museums on the first Sunday of the month](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-museums-milano-definitive-guide#blocco-redazionale-tab-10546)

metadata.title: Free museums in Milano: the definitive guide | YesMilano.it
metadata.heading_path: Check out all the museums you can visit for free

text:
### Museums always free in Milano

All these museums offer **free entry to their permanent collections**. Temporary exhibitions and events may require an additional ticket: please check the museums’ official websites before your visit for further information.

**Civic Museums**

- [Mudec - Museum of Cultures](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/mudec-museum-cultures "Mudec - Museum of Cultures") (permanent collection)
- [Palazzo Morando \| Costume Moda Immagine](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/palazzo-morando-costume-moda-immagine "Palazzo Morando | Costume Moda Immagine")
- [Palazzo Moriggia \| Museo del Risorgimento](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/palazzo-moriggia-museo-del-risorgimento "Palazzo Moriggia | Museo del Risorgimento")
- [Casa-Museo Boschi di Stefano](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/casa-museo-boschi-di-stefano "Casa Museo Boschi-Di Stefano")

**House Museums**

- [Spazio Alda Merini](https://www.spazioaldamerini.com/visita/ "Spazio Alda Merini")
- [Casa Verdi](https://www.casaverdi.it/visitare-casa-verdi/visitare-casa-verdi/ "Casa Verdi")
- [Museo Mangini Bonomi](http://www.museomanginibonomi.it/contacts/ "Museo Magnini Bonomi")

**Other museums**

- [Pirelli HangarBicocca](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/hangar-bicocca "Hangar Bicocca")
- [Cimitero Monumentale](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/monumental-cemetery "Monumental Cemetery")

metadata.title: Free museums in Milano: the definitive guide | YesMilano.it
metadata.heading_path: Check out all the museums you can visit for free > Museums always free in Milano

text:
### Free museums on the first Sunday of the month

The first Sunday of every month is **Domenica al Museo (Sunday at the Museum),** a cultural initiative launched by the Ministry of Culture: the city’s state and civic museums are open free of charge.

**Civic Museums**

- [Museo del Novecento](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/museo-del-novecento "Museo del Novecento")
- [Castello Sforzesco museums](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/castello-sforzesco "Castello Sforzesco Museums")
- [Archaeological Museum](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/archaeological-museum "Archaeological Museum")
- [Gallery of Modern Art](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/galleria-arte-moderna "GAM Gallery of Modern Art")
- [Museum of Natural History](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/natural-history-civic-museum "Museum of Natural History")
- [Civic Aquarium](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/milanos-civic-aquarium "Civic Aquarium")
- Booking is mandatory: [Palazzo Marino](https://palazzomarinomilano.madeticket.it/it/shop/biglietti "Bok to visit Palazzo Marino")

**State Museums**

**Please note: booking is mandatory before your visit**

- [Brera Art Gallery](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/pinacoteca-di-brera "Pinacoteca di Brera")
- [Leonardo's Last Supper](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/buy-your-ticket-leonardos-last-supper "Buy your ticket to Leonardo's Last Supper") (Please make your booking well in advance: visit the page for details)

**Other museums**

- [Armani Silos](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/armani-silos "Armani Silos")
- [Luigi Rovati Foundation](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/fondazione-luigi-rovati-art-museum "Luigi Rovati Foundation")
- [Gallerie d’Italia](https://gallerieditalia.com/en/milan/plan-your-visit/ "Gallerie d'Italia")

text:
### Free entry to museums during the week

#### Every first and third Tuesday of the month from 2:00 p.m.

Every **first and third Tuesday of the month, from 2:00 pm onwards**, the **Milano Civic Museums** offer free entry, in addition to those that always offer free admission:

- [Museo del Novecento](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/museo-del-novecento "Museo del Novecento")
- [Castello Sforzesco museums](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/castello-sforzesco "Castello Sforzesco Museums")
- [Archaeological Museum](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/archaeological-museum "Archaeological Museum")
- [Gallery of Modern Art](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/galleria-arte-moderna "GAM Gallery of Modern Art")
- [Museum of Natural History](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/natural-history-civic-museum "Museum of Natural History")
- [Civic Aquarium](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/milanos-civic-aquarium "Civic Aquarium")

- [DISCOVER THE CIVIC MUSEUMS, FIND OUT MORE](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/civic-museums-milano)

text:

#### Free museum entry up to 18 years of age

The following museums are **free for visitors under the age of 18**:

**Civic Museums**

- [Archaeological Museum](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/archaeological-museum "Archaeological Museum")
- [Gallery of Modern Art](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/galleria-arte-moderna "GAM Gallery of Modern Art")
- [Museum of Natural History](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/natural-history-civic-museum "Museum of Natural History")
- [Civic Aquarium](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/milanos-civic-aquarium "Civic Aquarium")

**Other Museums**

text:
### Free museum admission for disabled persons

The city of Milano guarantees persons with disabilities **free admission** to all its **museums, libraries and art galleries**. For all assistance, please go to the venue’s ticket office.

Visit all the museums listed here **for free**:

- [Duomo Museum](https://www.duomomilano.it/en/infopage/the-duomo-museum/50/ "Museo del Duomo")
- [Leonardo's Last Supper](https://cenacolovinciano.org/en/ "Leonardo's Last Supper")
- [Pinacoteca di Brera](https://pinacotecabrera.org/en/visit/tickets/ "Pinacoteca di Brera")
- [Museo Teatro alla Scala](https://www.museoscala.org/en/ "Museo Teatro alla Scala")
- [Pinacoteca Ambrosiana](https://www.ambrosiana.it/en/ "Pinacoteca Ambrosiana")
- [Museo Nazionale Scienza e Tecnologia](https://www.museoscienza.org/en "Museo Scienza e Tecnologia")
- [Musei del Castello sforzesco](https://www.milanocastello.it/en "Castello Sforzesco Museums")
- [Galleria d'Arte Moderna](http://www.gam-milano.com/en/home/ "Galleria d'arte moderna")
- [Museo del Novecento](https://www.museodelnovecento.org/en/ "Museo del Novecento")
- [Civico Museo Archeologico](https://www.museoarcheologicomilano.it/ "Museo archeologico")
- [Civico Museo di Storia Naturale](https://museodistorianaturalemilano.it/ "Museo di storia naturale")
- [Acquario Civico](https://www.acquariodimilano.it/ "Acquario civico ")
- [Museo Poldi Pezzoli](https://museopoldipezzoli.it/en/ "Museo Poldi Pezzoli")
- [Gallerie d’Italia](https://gallerieditalia.com/en/ "Gallerie d'Italia")
- [Memoriale della Shoah](https://www.memorialeshoah.it/?lang=en "Memoriale Shoah ")
- [Mondo Milan](https://www.acmilan.com/en/tickets/mondo-milan-museum "Mondo Milan")
- [San Siro Museum & Tour](https://www.sansirostadium.com/en/ "San Siro Stadium")
- [Fondazione Prada](https://www.fondazioneprada.org/?lang=en "Fondazione Prada")
- [Villa Necchi Campiglio](https://casemuseo.it/en/project/necchi-campiglio-eng/ "Villa Necchi Campiglio")

Admission reservation is not mandatory, but recommended.

- [ALL THE INFO ON ACCESSIBLE MILANO](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/accessible-milano)

text:
## From museums with free admission to street art tours, great experiences without spending a euro (or almost)

[Attractions](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#) [Museums and Galleries](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#) [My first trip to Milano](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#)


Who said nothing is free in Milano? Museums without entry fees, street art tours, Art Nouveau architecture, parks and even a visit to the opera for everyone. Here is a list of **10 things you can do without opening your wallet**.

- [1\. Free museums in Milan](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#blocco-redazionale-tab-123)
- [2\. Discover Milan’s “Sistine Chapel”](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#blocco-redazionale-tab-124)
- [3\. Admire the Monumental Cemetery](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#blocco-redazionale-tab-126)
- [4\. Have a Stroll in the City Parks](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/free-things-do-milano#blocco-redazionale-tab-130)

metadata.title: Free things to do in Milan | YesMilano.it
metadata.heading_path: From museums with free admission to street art tours, great experiences without spending a euro (or almost)

text:
### 1\. Free museums in Milan

Palazzo Morando \| Costume Moda Immagine

Anselm Kiefer, The Seven Heavenly Palaces, 2004-2015. Courtesy Pirelli HangarBicocca. Pic by Agostino Osio

Palazzo Morando \| Costume Moda Immagine

Anselm Kiefer, The Seven Heavenly Palaces, 2004-2015. Courtesy Pirelli HangarBicocca. Pic by Agostino Osio

Palazzo Morando \| Costume Moda Immagine

- 1
- 2

1 / 2

There are several **museums**, [**studios and house museums**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/house-museums "house museums in milano") in Milan that you can visit without spending a euro. The following can always be visited for free: **Palazzo Morando \| Costume Fashion Image**, **Palazzo Moriggia \| the Museum of the Risorgimento**, the **Boschi Di Stefano House Museum**, the **Francesco Messina Studio Museum** and **[MUDEC - Museum of Cultures permanent collection](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/mudec-museum-cultures "MUDEC")** which houses a large selection of finds from global cultures, ranging from the ancient Andean civilizations to the Far East.

Alternatively, if you are a contemporary art fan, the **[Pirelli Hangar Bicocca](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/hangar-bicocca "Pirelli Hangar Bicocca")** hosts free installations and exhibitions featuring prominent international artists. You can also walk amongst Anselm Kiefer's permanent site-specific installation, _The Seven Heavenly Palaces_.

- [The civic museums in Milano](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/civic-museums-milano)
- [House Museums](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/house-museums)

metadata.title: Free things to do in Milan | YesMilano.it
metadata.heading_path: From museums with free admission to street art tours, great experiences without spending a euro (or almost) > 1\. Free museums in Milan

text:
### 2\. Discover Milan’s “Sistine Chapel”

Santa Maria delle Grazie

San Maurizio al Monastero Maggiore

Santa Maria delle Grazie

San Maurizio al Monastero Maggiore

Santa Maria delle Grazie

- 1
- 2

1 / 2

Not everyone realises that Milan also has its very own “ **Sistine Chapel**” that can be visited for free. As soon as you step inside **San Maurizio al Monastero Maggiore** you will be awed by the stunning array of paintings, stuccos and frescoes that cover every wall and ceiling space.

With the exception of the [Duomo](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/10-1-amazing-things-milan-duomo "Duomo di Milano"), all the city churches, with their wonderful interior artworks, can be visited without an entrance fee.

- [Visit the 10 major churches in Milano for free](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/10-major-churches-milano)
- [Abbeys and monasteries in Milan](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/abbeys-and-monasteries)

Parco Sempione

- 1

1 / 1

There are over 50 parks, green zones and gardens in Milano, and these are ideal for a relaxing break in nature.

- [Best parks in Milan](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/node/2417)
- [Parks around the city](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/parks-around-city)

text:
### 5\. Hunt for Street Art around the city

Street art in Ortica neighborhood: Alda Merini portrait - Istituto alberghiero Pasolini, Ortica

- 1

1 / 1

Milano Street Art could surprise you around any corner, in the city centre and in the neighboroods alike. Street art hunting is free and alllows you to discover unespected places in the city. You may like:

- [Our street art in Ortica neighborhood](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/street-art-ortica-memoria "Follow the itinerary")
- Download the app and see street art get alive with [MAUA](https://mauamuseum.com/) \- _Museo d'Arte Urbana Aumentata_ (the website is in Italian, but the Language of art is universal).

text:
### 6\. Discover Brera Botanical Garden

Astronomical Museum seen from the Botanical Garden

- 1

1 / 1

A stone's throw from the [Pinacoteca](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/pinacoteca-di-brera "Pinacoteca di Brera") art gallery, still within **Palazzo Brera** itself, you will find the **Astronomical Museum** with the Schiaparelli dome, but also a precious eighteenth-century **Botanical Garden**, open to the public and free to visit.

- [Find out more](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/breras-botanical-garden)

text:
### 7\. Jump on a tram and see Milan

N. tram 1 passes in front of the Castello Sforzesco. Pic. by markino\_f85-Instagram

- 1

1 / 1

Well, it's not exactly free, but it is a great low-cost experience. And most importantly it enables you to get your bearings in the city. For the price of a public transport ticket, **you can make your own tram tour of Milan**.


Or, for a tram trip with a retro touch, you can catch the historic **n°1 tram** which crosses [via Montenapoleone](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/montenapoleone "Via Montenapoleone"), then passes in front of the [Teatro alla Scala](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/teatro-alla-scala "Teatro alla Scala") and the Arco della Pace.

- [How to get around Milano](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/node/259)

metadata.title: Free things to do in Milan | YesMilano.it
metadata.heading_path: From museums with free admission to street art tours, great experiences without spending a euro (or almost) > 7\. Jump on a tram and see Milan

text:
### 8\. Art Nuveau in Milan

La facciata di Casa Galimberti. Pic. by instagalluz

- 1

1 / 1

[**Porta Venezia**](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/articles/things-to-do-milano-porta-venezia "Porta Venezia") is one of the liveliest neighborhoods in Milan. But it is also one of the areas where you can see some of the most beautiful buildings in the city. This is where you have to come to discover the jewels of **Art Nuveau architecture**: the buildings around **Piazza Eleonora Duse**, the **Berri-Meregalli houses** on Via Mozart 21, Via Barozzi 7 and Via dei Cappuccini 8, **Casa Galimberti** on Via Malpighi, with its magnificent fully decorated façade, and **Casa Campanini** on Via Vincenzo Bellini 11, with its iconic entrance.

- [An Art Nouveau Walk in Milan](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/art-nouveau-walk-7-stops)
- [Hidden gens in Milano: discover secret places](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/itineraries/hidden-gems-milano)

metadata.title: Free things to do in Milan | YesMilano.it
metadata.heading_path: From museums with free admission to street art tours, great experiences without spending a euro (or almost) > 8\. Art Nuveau in Milan

text:
### 9\. Stroll around the Castello Sforzesco courtyard

Castello Sforzesco. Pic. by Andrea Cherchi

- 1

1 / 1

**The Cortile delle Armi** of the **[Castello Sforzesco](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/castello-sforzesco "Castello Sforzesco")** is always open to visitors, free of charge. You can also admire the Ducal and the Rocchetta courtyards. Alternatively, every first and third Tuesday of the month, from 14:00 onwards, and every first Sunday of the month, admission to the Castello museums is also free.

- [Scopri di più](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/castello-sforzesco)

metadata.heading_path: From museums with free admission to street art tours, great experiences without spending a euro (or almost) > 9\. Stroll around the Castello Sforzesco courtyard

text:
### 10\. See an opera at the Teatro alla Scala for free (or almost)

Teatro alla Scala di Milano. Pic. Rudy Amisano ©Teatro alla Scala

- 1

1 / 1

If you are not on the list of lucky invitees who will attend the **Prima della Scala**, every year **on December 7th**, you still have a chance to experience the magic of opera in Milan. Thanks to the **Prima Diffusa project**, a live broadcast of the special operatic event will be shown in numerous locations around the city.

Apart from the opening night, if you want the unforgettable experience of seeing the opera in one of the most famous theatres in the world, you can try to get a seat in the Balcony. For almost all the shows, **La Scala**, offers **numbered tickets for seats with reduced visibility**, with significant discounts (for the opera circa 14 euros, prices can go even lower for the ballet or the symphonic concerts): you just need a lot of patience and you must join the queue in via dei Filodrammatici ( [more details here - Limited view Balcony seats](https://www.teatroallascala.org/en/box-office/tickets-prices/box-office-guidelines.html "Limited view Balcony seats tickets")).

- [Find out more about the Prima](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/whats-on/all-events/la-scala-opening-night)

text:
[Museums and Galleries](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#) [Attractions](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#) [Students](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#)


**If you are under 25, or you are a university student**, you can visit numerous museums and cultural venues in Milano at a **reduced price**.

Here are the venues with reduced admission for young persons, and all the info needed for the visit. Don't forget: many museums permanently offer free admission or are open for free every first Sunday of the month.

- [Reductions for university students with valid university I.D. card](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#blocco-redazionale-tab-10545)
- [Discounts available for all students (with ID)](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#blocco-redazionale-tab-10565)
- [Reductions for age groups](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#blocco-redazionale-tab-10559)
- [Gratuito per facoltà](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#blocco-redazionale-tab-12768)
- [Free museums in Milano: the definitive guide](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/study/museums-reduced-price-young-and-students#blocco-redazionale-tab-10566)

text:
### Gratuito per facoltà

- **[Cenacolo Vinciano](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/whats-on/all-events/last-supper-opens-evenings "Cenacolo Vinciano")**(Brera Academy of Fine Arts, Graduate or doctoral programs in architecture, cultural heritage conservation, educational sciences or humanities and philosophy with an archaeological or art-historical focus)
- **[Pinacoteca di Brera](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/pinacoteca-di-brera "Pinacoteca di Brera")** (students of Italian universities, faculties of architecture, preservation of cultural heritage, design, education sciences and degree courses in literature or literary subjects with an archaeological or historical-artistic focus of faculties of literature and philosophy, students of Italian academies of fine arts, students of higher education: restoration, opificio delle pietre dure, school for mosaic restoration)
- [**Diocesan Museum**](https://chiostrisanteustorgio.it/en/orari-e-biglietti/ "Diocesan Museum") (students of architecture, design, cultural heritage and Academy of Fine Arts)
- **[ADI Design Museum](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/adi-design-museum-compasso-doro "ADI Design Museum")** (students and former students IED platform)
- **[Galleria D’Arte Moderna GAM](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/galleria-arte-moderna "Galleria D’Arte Moderna GAM")**(high schools)
- **[Archaeological Museum](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/archaeological-museum "Archaeological Museum")**(high schools)
- **[Milano's Civic Aquarium](https://www.yesmilano.it/en/see-and-do/venues/milanos-civic-aquarium "Milano's Civic Aquarium")**(high schools)
